Migration options: 1) App Routing Gateway API (meshless Istio) - Preview March 2026, GA May 2026. 2) Application Gateway for Containers - GA now, supports Ingress + Gateway API. 3) Istio add-on - Ingress GA, Gateway API Preview (GA targeted May 2026).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/Application Routing (nginx) Add-on

symptom: KEDA addon is enabled (az aks show reports enabled=true), but keda-* pods are not present in kube-system namespace. Helm reconciliation error: 'rendered manifests contain a resource that already exists' with 'invalid ownership metadata' for CRDs like cloudeventsources.eventing.keda.sh.
rootCause: Leftover CRDs, ValidatingWebhookConfigurations, and other resources from a previous self-managed open-source KEDA installation conflict with the managed KEDA add-on Helm chart deployment. Multiple KEDA installations are not supported.
solution: Clean up all residual self-managed KEDA components: 1) Remove CRDs: 'kubectl get crd | grep keda' and delete them, 2) Remove ValidatingWebhookConfigurations: 'kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations | grep keda' and delete, 3) Disable KEDA add-on, 4) Re-enable KEDA add-on. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/keda-about#add-on-limitations
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/KEDA

symptom: FluxConfiguration resource is stuck in Creating state or changes are not being applied; kubectl get fluxconfiguration shows the resource is not progressing
rootCause: fluxconfigs.clusterconfig.azure.com CRD does not exist on the cluster; typically caused by failed or incomplete Flux extension installation
solution: Re-issue PUT request on the microsoft.flux extension to reinitialize the helm install loop and re-provision the CRD. If re-issuing PUT fails, delete the current microsoft.flux extension and reinstall it
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Microsoft.Flux FluxConfiguration TSG

symptom: Pod subnet full: nodes cannot request more IPs from delegated subnet, pods stuck in ContainerCreating. DNC logs show subnet capacity exceeded. Affects AKS clusters using Azure CNI dynamic pod IP allocation (SWIFT)
rootCause: The pod subnet CIDR is exhausted because IPs are allocated in batches of 16 per node (minimum 16, requesting another 16 when <8 free). Shared pod subnets across multiple clusters/nodepools accelerate exhaustion
solution: Reduce pod count if possible. Create a new pod subnet on the same VNet and create a new node pool using the new pod subnet. Use AppLens Pod Subnet Full detector or Resource Health dashboard to confirm. Check for subnet sharing with Kusto: AgentPoolSnapshot | where podSubnetId contains '<subnet-id>' | distinct clusterName, name
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/AKS dynamic pod IP allocation

symptom: KMS deadlock: AKS cluster with KMS private keyvault + konnectivity gets stuck after stop/start; apiserver cannot start because KMS plugin cannot reach private Key Vault while konnectivity is down
rootCause: Circular dependency: konnectivity requires apiserver, apiserver requires KMS to decrypt etcd, KMS requires konnectivity to reach private Key Vault. During cluster stop/start konnectivity shuts down breaking the loop. Private keyvault + konnectivity support ended Aug 22 2025
solution: Enable API Server VNet Integration: az aks update --enable-apiserver-vnet-integration. This removes the konnectivity dependency for KMS access to private Key Vault. For public key vaults this issue does not occur
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/KMS etcd Encryption

symptom: AKS LocalDNS validation failure when configuring ServerStale=Verify with Protocol=ForceTCP for a given DNS zone. API rejects the combination.
rootCause: ServerStale Verify mode requires UDP protocol for stale response verification. ForceTCP is incompatible with the Verify mode of serve-stale.
solution: Either change Protocol from ForceTCP to PreferUDP, or change serveStale from Verify to Immediate or Disable when using ForceTCP.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/LocalDNS

symptom: Validation errors when creating AKS cluster from Managed Cluster Snapshot: CreatingFromManagedClusterSnapshotNotAllowed, SourceResourceIDNotValidManagedClusterSnapshotID, SourceManagedClusterSnapshotIDNotExistsOrNotInSameRegion, or SourceManagedClusterSnapshotNilProperties.
rootCause: Toggle not enabled for creating from MC snapshot, preview feature not registered, snapshot ID format is invalid, snapshot not found or not in same region as target cluster, or snapshot properties are nil (corrupted snapshot).
solution: 1) Ensure toggle for creating from MC snapshot is enabled; 2) Register ManagedClusterSnapshotPreviewFeature; 3) Verify snapshot ID format is valid; 4) Ensure snapshot exists and is in same region; 5) If NilProperties error, retake the snapshot. Kusto: AKSprod.AsyncQoSEvents with usedManagedClusterSnapshot property to verify.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Preview Features/Managed Cluster Snapshot

symptom: Customer reports Microsoft.Rest.Azure.CloudException errors when using Azure Libraries for .NET to deploy AKS/ACI resources
rootCause: Deployment errors from Azure fluent/.NET libraries are not supported by Containers team; these are library-level issues
solution: If unable to reproduce via portal or CLI, direct customer to GitHub: https://github.com/Azure/azure-libraries-for-net
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Processes/Case Management/Support Scope

symptom: AKS network routing issues caused by CIDR overlap between pod/node CIDRs and BGP Gateway routes or UDR routes in the VNET. Traffic may be dropped or misrouted when too many routes exist in customer environment.
rootCause: Conflicting routes pushed to VNET via BGP Gateway overlap with Pod or Node CIDRs. VNET also adds default routes with None nextHopType to drop traffic in certain scenarios. VNETs connected via Azure Virtual Network Manager may show unexpected ConnectedGroup nextHopType.
solution: Use cidroverlap tool to identify overlapping CIDRs: 1) Export NIC Effective Routes from ASC to Excel after running Test Traffic. 2) Run cidroverlap <target-CIDR> <excel-file> to find all overlapping routes with row numbers. Download: https://github.com/kennethgp/cidroverlap/releases. Use #.#.#.# format for single target IP.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Tools/CIDR overlap

symptom: Customer receives error 'Kubernetes Dashboard addon is deprecated for Kubernetes version >= 1.19.0' (Code: AddonInvalid) when trying to enable the Kube Dashboard addon on AKS.
rootCause: Kubernetes Dashboard addon has been deprecated and removed for AKS clusters running Kubernetes version 1.19 or later.
solution: Direct customer to use the community-maintained Kubernetes Dashboard instead. Check current supported AKS addons at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/integrations
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/Kube Dashboard Addon

symptom: FluxConfiguration changes not reflecting on cluster; configuration applying locally but stuck in Creating state; Kusto shows 'status not yet updated with the latest generation' for FluxConfigAgent
rootCause: FluxConfigAgent cannot communicate with the Azure dataplane to post status back; caused by invalid/missing identity token, network blocking HTTPS to DP endpoint, or HIS certificate retrieval failure
solution: Check fluxconfig-agent pod logs for communication errors; verify HTTPS connectivity from pod to <location>.dp.kubernetesconfiguration.azure.com; query Kusto ConfigAgentTraces for 401/403 responses or 'Failed to get certificate from HIS' errors; verify connectedCluster resource exists in ARM
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Microsoft.Flux FluxConfiguration TSG

symptom: High rate of HTTP 429 responses from API Server. aks-managed-apiserver-guard flowschema and prioritylevelconfiguration are automatically applied to the cluster, limiting client traffic
rootCause: API Server OOM killed event frequency exceeds 10 occurrences within 10 minutes. The overlaymgr/ccpautoscaler OOM watcher detects this and applies APF (API Priority and Fairness) throttling to protect the API server
solution: 1) Inform customer this is protective throttling. 2) Customer should identify and fix unoptimized API clients. 3) Remove the flowschema and prioritylevelconfiguration. 4) If overlaymgr keeps re-creating them, add label aks-managed-skip-update-operation:true to prevent reconciliation. 5) Increase prioritylevelconfiguration values to loosen limits
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/API Server Priority And Fairness Throttling

symptom: KMS plugin logs show 'using system-assigned managed identity to retrieve access token' causing confusion about whether system-assigned MI is supported for KMS
rootCause: KMS only supports user-assigned managed identity and service principal, NOT system-assigned MI. The log is misleading: msi-adapter internally uses system-assigned MI to provide user-assigned MI tokens to KMS plugin, which is by design
solution: This log is expected behavior. Ensure the cluster is configured with a user-assigned managed identity (not system-assigned) for KMS. The msi-adapter component handles the token bridge internally
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/KMS etcd Encryption

symptom: Customer updates VNET DNS servers directly via Azure portal or CLI on the VNET in the node resource group, but AKS nodes do not pick up the new DNS server settings. DNS resolution continues using old servers.
rootCause: Directly updating DNS Servers on the VNET via portal/CLI only updates NRP (Network Resource Provider). AKS RP is not aware of this change, so it is not propagated to the nodes.
solution: After updating VNET DNS servers, perform a nodepool reimage via AKS RP to apply and persist the changes: az aks nodepool upgrade --resource-group <rg> --cluster-name <cluster> --name <nodepool> --node-image-only
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/LocalDNS

symptom: Errors when enabling Shared SLB Health Probe with --cluster-service-load-balancer-health-probe-mode Shared. Error messages: unsupported mode, toggle disabled, not supported with basic LB, feature not registered, or unsupported kubernetes version.
rootCause: Multiple prerequisite failures: invalid mode value (must be Shared or ServiceNodePort), feature toggle off, Basic LB SKU used, feature EnableSLBSharedHealthProbePreview not registered, or Kubernetes version below v1.28.0.
solution: 1) Use valid mode: Shared or ServiceNodePort; 2) Ensure feature toggle is on; 3) Use Standard LB SKU; 4) Register: az feature register --namespace Microsoft.ContainerService --name EnableSLBSharedHealthProbePreview; 5) Upgrade to K8s v1.28.0+. Debug: check RP frontend logs for LoadBalancerProfile, overlaymgr logs for cloudConfigSecretResolver, cloud-node-manager daemonset for health-probe-proxy sidecar.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Shared SLB Health Probe

symptom: Customer attempts to move AKS agent pool resource group and the operation fails
rootCause: AKS cluster does not support resource move operations on the agent pool (MC_) resource group
solution: Inform customer that AKS does not support moving the agent pool resource group. Customer needs to recreate the cluster in the target resource group.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Processes/Case Management/Support Scope

symptom: Conflict when using Istio service mesh add-on and App Routing Gateway API Implementation simultaneously on AKS cluster.
rootCause: App Routing Gateway API deploys its own Istio control plane (non-revisioned, gatewayClassName: approuting-istio, controller: istio.aks.azure.com/gateway-controller) that conflicts with the Istio add-on (revisioned, gatewayClassName: istio, controller: istio.io/gateway-controller). They cannot coexist.
solution: Disable one before enabling the other. If migrating from Istio add-on to App Routing Gateway API: 1) Clean up all Istio CRDs and resources from prior installation. 2) Update gatewayClassName from 'istio' to 'approuting-istio'. 3) Remove revision labels (istio.io/rev=<asm-revision>). Verify via ASI or Kusto query that approuting-istio is enabled.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/Application Routing Gateway API (Istio) Add-on

symptom: Flux GitRepository shows not ready; FluxConfiguration not syncing or applying Git changes to cluster; kustomization resources not being created
rootCause: GitRepository CR does not exist; auth secret missing or contains invalid credentials (wrong SSH private key, missing known_hosts for private repo, invalid branch name)
solution: Verify GitRepository CR: kubectl get gitrepository -n <namespace> <config-name> -o yaml; check auth secret contains valid base64 credentials (username/password for HTTPS or identity/identity.pub/known_hosts for SSH); verify branch name matches actual repo branch
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Microsoft.Flux FluxConfiguration TSG

symptom: AKS does not natively support Application Security Groups (ASGs) in standard deployment. Customer wants to apply ASG rules to AKS node pools
rootCause: AKS does not natively integrate ASGs into its deployment process. ASGs are an Azure Virtual Network feature for grouping VM NICs, but AKS deployment does not include ASG configuration
solution: Option 1: Use Host IP path - configure node pool with public IPs and apply ASG via host-port connections. Option 2: After AKS provisioning, manually assign node NICs to desired ASGs. Option 3: Use cloud-native network security (Network Policies, Kata containers). See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/use-node-public-ips#allow-host-port-connections-and-add-node-pools-to-application-security-groups
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/ASG Support

symptom: Image Integrity policy reports 'System error calling external data provider' in Azure Policy compliance results
rootCause: Ratify pod (gatekeeper-system/ratify) does not exist or is unhealthy on the cluster; Image Integrity addon may not be properly enabled
solution: 1) Check Image Integrity addon enabled via ASI. If not, customer may be using OSS ratify (out of support). 2) If enabled, check ratify pod health: kubectl get pods -n gatekeeper-system. 3) Check logs if unhealthy. 4) If unresolved raise IcM to AKS/Security
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Image Integrity

symptom: DNS resolution issues when both upstream Kubernetes NodeLocalDNS (NodeLocal DNSCache) and AKS LocalDNS are enabled simultaneously on the same AKS cluster.
rootCause: AKS LocalDNS and upstream NodeLocalDNS are two distinct DNS caching mechanisms that conflict when both are enabled. They compete for DNS traffic interception on the node, causing unpredictable resolution behavior.
solution: Do not enable both NodeLocalDNS (upstream) and AKS LocalDNS on the same cluster. Migrate from upstream NodeLocalDNS to AKS LocalDNS for Azure-managed DNS caching with enhanced resiliency and automated management.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/LocalDNS

symptom: On Windows nodes with Shared SLB Health Probe enabled, kube-proxy does not start until the first non-HPC (non-HostProcess Container) pod is created on the node. Health probes fail and traffic is not routed to Windows nodes.
rootCause: Known issue with Windows nodes: kube-proxy will not start until user creates the first non-HPC pod. The health-probe-proxy sidecar monitors port 10356 and forwards to kube-proxy healthz on port 10256, which is unavailable until kube-proxy starts.
solution: Create a non-HPC pod on the Windows node to trigger kube-proxy startup. This is a known limitation; fix is under discussion by AKS PG. Monitor PG updates for a permanent fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Shared SLB Health Probe

symptom: Need to share sample/script code with customer and ensure compliance with Microsoft legal requirements
solution: Include the standard Sample Code Disclaimer when sharing any code/scripts. Full disclaimer text available in wiki. Reference: OneStop Sample Code Policy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Processes/Case Management/Sample Code Disclaimer

symptom: Azure Policy assigned to AKS cluster but policies are not being enforced or applied. Constraint templates or gatekeeper not functioning.
rootCause: azure-policy pod in kube-system namespace or gatekeeper pods in gatekeeper-system namespace are not running, crashing, or throwing errors.
solution: 1) Verify azure-policy pod running: kubectl get pods -n kube-system. 2) Verify gatekeeper pod running: kubectl get pods -n gatekeeper-system. 3) Check logs for errors. For azure-policy pod issues: collab to Azure Policy team (Azure/Azure Policy/Policy behavior not as expected). For gatekeeper pod issues (crashing/restarting): ICM to Support/EEE AKS with gatekeeper audit and controller-manager pod logs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/Azure Policy/Azure Policy Overview

symptom: Flux extension installation or uninstallation fails with AuthorizationFailed error: 'does not have authorization to perform action Microsoft.ContainerService/managedclusters/extensionaddons/read'
rootCause: Customer subscription not registered to required resource providers: Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration, Microsoft.Kubernetes, and/or Microsoft.ContainerService
solution: Register RPs: az provider register --namespace Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration --wait; az provider register --namespace Microsoft.Kubernetes --wait; az provider register --namespace Microsoft.ContainerService --wait. Verify with az provider show -n <namespace> -o table
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Microsoft.Flux FluxConfiguration TSG

symptom: After enabling addon-autoscaling, VPA recommends too large CPU/memory resources for AKS managed add-ons (CoreDNS, Workload Identity, etc.), causing pods to enter Pending state with FailedScheduling: Insufficient cpu/memory
rootCause: VPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaler) recommended resource requests exceed available node capacity. The add-on pods cannot find a suitable node to schedule on
solution: 1) Enable cluster autoscaler to add more nodes, or create a new node pool with larger VM SKUs. 2) To disable VPA for a specific add-on: set annotation kubernetes.azure.com/override-update-mode: enabled and change updateMode to Off in the VPA CR. 3) To disable addon-autoscaling entirely: az aks update --name <cluster> --resource-group <rg> --disable-addon-autoscaling
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Addon Autoscaling

symptom: Image Integrity policy reports 'failed notation verification' for container images that customer believes are properly signed with notation
rootCause: The verifier and/or certificateStore CRDs are not configured correctly; the certificateStore does not contain the correct signing certificate (CA) for the image notation signature
solution: 1) Verify the image is actually signed with notation. 2) Check verifier CRD configuration. 3) Ensure certificateStore has correct signing cert (CA) info and status shows success: kubectl get certificateStore -o yaml
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Image Integrity

symptom: AKS cluster with managedNATGateway or userAssignedNATGateway outbound type fails operations with NAT gateway related error messages such as 'NAT gateway does not exist', 'not Standard SKU', 'not regional', 'location mismatch', 'has public IP prefixes', or 'zero public IP addresses'.
rootCause: Customer-side configuration issues with the NAT gateway resource: gateway does not exist in expected RG, wrong SKU (must be Standard), has availability zones (must be regional), location mismatch with cluster, public IP prefixes associated instead of IPs, or no public IPs.
solution: Verify: 1) NAT gateway exists in the correct resource group and subscription. 2) SKU is Standard. 3) NAT gateway is regional (no zones). 4) Location matches the cluster. 5) Only public IP addresses (not prefixes) are associated. 6) At least one public IP is associated. For errors with 'EnsureManagedOutboundIPs', 'ProvisioningState not Succeeded', or 'nil IdleTimeoutInMinutes', escalate to NAT Gateway team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Managed NAT Gateway

symptom: Elastic SAN volume cannot be dynamically provisioned in AKS, or mount fails when using ReadWriteMany or ReadOnlyMany access modes with Elastic SAN volumes.
rootCause: Elastic SAN with AKS has known limitations: dynamic provisioning is not currently supported, and only ReadWriteOnce access mode is supported. The iSCSI CSI driver used for Elastic SAN only handles static provisioning.
solution: 1) Use static provisioning for Elastic SAN volumes (manually create PV/PVC); 2) Configure PV/PVC with ReadWriteOnce access mode only; 3) Ensure AKS node pool subnets are configured in Elastic SAN Volume Group network rules; 4) Install the iSCSI CSI driver on the cluster.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Preview Features/Elastic SAN with AKS

symptom: Customer asks support engineer to access portal or make changes in their environment
solution: Decline per Microsoft security policy: We do not access customer data/applications nor make changes on customer side. These policies protect customer from potential issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Case Handling E2E/On Going Cases/Example Scenario Based Responses/Accessing Customer Environment

symptom: Pod creation rejected: pods is forbidden: pod node label selector conflicts with its namespace node label selector. Or pods stuck Pending with FailedScheduling: node(s) did not match node selector. PodNodeSelector admission controller enabled on AKS >= 1.19
rootCause: PodNodeSelector admission controller enforces the namespace annotation scheduler.alpha.kubernetes.io/node-selector. If a pod specifies a conflicting nodeSelector it is rejected. If no nodes match the enforced label, pods remain Pending
solution: 1) Check namespace annotation: kubectl get ns <ns> -o yaml | grep node-selector. 2) Ensure pod nodeSelector matches or remove conflicting nodeSelector from pod spec. 3) Ensure nodes have the required label. 4) Remove the namespace annotation if unintended
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Admission Controllers

symptom: Image Integrity policy reports 'signature artifact not found' for container images, deployment denied or marked non-compliant
rootCause: The container image has not been signed with notation; no signature artifact exists in the registry for this image
solution: Customer must sign the container image using notation before deploying. As a temporary mitigation change the policy effect from Deny to Audit to unblock deployments
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Image Integrity

symptom: Creating managed namespace fails with error: 'The total number of the managed namespaces is 100 in the managed cluster <cluster-name> which exceeds the limit (100) allowed. Please delete some managed namespaces before creating new ones'.
rootCause: The default managed namespace limit per AKS cluster is 100. This is a configurable threshold, not a hard limit. The actual upper bound is governed by ARM limits (800 resources per resource group).
solution: The 100 limit can be increased by enabling the appropriate feature toggle at the cluster or subscription level. An IcM to PG is required to edit the toggle. The actual ARM limit allows up to 800 managed namespaces per resource group.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Managed Namespaces

symptom: Customer needs assistance configuring AKS environment with scripts but issue exceeds break-fix scope
solution: Premier customers: recommend CSA engagement through CSAM (check with account team first). Broad Commercial: refer to Azure Partners (https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/partners/).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Case Handling E2E/On Going Cases/Example Scenario Based Responses/Configuration Script Writing Assistance

symptom: Azure Backup Extension fails to install on AKS cluster or dataprotection-microsoft pods are not running.
rootCause: Extension Manager not deployed correctly in AKS cluster, or Azure Backup deployment (dataprotection-microsoft) fails due to AKS platform issue or network connectivity issue.
solution: 1) Check if Extension Manager is deployed. 2) Check dataprotection-microsoft pods: kubectl get pods -n dataprotection-microsoft. 3) Check extension-agent and extension-operator pods in kube-system. 4) Follow Extension installation TSG (aka.ms/k8s-extensions-TSG). For extension issues: ICM to K8s Connect RP (Cluster Configuration/Cluster Configuration Triage). For backup-specific issues: ICM to ABRS using AKS Backup CRI Diagnostic Template.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/Azure backup extension

symptom: ARM template deployment fails with BadRequest code NotAllAgentPoolOrchestratorVersionSpecifiedAndUnchanged when upgrading a single nodepool using Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters resource type
rootCause: The managedClusters ARM resource type requires all agent pools to be consistently specified; 2022-03-27 API behavioral change prevents implicit nodepool upgrades via cluster-level API
solution: Use Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters/agentPools resource type in ARM template for per-nodepool upgrades (equivalent to az aks nodepool upgrade CLI command)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/NotAllAgentPoolOrchestratorVersionSpecifiedAndUnchanged error when upgrading a single nodepool using ARM

symptom: Pod creation rejected: pod tolerations (possibly merged with namespace default tolerations) conflict with its namespace allowlist. PodTolerationRestriction admission controller on AKS >= 1.19
rootCause: PodTolerationRestriction admission controller verifies pod tolerations against namespace toleration whitelist (annotation scheduler.alpha.kubernetes.io/tolerationsWhitelist). Pod default tolerations (not-ready, unreachable) must also be in the whitelist
solution: 1) Check namespace annotations for tolerationsWhitelist. 2) Ensure ALL required tolerations are in the whitelist, including default tolerations: node.kubernetes.io/not-ready:NoExecute and node.kubernetes.io/unreachable:NoExecute. 3) Add any custom tolerations used by pods to the whitelist
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Admission Controllers

symptom: Attempting to change GPU driver installation mode on existing AKS nodepool fails with error: Changing property 'properties.gpuProfile.installGPUDriver' is not allowed
rootCause: GPU driver installation mode (Install/None) is immutable once configured on a nodepool; AKS does not support changing this property after nodepool creation
solution: Delete and recreate the nodepool with the desired GPU driver installation mode. There is no way to change this in-place
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Linux GPU

symptom: Creating, updating, or deleting managed namespaces fails with error: 'Creating or updating managed namespaces is not allowed when the managed cluster is not in running state' or 'Deleting managed namespaces is not allowed when the managed cluster is not in running state'.
rootCause: Managed namespace operations require the AKS cluster to be in a running state. If the cluster is stopped (deallocated), ARM cannot reconcile the namespace state with the underlying Kubernetes cluster.
solution: Start the AKS cluster first using 'az aks start', then perform the managed namespace create/update/delete operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Managed Namespaces

symptom: Customer asks about ETA for new AKS feature or Kubernetes version GA release
solution: If timeframe available: Product team targets [date], timeline not guaranteed and subject to change. If no timeframe: No ETA available, once provided timeline will not be guaranteed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Case Handling E2E/On Going Cases/Example Scenario Based Responses/ETA Request For Feature or Version

symptom: After upgrading to AKS 1.31+, workloads experience increased latency for container image pull operations or slower node startup compared to pre-1.31 behavior
rootCause: AKS 1.31 changed default image pull mode from serialized to parallel; parallel pulls can cause disk I/O contention in edge case workload patterns
solution: Use ephemeral OS disks; increase OS disk size; use newer/larger VM SKUs; or switch back to serialized image pulls (requires ICM to PG for configuration change)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Parallel Image Pulling

symptom: Agentic CLI for AKS error: The combined size of system_prompt and user_prompt exceeds the maximum context size of 4096 tokens available for input
rootCause: GPT-4 model has a smaller context window (4096 tokens). The combined prompt exceeds this limit
solution: Switch to a model with a larger context window (e.g., GPT-4o, GPT-4-turbo) using the --model parameter
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentic CLI for AKS

symptom: kubectl edit/delete operations on managed Kubernetes namespaces, resource quotas, or network policies fail with errors like 'Updating/deleting namespace <name> labels is not allowed because it is managed by ARM. Please update this namespace through the ARM API.'
rootCause: When Managed Namespaces feature is enabled, ValidatingAdmissionWebhooks (aks-namespace-validating-webhook and aks-networkpolicy-validating-webhook) block direct Kubernetes modifications to managed resources to maintain ARM consistency.
solution: Modifications to managed namespaces, resource quotas (defaultresourcequota), and network policies (defaultnetworkpolicy) must be made through the ARM API (e.g., az aks namespace update) rather than kubectl commands.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Managed Namespaces

symptom: Container Insights tables (ContainerInventory, ContainerLog, InsightsMetrics, etc.) show no data or missing data in Log Analytics workspace.
rootCause: ama-logs pods (daemonset or replicaset) not running, or component failures in the data pipeline: Fluentd (container/K8s inventory), Fluent-bit (container logs), Telegraf (metrics), or MDSD (data export to LA). Also requires cluster to use managed identity (not service principal) - AMA only supports MSI for token/data send.
solution: 1) Verify ama-logs pods running (daemonset + deployment). 2) Confirm cluster uses managed identity, not service principal. 3) Check component logs by table: ContainerInventory->Fluentd (/var/opt/microsoft/docker-cimprov/log/fluentd.log), ContainerLog->Fluent-bit (/var/opt/microsoft/docker-cimprov/log/fluent-bit.log), InsightsMetrics->Telegraf (stderr), all->MDSD (/var/opt/microsoft/linuxmonagent/log/mdsd.*). 4) For Azure Managed Prometheus (ama-metrics pods): check ama-metrics daemonset and deployments in kube-system.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/Container Insights

symptom: Open Service Mesh (OSM) addon is being retired and will be removed on 30 September 2027
rootCause: OSM is deprecated by Microsoft in favor of Istio service mesh
solution: Migrate to Istio following the migration guidance at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/open-service-mesh-istio-migration-guidance
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/Open Service Mesh

symptom: AKS node reports DiskPressure condition; kubelet logs show 'failed to garbage collect required amount of images. Wanted to free X bytes, but freed 0 bytes'; nodes flapping between Ready and NotReady
rootCause: Kubelet image garbage collection fails because it cannot determine the last-used timestamp for some images; images with unknown last-used times are not garbage collected even under disk pressure
solution: Access node via SSH or kubectl debug node <node-name> -it --image=mcr.microsoft.com/cbl-mariner/base/core:2.0; chroot /host; run 'crictl rmi --prune' to manually remove unused images; verify with df -h
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Pruning unused images from nodes

symptom: Agentic CLI error: litellm.NotFoundError: AzureException NotFoundError - The API deployment for this resource does not exist. Or: Could not find model name azure/<name> in litellm model list
rootCause: Azure OpenAI deployment name is different from the model name. The agentic CLI expects them to match
solution: Create a new Azure OpenAI deployment where the deployment name matches the model name exactly (e.g., deployment name gpt-4o for model gpt-4o)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentic CLI for AKS

symptom: AKS VirtualMachines (Mixed SKU) agent pool creation or update fails with VM size validation errors when trying to mix incompatible VM sizes (e.g., GPU with non-GPU, x86 with ARM64).
rootCause: VMs agent pool with mixed SKU requires all VM sizes to be compatible with the same distro and agent pool settings. GPU/non-GPU mixing, x86/ARM64 mixing, and mismatched trusted launch support are not allowed.
solution: Use VM sizes that are compatible within the same agent pool. Ensure all sizes share the same architecture (x86 or ARM64), GPU capability (all GPU or all non-GPU), and support the same security features (e.g., trusted launch).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Mixed SKU Nodepools

symptom: Engineer wants to raise ICM for AKS RP issue but unsure about approval process
rootCause: AKS RP ICMs require EEE team review before submission to avoid noise ICMs and ensure quality
solution: 1) Do NOT open ICM for AKS RP without going through EEE; 2) Consult SME/TA on Teams or Ava first; 3) Get TA approval in Teams channel; 4) Exception: weekends, can open directly if no TA available; 5) Ensure correct SAP; 6) Open from ASC; 7) Sev 2 ICM for RCA needs strong business impact + TA approval
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Processes/ICM Quality Improvement/Improving Quality raising of ICMs

symptom: Cost Analysis data not appearing in Azure portal for AKS cluster. Cost Analysis scraper CronJob/Job failures.
rootCause: Cost Analysis addon requires standard or premium tier (not free) and managed identity. Scraper agent runs in cluster overlay, exports resource usage to AKS PAV2 EventHub -> CostManagement. Failures can be due to agent pod issues, HTTP 5xx errors, or EventHub export problems.
solution: 1) Verify addon active in ASI (may take 24h to sync). 2) Check CCP Pod Events for cost-analysis-* CronJobs. 3) Query CostAnalysis Kusto table for exported data. 4) Check error logs (data.level == 'error'). 5) For agent pod issues: check KubeSystemEvents. 6) If data exported successfully but not showing in portal: escalate to C+E CGI Cost Management/AIP-ACISTORE.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/Cost Analysis Addon

symptom: AKS Dapr sidecar fails to initialize with error: error creating CA: issuer chain does not belong to trust anchors: x509: certificate has expired
rootCause: Dapr mTLS self-signed CA certificate has expired. Default validity is only 1 year for Dapr self-signed certificates.
solution: Manually rotate CA certs per MS docs (Configure Dapr extension > Generate new self-signed cert). Note: rotation causes downtime. Recommend customers use their own certificates instead of Dapr self-signed certs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/dapr sidecar initialization failure

symptom: Agentic CLI error: litellm.llms.azure.common_utils.AzureOpenAIError: Error code 400 - Unsupported value: temperature does not support 1E-8
rootCause: Default temperature parameter value (1E-8) is not supported by the model/deployment
solution: Set environment variable TEMPERATURE=1: export TEMPERATURE=1
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentic CLI for AKS

symptom: AKS VirtualMachines (Mixed SKU) agent pool scale profile update fails or produces unexpected results when customer provides only a partial virtualMachinesProfile.scale.manual array via REST API.
rootCause: The REST API replaces the entire scale profile with the new input rather than merging it. Partial PUT on the virtualMachinesProfile.scale field is not supported. CLI internally fetches and merges but REST API does not.
solution: Always provide the full desired state of the scale profile (all manual entries) when updating via REST API. When using CLI, use --current-vm-size flag which handles the merge automatically.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Mixed SKU Nodepools

symptom: Error: Creating Windows node pools using ARM64 SKU is not supported
rootCause: ARM64 is not supported for Windows node pools in AKS
solution: Use Linux node pools for ARM64 workloads. Windows workloads must use non-ARM64 VM sizes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Arm64 node pools

symptom: Agentic CLI error on Windows: ImportError: DLL load failed while importing win32file
rootCause: Corrupted or incompatible Azure CLI installation on Windows, missing win32file DLL dependency
solution: Uninstall and reinstall the latest Azure CLI client
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentic CLI for AKS

symptom: After adding a new load balancer configuration via 'az aks loadbalancer add', a Kubernetes LoadBalancer service created immediately targeting the new LB is not placed on it. The service goes to an existing load balancer instead.
rootCause: After 'az aks loadbalancer' commands update HCP and cloud provider config secret, cloud-controller-manager needs additional time to reload the new configuration. The AKS operation returns successfully before the reload completes.
solution: After adding/updating a load balancer configuration, monitor Kubernetes events for the 'EnsuredLoadBalancer' event which marks the completion of cloud provider restart. Only then create services targeting the new load balancer.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Multiple Standard Load Balancers

symptom: Error: CVM enabled clusters are not supported for ARM64 enabled nodes when using ARM64 with CVM, Trusted Launch, or Pod Sandboxing
rootCause: ARM64 is incompatible with CVM, Trusted Launch, and Pod Sandboxing (Confidential Containers) in AKS
solution: Cannot combine ARM64 with these security features. Choose either ARM64 or CVM/Trusted Launch/Pod Sandboxing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Arm64 node pools

symptom: Agentic CLI error: litellm.AuthenticationError: AzureException AuthenticationError - Access denied due to invalid subscription key
rootCause: Incorrect or conflicting Azure OpenAI configuration. The tool checks flags first, then config file, then environment variables. A mismatch causes authentication failure
solution: Verify the Azure OpenAI configuration: 1) Check config file (aksAgent.yaml) for correct API key and endpoint. 2) Check environment variables. 3) Check command-line flags. Priority order: flags > config file > environment variables
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentic CLI for AKS

symptom: AKS cluster creation or update fails with error: 'A hash conflict was encountered for the deny Assignment ID. Please use a new Guid.' when NRG Lockdown feature is enabled.
rootCause: This is a known issue within the RBAC dependency. A hash collision occurs when generating the deny assignment GUID for the node resource group, and AKS has no control over the RBAC internal GUID generation.
solution: Create a new cluster with a different NRG name. This can be done by directly specifying a new node resource group name in the API, or by changing the cluster name or resource group which causes a different NRG name to be generated.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/NRG Lockdown

symptom: Error: Creating FIPS-enabled node pools using ARM64 SKU is not supported for non-Azure Linux V3 OS Skus
rootCause: FIPS on ARM64 is only supported with AzureLinux 3.0+ (Kubernetes 1.32+). Ubuntu and AzureLinux 2.0 are not supported.
solution: Use AzureLinux 3.0+ (k8s 1.32+) OS SKU for FIPS-enabled ARM64 node pools.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Arm64 node pools

symptom: ErrorCode_InvalidCustomKubeletConfig on Windows nodepool: cpuManagerPolicy, cpuCfsQuota, cpuCfsQuotaPeriod, topologyManagerPolicy, allowedUnsafeSysctls, or podMaxPids is not supported by Windows. Also CustomLinuxOSConfig cannot be set for Windows agent pools
rootCause: By design - these kubelet config parameters and Linux OS config are not supported on Windows node pools in AKS
solution: Remove unsupported kubelet config parameters (cpuManagerPolicy, cpuCfsQuota, cpuCfsQuotaPeriod, topologyManagerPolicy, allowedUnsafeSysctls, podMaxPids) for Windows node pools. Do not set CustomLinuxOSConfig for Windows agent pools
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Custom Node Config

symptom: ErrorCode_CustomKubeletConfigOrCustomLinuxOSConfigCanNotBeChanged: CustomKubeletConfig or CustomLinuxOSConfig can not be changed for this operation when trying to update nodepool or cluster
rootCause: By design - CustomKubeletConfig and CustomLinuxOSConfig can only be set during initial node pool creation, not during update operations
solution: Create a new node pool with the desired CustomKubeletConfig/CustomLinuxOSConfig settings. These cannot be modified after creation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Custom Node Config

symptom: Unrecognized arguments --crg-id error when creating AKS cluster or nodepool with Capacity Reservation Group
rootCause: aks-preview CLI extension version is outdated and does not support --crg-id parameter
solution: Run: az extension update --name aks-preview to upgrade to the latest version.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Capacity Reservation Groups

symptom: Container logs do not get rotated upon hitting --container-log-max-size on Windows nodes. Log files grow unbounded despite kubelet log rotation configuration
rootCause: Kubelet log compression bug on Windows in k8s versions prior to v1.23.13, v1.24.7, and v1.25.0. Fixed in kubernetes/kubernetes#111548. Even after patching, heavy log writes may still cause rotation delays (upstream issue #110630)
solution: Upgrade k8s version to v1.23.15+ or v1.24.9+ or v1.25.0+. If logs still do not rotate after upgrade during heavy writes: restart containers, use higher disk I/O throughput VMs, or redirect logs to persistent volume
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Custom Node Config

symptom: LinkedAuthorizationFailed error when creating AKS cluster/nodepool with CRG: client does not have permission to perform write on linked scope
rootCause: AzureContainerService app/identity does not have permission to write to the Capacity Reservation Group resource group
solution: Assign the AKS service principal/managed identity write permission (Contributor role) on the CRG resource group.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Capacity Reservation Groups

symptom: Delete Machines API returns NotFound with resultSubCode GetAgentPool_NotFound: Could not find the agentpool in subscription/resourceGroup/resourceName
rootCause: Agentpool is not registered in DB yet, or the target URI does not have the correct agentpool name as path parameter
solution: Verify the agentpool exists by checking List AgentPools API response or GET Agentpool. Ensure the correct agentpool name is used in the request URI
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Delete Specific Node Machine

symptom: CreateVMSSAgentPoolFailed with BadRequest: Id is not a valid resource reference when using Capacity Reservation Group
rootCause: Customer provided incorrect CRG resource ID (e.g., pointing to capacityReservation instead of the group, or invalid format)
solution: Verify CRG resource ID is correct and points to the capacityReservationGroup, not individual reservations. Check CR inside CRG is in good state.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Capacity Reservation Groups

symptom: Delete Machines API returns InvalidParameter: Cannot find any valid machines to delete. Please check your input machine names
rootCause: Customer provided invalid machine names in the DELETE request body. Machine names must match existing VMSS instances in the agent pool
solution: Run az aks machine list to get valid machine names. Provide correct names in request body: {machineNames: [aks-agentpool0-xxxxx-vmss000001]}
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Delete Specific Node Machine

symptom: Azure Portal does not allow AKS cluster to scale past 1000 nodes even though the 5000 node limit has been increased via ICM
rootCause: Portal UI has a hardcoded 1000 node limit that has not been updated
solution: Use Azure CLI to scale the cluster past 1000 nodes. Portal will be updated in the future.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/5k Node Limit

symptom: Deployment Safeguards (Guardrails) enable fails with errors: Invalid K8s version, Azure policy not enabled, GuardrailsPreview feature not registered, invalid version, or Azure Policy checkaccess not allowed (missing permissions)
rootCause: Multiple prerequisites not met: K8s >= 1.19, Azure Policy Add-On enabled, GuardrailsPreview feature flag registered, and Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/read+write permissions. Contributor role is NOT sufficient for the write permission
solution: Ensure all prerequisites: 1) upgrade cluster to k8s >= 1.19, 2) enable Azure Policy Add-On, 3) register Microsoft.ContainerService/GuardrailsPreview feature flag, 4) grant Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/write permission (beyond Contributor). AKS RP calls Azure Policy OBO customer so both need permissions
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Deployment Safeguards (Azure Policy)

symptom: AKS node pool goes into failed state or takes very long when scaling by 200+ nodes, though all nodes are Ready in the cluster
rootCause: Internal bug: VMSS instance update for Application Security Groups is slow when processing large batches
solution: Scale up in smaller batches instead of large increments (avoid 200+ nodes at once).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/5k Node Limit

symptom: Deployment Safeguards (Guardrails) policy assignment does not appear on cluster after enabling. Customer cannot see guardrails-policy-assignment
rootCause: Expected behavior - Azure Policy assignment propagation to the AKS cluster can take up to 75 minutes after initial creation
solution: Wait up to 75 minutes for the assignment to appear. Verify via: az policy assignment show --scope <MC_resource_id> -n guardrails-policy-assignment. Check constraint download errors via GuardrailsContextActivity Kusto table. Escalate constraint issues to Azure Policy team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Deployment Safeguards (Azure Policy)

symptom: 429 TooManyRequests / OperationNotAllowed errors during AKS scale-up, cluster creation, or upgrades; cluster autoscaler unable to scale
rootCause: Azure API write/read throttling limits exceeded (e.g., HighCostGetVMScaleSet30Min quota)
solution: Run ARM throttling Kusto query in armprodgbl to identify throttled API requests by operationName/userAgent. File ICM to AKS/RP queue with query results.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/5k Node Limit

symptom: Customer wants to disable CSI storage drivers and use in-tree drivers, or needs to troubleshoot CSI driver enable/disable operations on AKS cluster
solution: Use CLI flags: --disable-disk-driver / --disable-file-driver / --disable-snapshot-controller (and --enable-* to re-enable). Check status in ASI Feature section. Troubleshoot: check kube-addon-manager logs, OverlayMgrEvents, Frontend/Async Kusto tables. Escalate to PG for unresolvable issues
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Disable CSI Drivers

symptom: Sporadic egress connectivity failures on AKS clusters with >500 nodes due to SNAT port exhaustion
rootCause: SNAT port exhaustion from high outbound connection count at large scale
solution: Use AKS Managed NAT Gateway add-on (supports up to 64,512 flows per IP, max 16 IPs). See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/nat-gateway
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/5k Node Limit

symptom: Dual-stack (IPv4/IPv6) kubenet cluster cannot scale beyond 200 nodes. Node additions fail or route table limit exceeded
rootCause: Azure route table limit is 400 routes. In dual-stack kubenet, each node requires 2 routes (one for IPv4, one for IPv6), limiting the cluster to 200 nodes maximum
solution: This is a platform limitation. For >200 nodes with dual-stack, migrate to CNI Overlay dual-stack (no route tables). For kubenet dual-stack, stay within 200-node limit. Also note: LoadBalancer services do not support ExternalTrafficPolicy:Cluster for Mariner nodes in dual-stack
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Dual-Stack IPv6

symptom: Internal load balancer service creation is slow or fails with timeout on AKS clusters with >750 nodes
rootCause: Known performance bottleneck in AKS for service/ILB creation at large node scale
solution: Scale down to <750 nodes, create internal LB service (type LoadBalancer with azure-load-balancer-internal annotation), then scale back up. Or create ILB at cluster creation before scaling.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/5k Node Limit

symptom: 401 Unauthorized errors when authenticating to AKS API server using external identity provider (GitHub OIDC, Google OAuth). Token validation fails at API server
rootCause: Token audience (aud claim) mismatch with JWT authenticator config, or issuer URL does not exactly match iss claim (including protocol/trailing slashes), or identity provider OAuth/OIDC settings are incorrect
solution: 1) Verify audience claim in token matches JWT authenticator config. 2) Correct issuer URL to exactly match iss claim. 3) For GitHub: ensure workflow has id-token:write permission and audience matches. 4) For Google: verify OAuth 2.0 client ID/secret. Update: az aks jwtauthenticator update --config-file updated-jwt-config.json
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/External Identity Providers for AKS Control Plane

symptom: Kaito gpu-provisioner controller pod in failed state with panic: Configure azure client fails. Please ensure federated credential has been created for identity
rootCause: Federated credential has not been created for the Kaito addon identity ai-toolchain-operator-CLUSTER_NAME
solution: Create federated credential and role assignment for the identity. Verify with: az identity federated-credential list --identity-name ai-toolchain-operator-$CLUSTER_NAME -g $RESOURCE_GROUP
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/AI Toolchain Operator (Kaito)

symptom: AKS external identity provider authentication fails: username/groups not recognized or conflicts. Missing required aks:jwt: prefix in claim mappings or invalid CEL expressions
rootCause: JWT authenticator claim mappings missing aks:jwt: prefix (required to prevent conflicts with other auth methods), or CEL expressions have syntax errors or return unexpected data types
solution: Update JWT authenticator config: 1) Add aks:jwt: prefix to all username/groups mappings (e.g. username: aks:jwt:github: + claims.sub). 2) Test CEL expressions with evaluator for correct return types (string for username, array of strings for groups). 3) Add userValidationRules to block system-prefixed usernames
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/External Identity Providers for AKS Control Plane

symptom: Kaito workspace RESOURCEREADY condition not true after 10+ minutes; GPU node never created
rootCause: Possible causes: (1) Identity lacks Contributor role on resource group, (2) Subscription lacks GPU SKU quota, (3) GPU instance type unavailable in region
solution: Check machine CR status for errors. Verify role assignment: az role assignment list --scope /subscriptions/$SUB/resourceGroups/$RG. Request quota increase if needed. Check GPU SKU regional availability.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/AI Toolchain Operator (Kaito)

symptom: AKS cluster minor version upgrade fails with UpgradeBlockedOnDeprecatedAPIUsage: Control Plane upgrade is blocked due to recent usage of a Kubernetes API deprecated in the specified version
rootCause: AKS fail-fast feature detects deprecated k8s API usage within last 1-12 hours for the target version. Applies to minor version MC upgrades, k8s >= 1.26, preview API >= 2023-01-02-preview. Detection via CCP prometheus + remediator recording in configmap
solution: Option 1: Remove deprecated API usage and wait 12 hours for records to expire. Option 2: Set upgradeSettings.overrideSettings with controlPlaneOverrides=[IgnoreKubernetesDeprecations] and until timestamp to bypass. Find usage: query kube-audit logs (AzureDiagnostics where Category == kube-audit and log_s has deprecated). Internal: CPRemediatorLogs or kubectl get configmap cluster-upgrade-configmap -n <ccpNamespace>
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Fail Fast Upgrade on Breaking API Changes

symptom: Kaito workspace INFERENCEREADY condition not true; inference pod starts but never becomes ready or cannot pull image
rootCause: Image pull failure (private mode: invalid URL or pull secrets), or inference image issue. Note: preset images are 30-100GB, expect long pull times.
solution: For image pull errors with private images: validate image URL and pull secrets. For preset model issues: contact Kaito dev team (kaito-dev@microsoft.com). For custom images: customer responsibility.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/AI Toolchain Operator (Kaito)

symptom: InvalidOSSKU error when creating AKS cluster/nodepool with osSku=Flatcar: Subscription needs to register AKSFlatcarPreview feature flag
rootCause: Flatcar Container Linux for AKS is in preview and requires explicit feature flag registration on the subscription
solution: Register the AKSFlatcarPreview feature flag: az feature register --namespace Microsoft.ContainerService --name AKSFlatcarPreview. Wait for propagation then retry
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Flatcar Container Linux for AKS

symptom: Error when updating existing AKS cluster to Automatic SKU because cluster SKU tier is not standard
rootCause: AKS Automatic SKU requires Standard tier as prerequisite
solution: Run: az aks update -g <resource-group> -n <cluster-name> --tier standard before converting to Automatic.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/AKS Automatic SKU

symptom: Flatcar nodepool creation fails with InvalidOSSKU/CVMIncompatibleWithConfig/BadRequest when combined with unsupported features: FIPS, SecurityPatch channel, Gen1 VMs, CVM, Artifact Streaming, Trusted Launch, or Pod Sandboxing
rootCause: Flatcar Container Linux has specific limitations: no FIPS, no SecurityPatch channel (only NodeImage/None), Gen2 VMs only, no CVM, no Artifact Streaming, no Trusted Launch, no Pod Sandboxing, no GPU workloads
solution: Remove the incompatible feature. Use Gen2 VM sizes. Use NodeImage or None upgrade channels. GPU workloads blocked pending AgentBaker support. Create new nodepools with Flatcar; OS migration to Flatcar is not supported
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Flatcar Container Linux for AKS

symptom: Multiple validation errors when updating existing AKS cluster to Automatic SKU: missing required features/addons (azure-policy, apiserver-vnet-integration, image-cleaner, safeguards, auto-upgrade, NAT gateway, web-app-routing, keyvault-secrets-provider, workload-identity, CNI overlay with Cilium, VPA, KEDA, NRG lockdown, node-provisioning, availability-zones, ephemeral-os-disks, node-os-upgrade, disable-ssh, system-pool-taint, azure-rbac, disable-local-accounts)
rootCause: AKS Automatic requires 20+ features/addons to be enabled. Some (AvailabilityZones, Web App Routing, Ephemeral OS Disks) must be enabled at cluster creation time.
solution: Enable each required feature using specific az aks CLI commands. Key creation-time requirements: --zones 1 2 3, --enable-app-routing, --node-osdisk-type Ephemeral. See ADO wiki for full list of CLI commands per error message.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/AKS Automatic SKU

symptom: InvalidOSSKU when trying to change existing nodepool OSSKU to Flatcar: Only allowed to change OSSKU to Ubuntu, AzureLinux, Ubuntu2404, Ubuntu2204, AzureLinux3
rootCause: OS migration to Flatcar is not currently supported. OSSKU change only allows migration between Ubuntu and AzureLinux variants
solution: Create a new node pool with osSku=Flatcar. Cordon/drain the old nodepool, move workloads, then delete the old nodepool
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Flatcar Container Linux for AKS

symptom: Host Network NSG Control: Reconcile application security group failed error, or customer cannot access through specified host ports on nodepool with allowed-host-ports configured
rootCause: Feature not enabled or misconfigured: allowed-host-ports range does not match port being accessed, NSG rules not created/reconciled, or resource is on nodepool without feature enabled
solution: 1) Verify feature enabled in ASI nodepool page. 2) Check allowed-host-ports matches desired ports. 3) Verify NSG has rules allowing allowed-host-ports with ASG (do not manually modify NSG). 4) Reconcile cluster if NSG rules missing. Update config via CLI
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Host Network NSG Control

symptom: Host Network NSG Control: cannot delete managed ASG because there are still references to it from VMSS or NSG. ASG deletion fails with reference error
rootCause: Managed ASG was deleted before VMSS and NSG references were removed
solution: Manually update VMSS and NSG to remove references to the managed ASG, then reconcile the agent pool
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Host Network NSG Control

symptom: AKS cluster delete fails with ResourceGroupDeletionBlocked and inner error NetworkProfileAlreadyInUseWithContainerNics - network profile vk-* (Virtual Kubelet/ACI) still in use with container NICs
rootCause: ACI network profile with vk- prefix (Virtual Nodes) still has container NICs bound to it. Known bug with supposed fix (PR 4202309) but may have regression. Tracked in Known Issue 14104.
solution: No customer-ready mitigation. Escalate via IcM to Support/EEE ACI. Provide: AKS operation ID, correlation ID, ARM logs showing HTTP 400 against Microsoft.Network RP on the vk- network profile.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Cluster delete with NetworkProfileAlreadyInUseWithContainerNics

symptom: Cannot delete subnet or VNet after AKS cluster deletion - subnet is delegated to Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters with lingering ServiceAssociationLink (SAL). Error: InUseSubnetCannotBeDeleted
rootCause: When AKS cluster uses Azure CNI Overlay or Pod Subnet, subnet delegation creates a ServiceAssociationLink (SAL). After cluster deletion, SAL should be auto-removed but sometimes persists.
solution: 1) Remove delegation via Azure Portal: VNet > Subnets > select subnet > Delegate subnet = None > Save. 2) Via CLI: az network vnet subnet update --remove delegations. 3) If above fails, use Jarvis actions for undelegation (CSS can perform, except China which is restricted).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Undelegate Subnet

symptom: ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady during CRUD/Start operations. kube-proxy pods stuck in Pending/ImagePullBackOff. Kubelet logs: x509 certificate signed by unknown authority when pulling images from mcr.microsoft.com.
rootCause: Third-party firewall performing SSL deep packet inspection intercepts and re-signs TLS certificates for container image pulls, causing x509 certificate validation failure on AKS nodes.
solution: Ask customer to disable SSL packet inspection on firewall for traffic to mcr.microsoft.com and container registries, then retry. Verify via SSH/node-shell into affected node checking kubelet/containerd logs for x509 errors.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady because kube proxy stuck pending

symptom: AKS operations fail with ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady/PodInitializing, kube-proxy stuck Pending with ImagePullBackOff, kubelet logs show x509: certificate signed by unknown authority
rootCause: Third-party firewall performing SSL/TLS deep packet inspection between AKS nodes and MCR, causing certificate validation failure when pulling container images.
solution: Ask customer to temporarily disable firewall or third-party SSL inspection device, retry the operation. Verify by SSH into affected node checking kubelet/containerd logs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady because kube proxy stuck pending

symptom: ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady during CRUD operations. Error mentions pods not in Running status but pod names are user workloads (e.g. 3rd-party metrics-server, custom jobs) deployed in kube-system namespace.
rootCause: AKS RP checks all pods in kube-system namespace to determine control plane readiness. User workload pods in kube-system in Succeeded/Failed/Pending state trigger false-positive ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady errors.
solution: Advise customer to move user workloads out of kube-system namespace per AKS FAQ. Delete or relocate completed Job pods from kube-system. Retry cluster operation after cleanup.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady because kube proxy stuck pending

symptom: AKS CRUD/Start operations fail with ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady, error references non-AKS pods (custom metrics-server, user jobs) in kube-system namespace not reaching Running state
rootCause: AKS RP checks ALL pods in kube-system namespace during operations. User workloads in Completed/Failed/Pending state cause AKS RP to misinterpret them as control plane addon failures.
solution: Advise customer to move all user workloads out of kube-system namespace per AKS FAQ. Verify failing pods are customer pods by checking CCP logs and pod labels.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady because kube proxy stuck pending

symptom: GetSurgedVms_CountNotMatch during nodepool upgrade. Autoscaler deletes surge node while AKS RP upgrade is in progress. Existing nodes upgraded in-place instead of creating new surge nodes. Pod eviction takes 5-10 min per node.
rootCause: Race condition between AKS RP and Cluster Autoscaler. Autoscaler issues forceDelete on surge nodes when RP sets new VMSS capacity. ScaleDownDisabledReasonUpgrade annotation not applied to surge node.
solution: Disable cluster autoscaler before nodepool upgrades (az aks nodepool update --disable-cluster-autoscaler), re-enable after upgrade completes. Known bug: ADO 30155571/31289428.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/GetSurgedVms_CountNotMatch_for_upgrade

symptom: AKS nodepool upgrade fails with GetSurgedVms_CountNotMatch or AgentCountNotMatch. Surge node deleted by cluster autoscaler during upgrade. Pod eviction takes 5-10 min per node.
rootCause: Race condition between AKS RP and cluster autoscaler: autoscaler forceDeletes surge nodes while RP is upgrading. ScaleDownDisabledReasonUpgrade annotation not applied to surge node. Bug 30155571, Issue 31289428.
solution: Disable cluster autoscaler before nodepool upgrades, re-enable after completion.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/GetSurgedVms_CountNotMatch_for_upgrade

symptom: Nodepool operations (scale, add disk) fail with MMAExtension error: Enable failed exit code 53, incorrect workspace key. OMSAgent extension failure blocks VMSS operations.
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace key in AKS omsagent-secret does not match actual LAW primary key. Or NSG rules block outbound to Log Analytics agent endpoints.
solution: Compare workspace key: kubectl get secrets omsagent-secret -n kube-system -o yaml, decode base64, compare with LAW Portal Agents Management Primary Key. If keys match, check NSG/firewall for blocked outbound to LA endpoints.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Invalid workspace key fails cluster operations

symptom: AKS nodepool operations fail with ProvisioningError on MMAExtension exit code 53: Installation failed due to incorrect workspace key
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace key in AKS (omsagent-secret) mismatches actual key, OR node cannot reach Log Analytics endpoints due to NSG/firewall.
solution: 1) Compare omsagent-secret (base64 decoded) with Log Analytics Primary Key. 2) Check outbound NSG rules for LA endpoints. 3) Engage Azure Monitor team if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Invalid workspace key fails cluster operations

symptom: Newly created nodes not registering with AKS API server. ScaleUpTimedOut after ~15min. VMSS creates VM but node never joins cluster. Persists with CAS disabled and manual scale.
rootCause: vmssCSE (Custom Script Extension) accidentally removed from VMSS. Without CSE, nodes lack bootstrap components and cannot register with K8s API server. Required extensions: vmssCSE, AKSLinuxBilling, AKSLinuxExtension.
solution: Verify all 3 VMSS extensions present. If vmssCSE missing, create new agent pool and migrate workloads (new pool will have correct extensions).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/New nodes are not getting registered with AKS API server

symptom: New AKS nodes not registering with API server. VMSS creates VMs but they never join cluster. Autoscaler ScaleUpTimedOut after 15 min.
rootCause: vmssCSE (Custom Script Extension) accidentally removed from VMSS. Without CSE, nodes cannot bootstrap kubelet/CNI. Required: vmssCSE, AKSLinuxBilling, AKSLinuxExtension.
solution: Create new agent pool, migrate workloads. Verify new VMSS has all 3 required extensions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/New nodes are not getting registered with AKS API server

symptom: NAP nodes stuck NotReady in Azure CNI Overlay cluster. NodeNetworkConfig events: primaryIP is nil, Subnet is full. New nodes cannot obtain Pod IPs.
rootCause: Pod CIDR exhaustion in CNI Overlay. Each node pre-allocates /24 block (256 IPs). Small Pod CIDR (e.g. /21=8 nodes max) runs out, new nodes cannot get Pod IPs and remain NotReady.
solution: Expand Pod CIDR without cluster recreation: see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/azure-cni-overlay-pod-expand. After expansion, NotReady nodes auto-recover. azure-cns pods redeploy but existing pods unaffected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node Auto Provision subnet is full and pod cidr exhausted

symptom: AKS NAP/Karpenter nodes stuck NotReady. NodeNetworkConfig events: primaryIP is nil, Subnet is full. NodeClaim Ready=Unknown.
rootCause: Pod CIDR exhaustion in CNI Overlay. Each node pre-allocates /24 (256 IPs). Small Pod CIDR (e.g. /21=8 nodes max) causes IP allocation failure.
solution: Expand Pod CIDR via Azure CNI Overlay expansion (learn.microsoft.com/azure/aks/azure-cni-overlay-pod-expand). NotReady nodes auto-recover.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node Auto Provision subnet is full and pod cidr exhausted

symptom: Cluster or node pool creation fails with SKU Not Found or VM Size Not Available error.
rootCause: Requested VM SKU restricted, unavailable, or not offered in target region/zone due to platform capacity limitations or subscription SKU restrictions.
solution: Check SKU availability: az vm list-skus --location <region> --size <sku>. Check ASC Resource Explorer SKU Restrictions. Use alternative VM size or zone. For quota increase route to SAP: Azure > Subscription management > Compute-VM subscription limit increases.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node scaling fails due to allocation or capacity issues

symptom: AKS cluster/nodepool creation or scaling fails with SkuNotAvailable or VM size not available error
rootCause: Requested VM SKU restricted in subscription, not offered in target region/zone, or has zonal capacity limitations.
solution: 1) az vm list-skus to check availability. 2) ASC Resource Explorer > SKU Restrictions. 3) Request quota increase or select alternative VM. 4) Transfer to SAP Compute-VM subscription limit increases if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node scaling fails due to allocation or capacity issues

symptom: AKS node scaling operations fail due to vCPU quota exceeded. Total Regional vCPU or VM family quota limit reached.
rootCause: vCPU quotas (Total Regional + VM family) exceeded. VMSS cannot allocate new instances when either limit is reached.
solution: Customer submits quota increase via Azure Portal (standard VM families often auto-approved). For high-demand sizes, route to SAP: Azure > Subscription management > Compute-VM subscription limit increases. Include Sub ID, Region, SKU, Restriction Type, cores requested. Do NOT transfer to VM/VMSS team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node scaling fails due to allocation or capacity issues

symptom: AKS node scaling fails with QuotaExceeded. Exceeds vCPU quota for VM size family or total regional quota.
rootCause: Subscription vCPU quota exhausted. Azure two-tier quota: VM-family-specific + total regional vCPU limit.
solution: 1) Submit quota increase via Portal (auto-approved for standard families). 2) Transfer to SAP Compute-VM subscription limit increases. Include: Sub ID, Region, SKU, Restriction Type, cores. Do NOT transfer to VM/VMSS team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node scaling fails due to allocation or capacity issues

symptom: AKS operations fail with Unable to get log analytics workspace info ResourceNotFound. Error misleadingly points to monitoring addon but may be Container Insights, OMSAgent, or Azure Defender.
rootCause: Log Analytics Workspace referenced by AKS addon has been deleted or is inaccessible. AKS pre-flight validates LAW existence, blocking all operations when LAW missing.
solution: Identify which component references missing LAW in ASC. For Defender: az aks update --disable-defender then --enable-defender. For monitoring addon: disable via https://aka.ms/aks-disable-monitoring-addon or re-create LAW and update addon config.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Operation fails due to missing Log Analytics workspace

symptom: AKS operations fail with ResourceNotFound: Unable to get log analytics workspace info. Error misleadingly references monitoring addon but may be Azure Defender or Container Insights.
rootCause: LAW referenced by monitoring addon, OMSAgent, or Azure Defender has been deleted or is inaccessible.
solution: 1) In ASC, identify which component uses the LAW. 2) Defender-linked: az aks update --disable-defender then --enable-defender. 3) OMSAgent-linked: disable monitoring addon, re-enable with valid LAW. 4) Custom LAW: use --defender-config with logAnalyticsWorkspaceResourceID.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Operation fails due to missing Log Analytics workspace
21vNote: Defender for Containers not supported in 21Vianet. OMSAgent scenario may still apply.

symptom: AKS CRUD operations (upgrade/scale) fail with InternalServerError. Underlying error: InvalidAuthenticationToken - access token missing claims puid/altsecid/oid, or GetSKUStoreError when AKS calls CRP for VM SKUs
rootCause: Service Principal linked to the AKS PG App ID was removed from the customer tenant, breaking AKS RP ability to authenticate against ARM/CRP on behalf of the cluster.
solution: 1) Confirm via AKS ContextActivity Kusto query filtering by operationID for InvalidAuthenticationToken messages. 2) Cross-reference ARM Traces table using correlationID to identify affected TenantID and AppID. 3) Re-create the Service Principal in the customer tenant for the AKS PG App ID.
