sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Operations Fail with code GetSKUStoreError InvalidAuthenticationToken

symptom: AKS operations fail with InvalidLoadBalancerProfileAllocatedOutboundPorts - allocated outbound ports not in allowable range given node count and outbound IPs. SNAT port exhaustion possible.
rootCause: Load balancer outbound port allocation formula violated: allocatedPortsPerNode * totalNodeCount exceeds 64000 * numberOfOutboundIPs. Each outbound IP provides 64000 SNAT ports; default 1024 ports per node for <=50 nodes.
solution: Before any scale/update operation, verify formula: 64000 * outbound_IPs / ports_per_node >= total_nodes (including maxSurge for upgrades). Fix by either: increasing outbound IP count, or reducing allocated outbound ports per node.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Operations fail with code InvalidLoadBalancerProfileAllocatedOutboundPorts

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with QuotaExceeded/ManagedClusterCountExceedsQuotaLimit - exceeds per-subscription per-region managed cluster count quota (enforced since Sep 2, 2025)
rootCause: AKS cluster count quota limit reached. AKS enforces maximum cluster count per subscription per region. Ineligible subscription types (Free Trial, Students, DreamSpark) cannot request increases.
solution: Customer must file NEW support ticket via correct path: Azure/Subscription limits and quota/Azure Kubernetes Service. Do NOT accept via AKS support path. ICM auto-created to AzureKubernetesService/ClusterQuotaRequests (Sev 3). Decline ineligible sub types, route internal to AZURECONTAINERSERVICE/Platform.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/QuotaExceeded QuotaExceededManagedCluster

symptom: AKS nodepool scale/upgrade fails with InvalidParameter: images referenced from deployment template are not found (e.g. WindowsServer 2019-datacenter-core-smalldisk)
rootCause: Kubernetes version is outdated/unsupported and the base VM images are no longer available in Azure marketplace. Old nodepool cannot create new nodes because the image reference is stale.
solution: 1) Create new nodepool with supported K8s version. 2) Copy custom labels. 3) Drain+cordon old nodes. 4) Delete old nodepool. 5) If cluster still Failed, az aks upgrade to same/higher version.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Scale or upgrade failed with image not found

symptom: AKS nodepool operations (scale/upgrade/reconcile) fail with VMExtensionHandlerNonTransientError - non-retriable failure from unsupported VM extension (e.g. DependencyAgentLinux) on VMSS
rootCause: Custom VM extensions manually installed at VMSS level outside AKS control. AKS manages VMSS lifecycle and cannot validate/maintain custom extensions, causing non-retriable reconcile failures.
solution: 1) Identify via AppLens detector or VMSS Extensions tab. 2) Remove extensions from VMSS (Portal/CLI/ARM). 3) Retry or reconcile. 4) If impossible, recreate nodepool. Never manually install VM extensions on AKS VMSS.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/UnsupportedVMExtensions

symptom: AKS cluster stuck in Starting state or autoscaler scale-up creates VMSS instances but new nodes never appear. Kusto shows 'has no vmss cse extension' errors.
rootCause: vmssCSE (Custom Script Extension) was removed from VMSS of the affected nodepool(s). Without vmssCSE, nodes cannot bootstrap and register with API server.
solution: 1) Abort ongoing operation: az aks operation-abort. 2) Confirm abort (Status=Canceled). 3) Upgrade nodepool image: az aks nodepool upgrade --node-image-only. This restores vmssCSE.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/vmssCSE deleted

symptom: AKS cluster CRUD operations (upgrade, scale) fail with InternalServerError/GetSKUStoreError. Underlying error: InvalidAuthenticationToken - The received access token is not valid: at least one of the claims puid or altsecid or oid should be present
rootCause: The Service Principal linked to the AKS PG well-known App ID (7319c514-..., Azure Container Service - AKS) was deleted or soft-deleted from the customer tenant, breaking AKS RP operations when calling CRP to get VM SKUs.
solution: Re-register the AKS resource provider to recreate the SP: az provider register --namespace Microsoft.ContainerService. If SP is in Soft-Delete mode, customer can try recovering it (may require collaboration with Azure Identity team). Confirm via AKS ContextActivity Kusto table and ARM Traces, check SP status in Azure TenantExplorer.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Operations Fail with code GetSKUStoreError InvalidAuthenticationToken

symptom: AKS cluster operations (update, scale up) fail with error code InvalidLoadBalancerProfileAllocatedOutboundPorts - Load balancer profile allocated ports X is not in an allowable range given the number of nodes and IPs provisioned
rootCause: The specified node count, number of outbound frontend IP addresses and number of SNAT ports per node do not add up to a viable configuration. Each outbound IP provides 64000 SNAT ports, and the total available ports must cover all nodes including surge nodes during upgrades.
solution: Use the formula: 64000 ports per IP / outbound_ports_per_node * number_of_outbound_IPs = max_nodes. Increase outbound frontend IPs (each adds 64000 ports) or reduce allocated outbound ports per node. Account for maxSurge buffer nodes during upgrades (default 1).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Operations fail with code InvalidLoadBalancerProfileAllocatedOutboundPorts

symptom: AKS cluster create/deploy/scale fails with QuotaExceeded - Operation results in exceeding quota limits of Core. Maximum allowed: N, Current in use: M, Additional requested: X
rootCause: Customer subscription has exceeded its CPU core quota for the region. The required VMs for the operation exceed the allowed core count.
solution: Customer should request a CPU core quota increase via Azure Portal. After quota increase, retry the operation. For upgrades, if portal shows upgraded version but operation failed mid-way, re-run upgrade via CLI. Confirm via ASC subscription quotas view or AKSprod Kusto tables.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Quota limits are exceeded

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with QuotaExceeded/ManagedClusterCountExceedsQuotaLimit - Operation results in exceeding quota limits for managed clusters. Maximum allowed: N, Current usage: M
rootCause: AKS instated per-subscription per-region quota limits on managed cluster count (effective September 2, 2025). Customer has reached the maximum number of clusters allowed.
solution: Customer should request quota increase via Azure Portal Quotas page. Must file under support path: Azure/Subscription limits and quota/Azure Kubernetes Service (not AKS support path). Quota ICMs auto-created against AzureKubernetesService/ClusterQuotaRequests team (all Sev 3). Decline for student/free-trial/DreamSpark subscriptions; route internal requests to AZURECONTAINERSERVICE/Platform.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/QuotaExceeded QuotaExceededManagedCluster

symptom: AKS nodepool scale or upgrade fails with image not found - InvalidParameter: images referenced from the deployment template are not found (Publisher: MicrosoftWindowsServer, Offer: WindowsServer, Sku: ..., Version: ...)
rootCause: The Kubernetes version is outdated/no longer supported and the base VM images for the node pool are no longer available in the image gallery, preventing new node creation.
solution: 1) Create new nodepool with supported K8s version. 2) Migrate pods to new nodes (apply custom labels if needed). 3) Drain and cordon old nodes: kubectl drain <node>. 4) Delete old nodepool. 5) If cluster is in Failed provisioning state, upgrade cluster to current K8s version. 6) Verify cluster provisioning status.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Scale or upgrade failed with image not found

symptom: AKS node pool operations (scale, upgrade, reconcile) fail with VMExtensionHandlerNonTransientError for unsupported VM extensions (e.g., DependencyAgentLinux, AzureMonitorLinuxAgent) manually installed on VMSS
rootCause: Custom VM extensions were manually installed on the AKS node pool VMSS outside of AKS control. AKS cannot validate or maintain these extensions, causing non-retriable reconcile failures that block all CRUD operations on the affected node pool.
solution: 1) Identify unsupported VM extensions on the VMSS (check AppLens detector or Azure Portal VMSS Extensions). 2) Remove extensions directly from VMSS (Portal/CLI/ARM). 3) Perform node pool reconcile or retry failed operation. 4) If removal not possible, recreate node pool without the extension. Prevention: never manually install VM extensions on AKS VMSS.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/UnsupportedVMExtensions

symptom: AKS cluster stays in Starting state, nodes not visible after start or scale-up despite VMSS instances being created. kubectl get nodes shows missing nodes.
rootCause: The vmssCSE (Custom Script Extension) was removed/deleted from the VMSS of the node pool. Without this extension, new nodes cannot complete provisioning and join the cluster.
solution: 1) Abort any ongoing AKS operation: az aks operation-abort -n <name> -g <rg>. 2) Confirm abort succeeded (Status=Canceled): az aks operation show-latest. 3) Upgrade node pool image (works even if already on latest): az aks nodepool upgrade --node-image-only --cluster-name <name> -g <rg> --name <pool>. This reinstalls the vmssCSE extension. Verify with Kusto query checking for 'has no vmss cse extension' in AKSprod.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/vmssCSE deleted

symptom: AKS upgrade/update fails with FailedToWaitForOverlayExtensionConfigCleanup. agic-overlay-extension-config OEC resource stuck in Terminating state in kube-system namespace with DNC finalizer
rootCause: On Azure CNI Overlay clusters with AGIC/AGC, the OverlayExtensionConfig resource enters deletion but DNC-managed finalizer (finalizers.acn.azure.com/dnc-operations) cannot be removed due to DNC-RC cleanup failure (e.g. missing Cosmos DB SubnetTokenMap entry). OEC stays stuck, blocking control plane operation
solution: 1) Verify OEC stuck: kubectl get oec -n kube-system agic-overlay-extension-config -o yaml (check deletionTimestamp + finalizer present); 2) Confirm no pending DNC cleanup activity via Kusto; 3) Only if AGIC extension is NOT actively in use: remove finalizer with kubectl edit/patch to unblock deletion; 4) If AGIC is actively in use, do NOT remove finalizer - escalate to PG
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/AKS Upgrade FailedToWaitForOverlayExtensionConfigCleanup

symptom: AKS cluster enters Failed state with errors referencing Log Analytics workspace: 'Unable to get log analytics workspace info' (ResourceGroupNotFound/404), AddContainerInsightsSolutionError, or MMAExtension provisioning failure with exit code 53
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace or its resource group was deleted, or workspace key is invalid. The monitoring addon cannot reconcile, causing cluster provisioning failure
solution: Disable monitoring addon via CLI: az aks disable-addons -a monitoring. Then re-enable through Azure Portal with a valid LA workspace
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster enters Failed state with 'Reconcile standard load balancer failed' - InvalidResourceReference: frontendIPConfigurations resource was not found
rootCause: Load balancer FrontEndIpConfiguration was accidentally removed by customer, causing LB reconciliation failure
solution: Manually add back the FrontEndIpConfiguration with the same configuration ID referenced in the error message
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster enters Failed state with SubnetIsFull - subnet does not have enough capacity for N IP addresses
rootCause: The subnet available IP address space is exhausted. Cannot provision new nodes/pods
solution: Either: 1) Reduce node count to free up address space; 2) Create temporary nodepool in a different subnet, migrate workloads, then recreate original nodepool
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS operations fail with DeploymentQuotaExceeded - creating deployment would exceed quota of 800. Current deployment count is 800
rootCause: ARM deployment history limit (800 per resource group) reached in the MC_ resource group
solution: Delete old ARM deployments from the MC_ resource group to free quota. Can self-service via Azure Portal Quota blade or delete deployments via CLI
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster enters Failed state with RequestDisallowedByPolicy - resource was disallowed by Azure Policy (e.g. missing required tags)
rootCause: Azure Policy assignments on the subscription/resource group block AKS resource creation (disks, IPs, etc.) due to unmet policy conditions
solution: Guide customer to locate the blocking policy assignment (shown in error details), then either: 1) Create policy exemption for the MC_ resource group; 2) Adjust policy rule to allow AKS-managed resources
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster enters Failed state with ScopeLocked during upgrade/scale - cannot perform delete/write operation on locked resources in MC_ resource group
rootCause: Resource locks (CanNotDelete/ReadOnly) applied to nodes, disks, or other resources in the MC_ managed resource group prevent AKS from managing its infrastructure
solution: Remove or temporarily disable resource locks on the MC_ resource group and its resources, then retry the AKS operation. Locks on MC_ group are not recommended
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster update/upgrade fails with CreateOrUpdateVirtualNetworkLinkFailed - 'virtual network cannot be linked to multiple zones with overlapping namespaces' for privatelink.<region>.azmk8s.io
rootCause: Customer created a private DNS zone with the exact same name as AKS private DNS zone (*.privatelink.<region>.azmk8s.io) in a different subscription and linked it to the same VNet. AKS RP cannot find the correct zone
solution: Remove the VNet link between the AKS cluster VNet and the duplicate private DNS zone (the one in the wrong subscription). Then reconcile the cluster and retry the operation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Create or update virtual network link failure

symptom: AKS upgrade fails with CreateVMSSAgentPoolFailed - 'Cannot complete the operation since the provided disk size N is lesser than the existing disk size M' (OperationNotAllowed)
rootCause: Customer manually resized VMSS OS disks at the IaaS/VMSS level, creating discrepancy between AKS control plane expected disk size and actual VMSS disk size. Manual VMSS changes are unsupported
solution: 1) Identify affected node pools by running per-nodepool upgrade; 2) Compare AKS config vs CRP config via Jarvis/SAW; 3) Delete and recreate affected node pools with correct disk size via az CLI; 4) Re-run upgrade
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Disk size mismatch causes upgrade failures

symptom: AKS cluster update or delete fails with EtagMismatch - 'Operation is not allowed: Another operation is in progress' (PutManagedClusterAndComponents_FailedPrecondition)
rootCause: Another operation is already in progress or the cluster resource entered an inconsistent state due to previous failed operations, causing etag mismatch
solution: 1) Reconcile the cluster: az resource update --ids <AKS cluster resource ID>; 2) If fails, attempt reconcile via Geneva action (SAW required); 3) If persists, file IcM to AKS PG
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/EtagMismatch error when updating or deleting AKS cluster

symptom: After AKS upgrade, pods schedule on unintended node pools. Custom taints previously applied at the node level are missing. No errors, upgrade succeeds but scheduling behavior changes
rootCause: AKS upgrades recreate nodes. Taints applied manually at the node level (kubectl taint) are not persisted and are lost when nodes are replaced. Only nodepool-level taints survive upgrades
solution: 1) Set taints at node pool level: az aks nodepool update --node-taints workload=restricted:NoSchedule; 2) Verify pod tolerations match; 3) Add nodeSelector/nodeAffinity for deterministic placement; 4) Always configure taints at nodepool level before future upgrades
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Lost Taint After Upgrade

symptom: After AKS upgrade, pods unexpectedly schedule onto system or untainted node pools despite having tolerations configured. No errors but workloads land on wrong nodes
rootCause: Tolerations only ALLOW pods to schedule on tainted nodes but do NOT force or prefer placement. Without nodeSelector or nodeAffinity, the scheduler may place pods on any eligible untainted node
solution: Use BOTH tolerations AND nodeSelector/nodeAffinity: 1) Define matching toleration for target nodepool taint; 2) Add nodeSelector (agentpool: workloadpool) or nodeAffinity to enforce placement
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Misunderstanding tolerations behavior

symptom: AKS upgrade/update fails with FailedToWaitForOverlayExtensionConfigCleanup; agic-overlay-extension-config OEC stuck in deletion with dnc-operations finalizer
rootCause: On Azure CNI Overlay clusters with AGIC/AGC, the OEC cleanup fails because the DNC-RC reconciler cannot remove the finalizer (missing Cosmos DB SubnetTokenMap entry), leaving OEC in terminating state
solution: 1) Verify cluster uses overlay and OEC is stuck in deletion with finalizers.acn.azure.com/dnc-operations. 2) Confirm no pending DNC cleanup via Kusto. 3) kubectl edit oec and remove finalizers block. 4) Retry via cluster reconcile.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/AKS Upgrade FailedToWaitForOverlayExtensionConfigCleanup

symptom: AKS cluster operations fail with SubnetIsFull error: Subnet does not have enough capacity for N IP addresses
rootCause: The subnet assigned to the AKS node pool has exhausted its available IP address space
solution: Either reduce the node count to free up address space, or create a temporary nodepool in a temporary subnet, move workloads over, and recreate the initial nodepool
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster operations fail with RequestDisallowedByPolicy error: resource was disallowed by Azure Policy
rootCause: Customer has Azure Policy assignments (e.g. requiring specific tags) that block AKS resource operations
solution: Guide the customer to locate the blocking policy assignments, then either create a policy exemption or adjust the policy rules
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster operations fail with ScopeLocked error: resource group cannot perform operation because scope is locked
rootCause: Customer has resource locks (CanNotDelete or ReadOnly) on the AKS resource group preventing AKS operations like node deletion during upgrade
solution: Remove the corresponding resource locks on the AKS managed resource group (MC_*) and/or the AKS resource itself
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster enters Failed state due to Log Analytics workspace errors: Unable to get log analytics workspace info or AddContainerInsightsSolutionError
rootCause: The Log Analytics workspace referenced by the AKS monitoring addon was deleted, moved, or has incorrect workspace key/configuration
solution: Disable the monitoring addon via Azure CLI (az aks disable-addons --addons monitoring), then re-enable it through Azure Portal with a valid workspace
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade causes workload disruption or stalls when system node pool has only one node
rootCause: System node pools cannot configure maxUnavailable; during upgrades at least one additional system node is needed to maintain availability of kube-system pods
solution: Ensure system node pools contain at least 2 nodes (3 recommended for production) before performing upgrades
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster update/upgrade fails with CreateOrUpdateVirtualNetworkLinkFailed: A virtual network cannot be linked to multiple zones with overlapping namespaces
rootCause: Customer created a private DNS zone with the same name as the AKS-managed private DNS zone in a different subscription and linked it to the AKS cluster VNet
solution: Remove the link between the AKS cluster VNet and the duplicate private DNS zone in the wrong subscription, then reconcile the cluster
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Create or update virtual network link failure

symptom: AKS upgrade fails with CreateVMSSAgentPoolFailed: Cannot complete the operation since the provided disk size 128 is lesser than the existing disk size 256
rootCause: Customer manually resized VMSS OS disks at the IaaS level, causing a mismatch between AKS control plane expected config and actual VMSS disk size
solution: Identify affected node pools by per-nodepool upgrade. Compare AKS config vs CRP config via Jarvis. Delete and recreate affected node pools with correct OS disk size, then re-run AKS upgrade.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Disk size mismatch causes upgrade failures

symptom: Unable to update or delete AKS cluster due to EtagMismatch error: Operation is not allowed, Another operation is in progress
rootCause: A concurrent or previous operation left the cluster resource in an inconsistent state, causing etag precondition failures
solution: 1) Reconcile: az resource update --ids <AKS cluster id>. 2) Or use Geneva action. 3) If persists, file IcM to AKS PG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/EtagMismatch error when updating or deleting AKS cluster

symptom: After AKS upgrade, node taints are lost and pods schedule on unintended node pools without errors
rootCause: Taints were applied manually at the node level (kubectl taint) instead of at the node pool level. AKS upgrades recreate nodes, so manually applied taints are not persisted.
solution: Configure taints at the node pool level using az aks nodepool update --node-taints. Combine with nodeSelector or nodeAffinity for deterministic scheduling.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Lost Taint After Upgrade

symptom: Windows pod sandbox creation fails with 'The user name or password is incorrect.: unknown' error after migrating from Docker to containerd runtime
rootCause: Docker ignored runAsUserName at pod-spec level when starting pause container, but containerd does not. containerd attempts to find the specified user inside the pause container image, causing authentication failure.
solution: Move securityContext.windowsOptions.runAsUserName from pod-level spec to individual container-level spec. Do not set runAsUserName in pod.spec.securityContext; set it only in pod.spec.containers[].securityContext instead.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Docker Deprecation

symptom: Windows pod enters CrashLoopBackOff with error 'hcs::System::CreateProcess <deployment>: The system cannot find the file specified.: unknown' after migrating from Docker to containerd
rootCause: Dockerfile ENTRYPOINT uses backslash-relative path format (.\ScriptName) which Docker handled but containerd does not. containerd cannot resolve the backslash path correctly.
solution: Change ENTRYPOINT from shell form with backslash path to exec form using PowerShell: ["powershell", "-NoProfile", "-Command", ".\\script.ps1"]. See https://github.com/containerd/containerd/issues/5067
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Docker Deprecation

symptom: ContainerRuntime remains containerd even when specifying --aks-custom-headers WindowsContainerRuntime=docker when creating Windows agent pools on Kubernetes v1.24+
rootCause: Docker is no longer supported as container runtime in AKS Windows agent pools for k8s v1.24+. The flag WindowsContainerRuntime=docker is silently ignored for v1.24+ Windows node pools.
solution: Use containerd as the container runtime on AKS Windows nodes with k8s v1.24+. Docker was fully removed from AKS Windows nodes as of September 2022. Migrate workloads to be containerd-compatible.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Docker Deprecation

symptom: Client requests are not being routed to the same pod consistently across consecutive requests in an AKS Kubernetes service
rootCause: Kubernetes Service sessionAffinity is set to None by default, so requests are distributed across pods without stickiness.
solution: Set service.spec.sessionAffinity to ClientIP and optionally configure service.spec.sessionAffinityConfig.clientIP.timeoutSeconds (default 10800 = 3 hours). Example: spec.sessionAffinity: ClientIP, spec.sessionAffinityConfig.clientIP.timeoutSeconds: 900
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/How Tos/Networking/Configuring session stickiness with services

symptom: Running 'az aks upgrade' or 'az aks nodepool upgrade' does not upgrade existing Windows nodepools from Gen1 to Gen2 VM generation as expected
rootCause: By design, az aks upgrade / az aks nodepool upgrade only upgrades the node image version, not the VM Generation. Changing VM Generation requires creating a new nodepool.
solution: Create a new nodepool that supports Gen2 on the upgraded cluster: for k8s >= 1.25 specify a Gen2 VM size; for k8s < 1.25 specify --os-sku Windows2022 and a Gen2 VM size. Migrate workloads to the new nodepool and delete the old Gen1 nodepool.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Gen2 VMs

symptom: AKS Windows nodepool creation fails with: '<VM-SKU> is a Gen 2-only VM. Windows2019 does not support Gen 2 VMs. However, you can select a Gen 1 VM size or set os_sku to 2022.' (error code: ErrorCode_Windows2019NotSupportedWithGen2VM)
rootCause: Gen2 VM sizes are not supported on Windows2019 nodepools by design. Windows2019 only supports Gen1 VMs.
solution: Select a Gen1 VM size to use on Windows2019, or specify --os-sku Windows2022 to create a Windows2022 nodepool which supports Gen2 VM sizes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Gen2 VMs

symptom: AKS nodes cannot resolve API server private FQDN in a private AKS cluster with custom DNS server
rootCause: Custom DNS server cannot resolve A records under Azure private DNS zone. DNS queries not forwarded to Azure default DNS (168.63.129.16), or virtual network link between custom DNS VNet and private DNS zone is missing.
solution: For Azure-hosted custom DNS: 1) Link private DNS zone to custom DNS server VNet. 2) Forward queries to 168.63.129.16. For on-prem DNS: 1) Set up Azure VM as forwarder. 2) Link private DNS zone to VM VNet. 3) On-prem forwards to Azure VM, VM forwards to 168.63.129.16. AKS VNet auto-linked but custom DNS VNet must also be linked.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/How Tos/Networking/Setup private AKS with custom DNS server

symptom: After upgrading AKS cluster to k8s >= 1.25, Windows2019 application pods are rescheduled to Windows2022 Gen2 nodepool; node label kubernetes.azure.com/os-sku changes to Windows2022 unexpectedly
rootCause: Cluster upgrade reprovisions all nodes and refreshes all labels from scratch. Default Windows version for k8s >= 1.25 is Windows2022. Without explicit node selector for Windows2019, pods may be scheduled onto the new Windows2022 nodes.
solution: Before upgrading to k8s >= 1.25, add node affinity or selector to Windows2019 pods: Option A: Use affinity with NotIn operator for kubernetes.azure.com/os-sku: Windows2022. Option B: Recreate Windows2019 nodepools and add NodeSelector for Windows2019 os-sku. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/upgrade-windows-2019-2022
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Gen2 VMs

symptom: Customer's own windows-exporter or Windows service fails to start on AKS Windows node: 'listen tcp :19182: bind: Only one usage of each socket address (protocol/network address/port) is normally permitted.'
rootCause: AKS installs windows-exporter via AKSWindowsExtension using port 19182 (not the open-source default 9182). If a customer deployment also uses port 19182, a port conflict occurs.
solution: Configure the customer's windows-exporter to use a different port (e.g., 29182). Use Kusto query on ControlPlaneEvents kube-audit category to identify DaemonSets with hostPort 19182 conflicts. ICM ref: 530938116.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Node Extension

symptom: Pod fails with ImagePullBackOff or ErrImagePull on Windows2022 node: 'Back-off pulling image mcr.microsoft.com/windows/servercore:ltsc2019' or similar Windows2019-based container image
rootCause: Windows2022 nodes cannot host and run Windows2019 (ltsc2019) container images due to OS version incompatibility. Windows container images are strictly OS-version specific.
solution: Update the application container image from Windows2019 base (e.g., mcr.microsoft.com/windows/servercore:ltsc2019) to Windows2022 equivalent (mcr.microsoft.com/windows/servercore:ltsc2022). Follow migration guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/upgrade-windows-2019-2022
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Retirements

symptom: AKS nodepool creation fails with: 'Cannot create Windows2022 node pools with the current Kubernetes version VERSION because Windows2022 was deprecated after Kubernetes version 1.34.0. Please create Windows2025 or Windows Annual Channel node pools instead.'
rootCause: AKS stopped supporting Windows2022 node pools from Kubernetes version 1.34.0 as part of the Windows2022 retirement plan (planned retirement: March 2027).
solution: Create Windows2025 or Windows Annual Channel node pools: --os-type Windows --os-sku Windows2025 or --os-sku WindowsAnnualChannel. Migrate workloads from Windows2022 to the new nodepools.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Retirements

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade fails with: 'Cannot upgrade to the target Kubernetes version VERSION because there are Windows2022 node pools deprecated after Kubernetes version 1.34.0. Please migrate workloads to Windows2025 or Windows Annual Channel and remove Windows2022 node pools before the upgrade.' Both control-plane-only and full upgrade are blocked.
rootCause: AKS blocks cluster upgrade to k8s >= 1.34.0 when Windows2022 nodepools exist, because Windows2022 is not supported from k8s 1.34.0 onwards.
solution: 1. Create new Windows2025 or WindowsAnnualChannel nodepools. 2. Migrate workloads from Windows2022. 3. Delete all Windows2022 nodepools. 4. Retry the cluster upgrade.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Retirements

symptom: After scaling up Windows nodepool with Standard_E32s_v5 SKU, nodes enter NotReady state; kubelet crashes with configMapVolumeMounter.SetUpAt() panic (invalid memory access 0xffffffffffffffff); pods mounting specific ConfigMap stuck in Terminating; CNS becomes unresponsive
rootCause: ConfigMap containing Windows-specific UNC file paths and PowerShell scripts triggers invalid memory access during volume mounting on Standard_E32s_v5. Likely caused by memory architecture differences in v5 series vs v4, creating incompatible access pattern when processing ConfigMap content.
solution: Modify the ConfigMap to remove or encode UNC file paths and embedded PowerShell scripts. Workaround: switch to Standard_E32s_v4 VMs. Escalate to AKS engineering (kubelet team) for a permanent fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/kubelet crash different memory arch

symptom: AKS PUT operations (upgrade/update) fail with OverlaymgrReconcileError / OverlayMgrAPIRequestFailed caused by Kyverno webhook failures. Error: failed calling webhook validate.kyverno.svc-fail: no endpoints available for service kyverno-svc
rootCause: Kyverno validatingwebhookconfiguration is configured with failurePolicy=Fail, but the Kyverno service pods are down or have no endpoints. This blocks API server requests including overlay manager reconciliation, causing OverlaymgrReconcileError with context deadline exceeded.
solution: 1. Remove readiness/liveness probes from kube-apiserver container. 2. Access overlay cluster and delete the kyverno-resource-validating-webhook-cfg ValidatingWebhookConfiguration. 3. Verify APIService v1beta3.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io is created. 4. Use Geneva action to reconcile the cluster. 5. Wait for new API Servers to come up healthy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/OverlaymgrReconcileError caused by kyverno webhook failures

symptom: AKS PUT operations fail with OverlaymgrReconcileError / OverlayMgrAPIRequestFailed / context deadline exceeded. OverlayManager pod CPU usage abnormally high (~60%). Another AKS cluster on same underlay generating excessive operations (noisy neighbor).
rootCause: A noisy neighbor AKS cluster on the same underlay generates excessive API operations (often from misconfigured DiagnosticsSettings with thousands of LinkedNotificationHandler.POST per hour), overloading shared OverlayManager pods and causing CPU saturation that prevents overlay reconciliation.
solution: 1. Identify noisy neighbor CCP via OverlaymgrApiQos Kusto query filtering by RPTenant and ReconcileOverlayError. 2. Look up offending CCP in ASI. 3. AKS PG contacts offending customer. 4. Alternative: migrate cluster to different underlay (requires customer approval + IcM to AKS PG). 5. Reconcile affected cluster after mitigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/OverlaymgrReconcileError caused by noisy neighbouring

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade or nodepool scaling fails with PodDrainFailure: Drain did not complete pods within 10m0s on vm. Check Pod Disruption Budget. Upgrades are slow and eventually fail.
rootCause: Pod Disruption Budgets (PDBs) with AllowedDisruptions=0 prevent pods from being evicted during node drain. Occurs when PDB minAvailable threshold cannot be satisfied given current replica count and cluster topology.
solution: Option 1: Delete blocking PDB temporarily (backup with kubectl get pdb -o yaml, delete, upgrade, restore). Option 2: Scale up deployment/statefulset replicas so PDB allows disruptions, upgrade, then scale back. After mitigation, restart upgrade if not in-progress.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Pod Disruption Budgets cause nodepool operations to fail

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade, scaling, nodepool create/delete fails with ScopeLocked error: The scope cannot perform delete operation because following scope(s) are locked. Error references MC_ resource group.
rootCause: A resource lock (CanNotDelete or ReadOnly) is present on the MC_ managed resource group or individual resources within it. AKS lifecycle operations require modifying resources in MC_ RG, and the lock blocks these operations.
solution: Remove the resource lock from MC_ resource group or the specific locked resource via Azure Portal, CLI (az lock delete), or PowerShell. After removing the lock, re-run the failed operation. Advise customer not to place locks on MC_ managed resource group.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Resource locks block cluster operations

symptom: AKS upgrade or scale fails with Unable to get log analytics workspace info. Resource ID: .../workspaces/... Detail: ResourceGroupNotFound (HTTP 404). Log Analytics Workspace or its RG no longer exists.
rootCause: Customer deleted the Log Analytics Workspace or its containing Resource Group without first disabling the monitoring (omsagent) addon on the AKS cluster. The AKS control plane still references the workspace and fails when it cannot find it.
solution: Mitigation 1 (within 14 days): Recover soft-deleted workspace using PowerShell New-AzOperationalInsightsWorkspace with same name/RG/region. Mitigation 2 (after 14 days): Disable monitoring addon (az aks disable-addons -a monitoring). Mitigation 3: If disable-addons fails, recreate the missing workspace from Portal with same resource ID. Then retry the operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Upgrade or Scale failed due to Log Analytics Workspace not found

symptom: Azure Files PVC mount fails with MountVolume.MountDevice failed and mount error(13): Permission denied after storage account access key rotation. Pods enter CreateContainerError/FailedMount state.
rootCause: Storage account access key was rotated but the Kubernetes secret (azure-storage-account-{accountname}-secret) used by CSI driver still contains the old key, causing CIFS mount authentication failure.
solution: Delete the old Kubernetes secret and recreate it with the new storage access key: kubectl delete secret <secret-name> -n <ns> && kubectl create secret generic azure-storage-account-{accountname}-secret --from-literal=azurestorageaccountname=xxx --from-literal=azurestorageaccountkey=xxx --type=Opaque -n <ns>. Then restart the deployments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/storage accesskey changed

symptom: PV/PVC creation from cross-subscription storage account fails with AuthorizationFailed 403: client does not have authorization to perform action on storage account in different subscription.
rootCause: AKS control plane identity (system-assigned managed identity) lacks permissions on the storage account located in a different subscription under the same tenant.
solution: Grant Storage Account Contributor role to the AKS control plane identity (same name as cluster) on the cross-subscription storage account via Azure IAM. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/use-managed-identity
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/storage account cross subscription aks

symptom: PVC expand/resize fails with OperationNotAllowed: Change in disk property of VM of size Standard_D4_v3 is not supported when using classic VM SKUs. VolumeResizeFailed warning from external-resizer.
rootCause: Classic VM types (e.g. Standard_D4_v3) do not support online disk resize. The CSI driver online resize request is rejected by the compute API.
solution: 1) Cordon all classic VM nodes. 2) Delete the application pods to put them in pending state. 3) Disk resize will happen automatically within minutes (offline resize). 4) Run kubectl describe pvc to confirm resize complete (look for Require file system resize of volume on node message).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/pvc expand issue with classic vm

symptom: Volume mount fails with applyFSGroup failed for vol /subscriptions/.../disks/<disk-name>: input/output error when securityContext.fsGroup is configured and volume has many existing files.
rootCause: Kubernetes recursively updates ownership (chown/chmod) of all files under the mount point when fsGroup is set. With a large number of files, this operation times out and results in input/output error.
solution: Set fsGroupChangePolicy to OnRootMismatch in the pod securityContext. This reduces ownership changes by only performing them when the root directory group ownership differs from fsGroup. Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#configure-volume-permission-and-ownership-change-policy-for-pods
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/applyFSGroup failed for vol input output error

symptom: After AKS upgrade to 1.28.x, Blob CSI volume mount fails with failed to initialize new pipeline [Azure CLI not found on path]. Pods using blobCSI driver with MSI authType cannot mount storage.
rootCause: BlobCSI driver with azureStorageAuthType=MSI and azureStorageIdentityObjectID requires Azure CLI on the node for authentication. After AKS 1.28.x upgrade/node image update, Azure CLI may not be available on the node path.
solution: Option 1 (recommended): Change PV configuration from azureStorageIdentityObjectID to azureStorageIdentityResourceID (MSI resource ID), which bypasses Azure CLI dependency. Works for dynamic PVCs. Option 2: Install Azure CLI on all nodes via DaemonSet. Option 3: Use SAS token or storage account key instead of MSI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/Volume Issues AKS 1 28 x

symptom: Volume mount times out with Unable to attach or mount volumes: timed out waiting for the condition. CSI driver logs show LinkedAuthorizationFailed 403: kubelet identity lacks read permission on DiskEncryptionSet.
rootCause: The kubelet managed identity used by the AKS cluster does not have read permission on the DiskEncryptionSet resource specified in the PersistentVolume, causing disk attach to fail with LinkedAuthorizationFailed.
solution: Grant the kubelet identity Reader role on the DiskEncryptionSet resource via Azure IAM. Check CSI Driver control plane logs in ASI under Storage and Disks > CSI Driver - Control Plane Events to identify the exact missing permission.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/Unable to attach or mount volumes, timed out while waiting - DiskEncryptionSet permission issue

symptom: Azure Machine Learning endpoint deployment on AKS fails with pods stuck in init state. MountVolume.MountDevice fails with context deadline exceeded for AML-created PV/PVC.
rootCause: Creating PV/PVCs within pods using AML services on AKS is not a supported scenario. AML default blobfuse mount path configuration leads to PVC mount failures.
solution: 1) Create ConfigMap azuremlaksconfig in default namespace with disableHostPathMount: true annotation. 2) Redeploy the AML model with blobfuse_enabled=False to prevent PV/PVC creation. 3) Verify pods transition to running state after redeployment.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/Support for PV and PVC on AML workloads

symptom: Disk attach fails with Only Disk CachingType None is supported for disk with size greater than 4095 GB (BadRequest 400). FailedAttachVolume warning from attachdetach-controller for PVC-backed disks >= 4 TiB.
rootCause: Azure Managed Disks of 4 TiB and larger only support cachingmode=None. If the StorageClass or PV spec has cachingmode set to ReadOnly or ReadWrite, the disk attach operation is rejected by the compute API.
solution: Set cachingmode: none in the StorageClass parameters or PV spec for disks >= 4 TiB. For existing PVs: edit the PV to change cachingmode to None, delete and recreate the pod. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/premium-storage-performance#disk-caching
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/DiskCachingInvalidForDiskSize - Only Disk CachingType 'None' is supported for disk with size greater than 4095 GB

symptom: Disk attach fails with AttachDiskWhileBeingDetached: Cannot attach data disk to VM because the disk is currently being detached or the last detach operation failed. FailedAttachVolume from attachdetach-controller.
rootCause: Concurrent attach/detach conflict: a new attach request is issued while the previous detach operation on the same disk is still in progress or has failed, leaving the disk in a transitional state.
solution: 1) Confirm disk is not attached by viewing VMSS instances in Azure portal. 2) Run az vmss update-instances -g <MC_RG> --name <VMSS_NAME> --instance-id <ID> (for VMSS) or az vm update -n <vm-name> -g <MC_RG> (for availability set) to reconcile the VM state and resolve the attach/detach conflict.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/PVC failure with error "AttachDiskWhileBeingDetached"

symptom: AKS PUT operations fail with OverlaymgrReconcileError (SubCode: OverlayMgrAPIRequestFailed, context deadline exceeded). Kyverno validating webhook 'validate.kyverno.svc-fail' returns 'no endpoints available for service kyverno-svc'. APIServer poststarthook priority-and-fairness-config-producer fails.
rootCause: Kyverno validating webhook configuration (kyverno-resource-validating-webhook-cfg) blocks kube-apiserver readiness. The webhook calls kyverno-svc which has no running endpoints, causing a deadlock that prevents overlaymgr from communicating with the CCP API server.
solution: 1) Remove readiness/liveness probes from kube-apiserver container. 2) Delete the 'kyverno-resource-validating-webhook-cfg' ValidatingWebhookConfiguration from the overlay cluster. 3) Verify APIService v1beta3.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io is created. 4) Use Geneva action to reconcile the cluster. 5) Wait for new API servers to come up healthy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/OverlaymgrReconcileError caused by kyverno webhook failures

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade or nodepool operations fail with PodDrainFailure error: 'ScaleVMSSAgentPoolFailed - Drain did not complete pods within 10m0s. Check Pod Disruption Budget.' Slow upgrades that eventually fail.
rootCause: Pod Disruption Budgets (PDBs) with AllowedDisruptions=0 prevent pods from draining off nodes pending upgrade. When replica count <= PDB minAvailable, draining a node would violate the PDB constraint.
solution: Option 1: Back up (kubectl get pdb -o yaml) and delete the blocking PDB, re-run upgrade, restore PDB after. Option 2 (production): Scale up deployment/statefulset replica count (kubectl scale deploy --replicas N) to allow disruptions, re-run upgrade, scale back.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Pod Disruption Budgets cause nodepool operations to fail

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade, scaling, or nodepool operations fail with ScopeLocked error: 'The scope cannot perform delete operation because following scope(s) are locked'. Affects CRUD on resources in MC_ resource group.
rootCause: A resource lock (CanNotDelete or ReadOnly) exists on the MC_ resource group or individual resources, preventing AKS from modifying or deleting resources during lifecycle operations.
solution: Remove the resource lock from the MC_ resource group or affected resources via Azure Portal/CLI/PowerShell. After the lock is removed, re-run the failed operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Resource locks block cluster operations

symptom: AKS update/upgrade fails: 'Unable to get log analytics workspace info. StatusCode=404 Code=ResourceGroupNotFound'. Occurs during cluster PUT operations when monitoring addon references deleted workspace.
rootCause: Customer deleted the Log Analytics Workspace or its Resource Group without first disabling the monitoring (omsagent) addon on the AKS cluster.
solution: Mitigation 1: If deleted <14 days ago, recover soft-deleted workspace (New-AzOperationalInsightsWorkspace). Mitigation 2: If >14 days, disable monitoring addon: 'az aks disable-addons -a monitoring'. Mitigation 3: If disable-addons fails too, recreate the Log Analytics Workspace from Portal, then retry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Upgrade or Scale failed due to Log Analytics Workspace not found

symptom: Azure Backup extension install on AKS hangs until timeout. Extension agent logs: 'Failed to copy chart content for mcr.microsoft.com/azurearck8s/extension-catalog - dial tcp: lookup mcr.microsoft.com: i/o timeout'. EgressBlocked warnings on nodes.
rootCause: AKS cluster cannot reach MCR endpoints due to restrictive NSGs, DNS issues, or NVA/firewall rules. Extension agent cannot download Helm chart from MCR.
solution: Allow outbound access to: mcr.microsoft.com:443, *.data.mcr.microsoft.com:443, mcr-0001.mcr-msedge.net:443. Verify with 'curl -I https://mcr.microsoft.com/v2/_catalog' from debug pod. Check ASI Kube Events for EgressBlocked. Also ensure NSG inbound allows azurebackup and azurecloud service tags.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/AKS azure backup troubleshooting

symptom: Azure Backup extension appears installed (extension-agent/operator pods running in kube-system), ASI shows succeeded, but no 'dataprotection-microsoft' namespace or pods created. Backup/restore operations cannot proceed.
rootCause: 1) Azure Policy in enforced mode blocks pods with admin role/rolebinding in dataprotection-microsoft namespace. 2) Insufficient node resources (needs 128/1280Mi CPU, 500/1000m memory) to schedule dataprotection pods.
solution: 1) Add 'dataprotection-microsoft' to Azure Policy exclusion list. 2) Scale out or scale up worker nodes. Verify: kubectl get pods -n dataprotection-microsoft.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/AKS azure backup troubleshooting

symptom: 'az dataprotection backup-instance update-msi-permissions' fails: NoRegisteredProviderFound for API version '2023-02-02-preview' for type 'managedClusters'.
rootCause: The dataprotection CLI extension bundles a vendored AKS SDK with an outdated preview API version not registered in the target region.
solution: Create IcM with debug output (--debug flag). After PG repackages the vendored SDK, customer runs 'az extension update --name dataprotection'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/AKS azure backup troubleshooting

symptom: AKS API calls fail after preview API version deprecated; Terraform/az cli/SDK using retired preview API versions (e.g. 2022-07-02-preview through 2023-08-02-preview)
rootCause: AKS deprecates preview API versions on a rolling schedule. Old Terraform/SDK versions continue calling retired preview APIs removed from RP manifest.
solution: Update tools to latest GA API version: az cli 'az upgrade' + 'az extension update --name aks-preview'; Terraform update AzureRM; SDK update to latest. Check activity logs with az monitor activity-log list --offset 30d to identify deprecated API callers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/API Deprecation

symptom: Node drain times out leaving node cordoned with NoSchedule taint; node stuck in Ready,SchedulingDisabled or NotReady/Unreachable; AKS reconciler cannot return node to ready
rootCause: Pod eviction during drain timed out. Node controller cordon adds NoSchedule taint at drain start, only removed on uncordon. Timeout leaves taint permanently.
solution: kubectl drain <node> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data then kubectl uncordon <node>. For automation: nodify, draino, drainsafe tools. AKS platform built-in safe drain with node-problem-detector also available.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Automatic Safe Drain Alternatives (Ready,SchedulingDisabled   NodeNotReady  Unreachable)

symptom: CCP scheduler spreads pods aggressively across zones; unexpected scheduling behavior differs from upstream default; pods not placed on same-zone nodes with available resources
rootCause: Since March 2025, AKS CCP scheduler uses maxSkew=1 (down from 5) for topology.kubernetes.io/zone, aggressively spreading multi-replica deployments across zones.
solution: Specify TopologySpreadConstraints in deployment spec with desired maxSkew (e.g. maxSkew: 5) to override AKS CCP default. Verify via KubeScheduler logs for --config= and maxSkew entries.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/CCP Scheduler

symptom: AKS cluster Failed with CannotSwitchBetweenAutoAndManualApproval; CreateOrUpdatePrivateEndpointFailed for kube-apiserver PE; PE in pending state; all node pools failed
rootCause: Customer manually deleted auto-created kube-apiserver PE and recreated it manually, causing auto/manual approval mode conflict. Manual PE stays pending and blocks reconciliation.
solution: Delete the pending-state PE, then reconcile cluster (az aks update). kube-apiserver PE will be auto-recreated. Never manually modify/delete kube-apiserver PE.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/CannotSwitchBetweenAutoAndManualApproval

symptom: AKS with CAS shows wrong node count; fewer nodes registered than CAS target (e.g. MIN=60 but only 51); CAS logs show negative delta=-9
rootCause: VMSS instances failed bootstrap with CSE exit code (e.g. 50) and never registered to k8s API server. CAS still counts unregistered VMSS instances causing persistent mismatch.
solution: Identify failed VMSS instances. Reimage (Use Temp Disk for Ephemeral OS) or delete them, then reconcile cluster. See vmssCSE failures TSG for exit codes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Cluster Autoscaler/AKS_not_showing_correct_node_count_when_CAS_is_enabled

symptom: CAS not scaling down to MIN count; nodes not suitable for removal can reschedule 0 out of N pods; nodepool scales back up after brief scale-down
rootCause: calico-typha deployment uses hostPort binding each replica 1:1 to a node. Tigera operator auto-scales typha replicas to match node count (1 per node for 1-3 nodes). hostPort prevents pod relocation.
solution: Set CAS MIN COUNT >= 3 (matching calico-typha replica needs) or use manual scaling for fewer nodes. Expected behavior with hostPort deployments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Cluster Autoscaler/Cluster Autoscaler is not scaling down when using Calico typha Operator

symptom: AKS cluster autoscaler creates new VMSS instances but they get deleted within seconds in a loop. New node added, then deleted, cycle repeats. Pod pending due to resource unavailability.
rootCause: VMSS autoscale is enabled alongside AKS cluster autoscaler. VMSS autoscale has a max instance count lower than what cluster autoscaler is trying to scale to. VMSS deletes nodes exceeding its configured maximum, then cluster autoscaler creates new ones again.
solution: Disable VMSS autoscale: use 'az monitor autoscale delete --name <autoscale-name> --resource-group <MC_RG>' or disable via Portal (VMSS > Scaling > Disable autoscale). After disabling, cluster autoscaler will work as expected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Cluster Autoscaler/New VMSS instances keep creating and deleting

symptom: AKS cluster has duplicate control planes (multiple CCPs). Symptoms include: duplicate control plane logs, inaccurate Container Insights metrics, cluster autoscaler may not function.
rootCause: Rapidly deleting and creating an AKS cluster with the same name in the same resource group and subscription causes race condition. Backend resources (CCP, Network) are not fully cleaned up before the new cluster is created, resulting in two control plane instances.
solution: Escalate to AKS PG via IcM mentioning duplicate control planes for the cluster. No customer or CSS action can resolve this. PG will delete the duplicate CCP. Advise customer to add delay between DELETE and CREATE operations. Verify via AppLens detector or ASI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Duplicate cluster control planes

symptom: AKS API returns BadRequest: If-None-Match header is not empty or * when customer sends ETag request with invalid If-None-Match header value.
rootCause: Client sends If-None-Match header with a value other than * or empty. AKS pre-validation rejects the request. Only * or empty is accepted for If-None-Match.
solution: Client error. Customer should only pass * or leave If-None-Match header empty. Use FrontEndContextActivity Kusto query to verify.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Entity Tag (ETag) Support

symptom: AKS API returns BadRequest: If-Match header is not empty and If-None-Match header is * - both ETag headers set simultaneously.
rootCause: Client sends both If-Match and If-None-Match headers in the same request. AKS pre-validation rejects this as a conflict.
solution: Client error. Customer must use only one of If-Match or If-None-Match per request, not both.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Entity Tag (ETag) Support

symptom: AKS API returns PreConditionFailed: If-None-Match precondition failed. The given resource already exists.
rootCause: If-None-Match: * means only succeed if resource does not exist. Since the resource already exists, the precondition fails. This is by design for concurrency protection.
solution: Client error. Customer needs to use GET to check if resource exists first, or use If-Match with the current ETag value for update operations instead.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Entity Tag (ETag) Support

symptom: AKS API returns PreConditionFailed: The value of If-Match header does not match the latest ETag value of the resource. Stale ETag.
rootCause: The resource was updated by another request between when the customer read the ETag and when they submitted their update. The If-Match ETag value is now stale.
solution: Client error. Customer must do a fresh GET to obtain the latest ETag, then retry the update with the new ETag value. Check FrontEndContextActivity Kusto query with pre-validating ETag headers message.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Entity Tag (ETag) Support

symptom: After migrating AKS KMS from v1 to v2, kubectl get secrets returns unable to transform key: no matching prefix found; reinitializing error. Secrets cannot be listed.
rootCause: Customer did not complete storage migration after KMS v1 to v2 migration. Secrets stored in etcd still have v1 prefix (k8s:enc:kms:v1:azurekmsprovider0) but API server expects v2 prefix. For clusters 1.27+, KMS must be disabled before upgrade and re-enabled after.
solution: Escalate to AKS PG via IcM. PG will: 1) access underlay via hcpdebug, 2) list secrets in etcd to find v1-prefixed entries, 3) edit encryption-provider-config configmap to change apiVersion from v2 back to v1, 4) restart kube-apiserver, 5) re-encrypt affected secrets, 6) verify recovery. CSS cannot resolve without PG engagement.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/KMS list secrets error after migration
Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/Managed Istio/Port collision with Redis with Geo Replication enabled
symptom: Applications connected to Redis DB via Azure Cache for Redis with Geo Replication are suddenly disconnected. Traffic on port 15001 is rejected. Error: RedisConnectionFailureException Unable to connect to host:15001. Occurs on any AKS cluster with Istio service mesh enabled.
rootCause: Port collision between Istio Envoy sidecar (default outbound port 15001) and Redis Geo Replication (uses ports 15000-15999). Envoy intercepts traffic destined for port 15001, preventing Redis connectivity.
solution: Change Istio proxyListenPort via MeshConfig. Create a revision-specific shared ConfigMap (e.g. istio-shared-configmap-asm-1-24) in aks-istio-system namespace with defaultConfig.proxyListenPort set to a non-conflicting port (e.g. 15002). Do NOT edit the default ConfigMap directly as it gets reconciled.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/Outbound Connectivity/Azure Firewall connectivity issue when using FQDN in network rules
symptom: Outbound traffic from AKS cluster intermittently times out. Cluster uses UDR routing through Azure Firewall with FQDN-based network rules. DNS for AKS VNet is NOT set to Azure Firewall IP. Destination FQDN resolves to multiple IP addresses.
rootCause: DNS caching mismatch between CoreDNS in cluster and Azure Firewall. Azure Firewall caches DNS for 15 minutes. When CoreDNS resolves FQDN to IP-Y but Firewall cache still has IP-X, Firewall forwards traffic to IP-X. Response from IP-X is rejected by conntrack (expected IP-Y), causing connection timeout.
solution: 1) Switch to application rules instead of network rules when using FQDN. 2) OR enable Azure Firewall DNS proxy and set Azure Firewall IP as DNS server on AKS VNet, then reboot nodes.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/Outbound Connectivity/HTTP 470 responses from Azure Firewall
symptom: Customer applications in AKS receive HTTP 470 error when accessing another service in the same cluster. Exceptions observed when destination pods are all in non-ready state. Cluster uses route table with custom entries redirecting traffic to Azure Firewall.
rootCause: Pods in CrashLoopBackOff are removed from kube-proxy routing, changing iptables from ALLOW to REJECT. Traffic falls through to default route. Typoed service CIDR in route table causes intra-cluster traffic to route to Azure Firewall instead of nodes. Azure Firewall returns HTTP 470 when no explicit allow/deny rule matches.
solution: Fix the route table entries to correctly include the service CIDR, preventing intra-cluster traffic from being incorrectly routed to Azure Firewall.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/Outbound Connectivity/Kubenet UDR (User Defined Route) asymmetric routing from firewall missing routes configuration
symptom: External client cannot reach AKS service via Load Balancer public IP. Error: upstream connect error or disconnect/reset before headers, connection failure 503. Cluster uses Kubenet with UDR outbound type routing through Azure Firewall.
rootCause: Asymmetric routing: UDR missing routes for firewall public egress IP, and DNAT rule not configured on firewall. Inbound traffic arrives via LB public IP but return traffic goes through firewall which drops it.
solution: 1) Add route with Address Prefix of public egress IP (firewall public IP) and Next Hop Type Internet to the UDR. 2) Create DNAT rule on Azure Firewall. Both required. Cluster must have --outbound-type userDefinedRouting.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/Outbound Connectivity/Load Balancer Source Ranges service conf blocking in traffic
symptom: After upgrading AKS from 1.22 to 1.25+, intra-cluster traffic via external LB IP is blocked. Internal LB IP works. End customers via external LB IP work. Only cluster-to-cluster via external LB IP fails.
rootCause: K8s 1.25 fix (PR #109826) corrected syncProxyRules LB loop nesting. LoadBalancerSourceRanges enforcement now works correctly, and cluster subnet was not in allowed ranges.
solution: Add AKS cluster subnet IP range to the service loadBalancerSourceRanges configuration.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/Outbound Connectivity/Troubleshoot Static Egress Gateway Feature
symptom: kube-egress-gateway-daemon-manager pod CrashLoopBackOff. Logs: unable to retrieve node metadata, failed to get loadbalancer metadata, IMDS 404 Not Found. Cluster uses UDR or NatGateway outbound type.
rootCause: Gateway daemon calls IMDS for LB config. With UDR/NatGateway, no LB exists by default. LB kubeegressgateway-ilb is only created after StaticGatewayConfiguration (SGC) is created.
solution: Create the SGC resource first. The kubeegressgateway-ilb will then be provisioned, resolving the IMDS 404.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/Outbound Connectivity/Troubleshoot Static Egress Gateway Feature
symptom: Gateway nodepool cannot join kubeegressgateway LB backend pool. Controller logs: RequestDisallowedByPolicy, Not allow Public IP or Public IP Prefix.
rootCause: Azure Policy blocks public IP/prefix creation. Static Egress Gateway tries to create managed public IP prefix which is denied.
solution: Set provisionPublicIps: false in StaticGatewayConfiguration to skip public IP creation.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/Outbound Connectivity/Troubleshoot Static Egress Gateway Feature
symptom: Gateway nodepool cannot join kubeegressgateway LB backend pool. Controller logs: SubnetIsFull, does not have enough capacity for IP addresses.
rootCause: Gateway nodepool subnet too small. Requires minimum 9 IPs (2 primary + 2 secondary + 5 reserved), minimum subnet /28.
solution: Recreate egress nodepool in larger subnet (minimum /28). SGC and egress LB must also be recreated.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/Outbound Connectivity/Unable to connect to private ACR via proxy
symptom: Image pull failures (403 Forbidden / 401 Unauthorized) from private ACR. AKS has HTTP proxy configured. ACR has private endpoints. Kubelet: failed to pull image 403 Forbidden or failed to authorize 401 Unauthorized.
rootCause: Private ACR has two private endpoints (registry + data) that must be reachable directly. HTTP proxy routes traffic to ACR endpoints through proxy which lacks connectivity to private endpoints.
solution: Option 1: Configure proxy to allow traffic to both ACR endpoints. Option 2: Add both endpoints to AKS noProxy config, then update (not reimage) node images.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Others/AKS Automatic Clusters HostPath Limitation Troubleshooting Guide
symptom: Pod deployment in AKS Automatic cluster fails with error: 'HostPath volumes are forbidden under restricted security policy unless containers mounting them are from allowed images' from ValidatingAdmissionPolicy 'aks-managed-baseline-hostpath-volumes'.
rootCause: AKS Automatic clusters enforce immutable Baseline Pod Security Standards via ValidatingAdmissionPolicy that forbids HostPath volumes. These safeguards cannot be overridden - 'az aks safeguards update --excluded-ns' fails with 'RequestNotAllowedBecauseAssociatedClusterIsAutomaticCluster'.
solution: 1) Use AKS Standard SKU if HostPath volumes are required. 2) Use supported volume types (emptyDir, configMap) instead of HostPath. 3) Use Azure Monitor Container Insights or Azure-native logging solutions for log collection.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Others/Backup failing due to orphaned aad nmi pod
symptom: Backup job fails on AKS cluster due to presence of residual nmi pod from AAD Pod Identity addon that was previously removed.
rootCause: After removing AAD Pod Identity addon, the nmi DaemonSet/pod remains as an orphan on the cluster, interfering with backup operations.
solution: Run 'az aks update -g <resource-group> -n <cluster-name> --disable-pod-identity' to clean up the orphaned nmi DaemonSet. This command works even if Pod Identity addon is not currently installed. Prerequisite: install/update aks-preview extension.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Others/Cgroups V2
symptom: Application crashes or fails after AKS cluster upgrade to Kubernetes 1.25+ due to Cgroups V2 incompatibility. Affected frameworks include .NET Core 3.1, JDK versions below 11.0.16 or below JDK 15.
rootCause: AKS Kubernetes 1.25+ defaults to Cgroups V2 which is not supported by older application frameworks (.NET Core 3.1, JDK <11.0.16/<15). Applications relying on Cgroups V1 behavior break under V2.
solution: 1) Upgrade framework: JDK to 11.0.16+/15+, .NET to 5+. 2) For AKS <1.28: apply CGroups V1 revert daemonset 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/AKS/master/examples/cgroups/revert-cgroup-v1.yaml' (apply per nodepool, verify with 'stat -fc %T /sys/fs/cgroup/' - 'tmpfs' means V1). 3) For AKS 1.28+: daemonset workaround no longer supported; use AzureLinux OS which supports Cgroups V1.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Others/Error loading seccomp filter bpfjitlimit
symptom: Pods stuck in pending state with kubelet error: 'unable to init seccomp: error loading seccomp filter into kernel: error loading seccomp filter: errno 524: unknown'. Often occurs after node image upgrade.
rootCause: Linux kernel BPF JIT memory limit (net.core.bpf_jit_limit) is exhausted. Each container's seccomp filter consumes BPF JIT memory. When many containers run on a node, the default limit is insufficient, causing new containers to fail seccomp filter loading.
solution: 1) Increase BPF JIT limit: 'sysctl net.core.bpf_jit_limit=452534528' on affected nodes. 2) For persistent fix, apply via DaemonSet or node configuration to set sysctl at boot. 3) Upgrade to newer node image with kernel fix if available.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Others/ImagePullBackOff 401 Unauthorized
symptom: Pod stuck in ImagePullBackOff with '401 Unauthorized' when pulling images from ACR, despite Kubelet identity having AcrPull role. No unusual entries in RegistryActivity logs.
rootCause: Kubelet identity on AKS cluster was changed but the new identity was not properly assigned to the VMSS. IMDS returns 'Identity not found' (400 Bad Request) when kubelet tries to acquire token, causing ACR authentication failure.
solution: Ensure the Kubelet identity assigned to AKS cluster matches the identity on the underlying VMSS. Reconcile with 'az aks update' or manually assign the correct managed identity to the VMSS. Verify by checking IMDS response for the kubelet identity client ID.

symptom: AKS virtual node fails with 'error creating provider: error setting up network: unable to delegate subnet to Azure Container Instance since it references the route table' - aci-connector-linux pod fatal error
rootCause: ACI does not support user-defined routing (UDR). The aci-connector has a validation check that blocks subnet delegation to ACI when a route table is associated with the virtual node subnet.
solution: Remove the user-defined routing (route table association) from the subnet used for ACI virtual node. UDR is not supported on the ACI subnet for virtual nodes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/error setting up network - AKS virtual node

symptom: When trying to enable AppArmor profile on AKS node using kubectl debug, user gets error: apparmor_parser: Unable to add k8s-apparmor-example-deny-write. Permission denied; attempted to load a profile while confined?
rootCause: The kubectl debug command does not provide sufficient privilege escalation to enable AppArmor profiles on AKS nodes.
solution: Use node-shell or establish a direct SSH connection to the AKS node instead of kubectl debug. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/manage-ssh-node-access
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/AKS cluster security AppArmor Linux kernel security module

symptom: AGIC pod logs show 'ErrorGetApplicationGatewayError' with 'Failed fetching configuration for Application Gateway' followed by 'dial timeout' and 'Retrying in 10s' - AGIC cannot configure Application Gateway
rootCause: Network connectivity from AGIC pod to Azure Resource Manager endpoint (management.azure.com:443) is blocked. Common causes: UDR routing through NVA/Azure Firewall without allowing ARM traffic, NSG denying AzureResourceManager service tag, or Network Policies blocking AGIC pod outbound.
solution: Check and fix egress rules: (1) If UDR outbound, verify NVA/Firewall allows traffic to management.azure.com; (2) If LB outbound with route table 0.0.0.0/0 to NVA, check NVA rules; (3) Verify NSG allows AzureResourceManager service tag; (4) Check Network Policies do not block AGIC pod outbound on port 443.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AKS cluster operations fail with error RequestDisallowedByPolicy. Cluster enters failed provisioning state due to Azure Policy blocking resource creation.
rootCause: Azure Policy assignments (e.g. Require specified tag) block AKS RP or cluster resource operations. AKS-managed resources do not satisfy the enforced rules.
solution: 1) Identify blocking policy from error message. 2) Update resources to comply OR add policy exemptions. 3) Reconcile cluster: az aks update -g <rg> -n <cluster> with no additional params.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/Azure Policy blocks cluster operations

symptom: AGIC pod logs show 'AuthorizationFailed' error when trying to fetch or apply configuration to Application Gateway
rootCause: Missing required RBAC role assignments for AGIC identity. AGIC needs: Contributor on Application Gateway scope, Reader on Application Gateway Resource Group.
solution: Create the missing role assignments: assign AGIC managed identity as Contributor on the Application Gateway resource and Reader on the Application Gateway resource group. For AGIC addon clusters, the identity name is 'ingressapplicationgateway-<CLUSTER_NAME>'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: Microsoft Defender for Cloud reports AKS clusters as noncompliant for policy: Kubernetes clusters should disable automounting API credentials.
rootCause: Default Kubernetes pods automount API credentials. The Defender policy (enabled by default) flags pods not in excluded namespaces that have automounting enabled.
solution: Options: 1) Disable or exclude the policy. 2) Modify policy to exclude namespaces. 3) Add automountServiceAccountToken: false to pod YAML.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/Defender reports automount credentials

symptom: AGIC pod logs show 'invalid_client' error - Application Gateway configuration updates fail due to authentication issue
rootCause: The Service Principal secret used by AGIC to authenticate with Azure has expired.
solution: Generate a new secret for the Service Principal and update AGIC to use the new secret following: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/application-gateway/ingress-controller-install-existing#using-a-service-principal
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues
