symptom: Enabling/disabling FIPS on existing AKS node pool with Mariner OS fails: Creating FIPS enabled agent nodes is not allowed for Mariner agent nodes.
rootCause: Mariner OS does not support updating existing node pools to enable or disable FIPS.
solution: Use --os-sku AzureLinux with --enable-fips-image to create a new FIPS-enabled Azure Linux node pool and migrate workloads.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/FIPS nodes

symptom: AGIC pod logs show 'Ignore mutating App Gateway as it is not mutable' and 'Application Gateway is in stopped state' - AGIC cannot apply configuration changes
rootCause: Application Gateway is in Stopped operational state (customer may have stopped it via CLI to reduce costs). AGIC requires AppGW to be in Running state.
solution: Start the Application Gateway: `az network application-gateway start -g <RG> -n <NAME>`. Verify AGIC pod logs show successful deployment after AppGW returns to Running state.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: Creating FIPS-enabled AKS node pool with ARM64 SKU fails: Creating FIPS-enabled node pools using ARM64 SKU is not supported for non-Azure Linux V3 OS Skus.
rootCause: ARM64 + FIPS combination is only supported on Azure Linux V3 OS.
solution: Use --os-sku AzureLinux as the OS when creating ARM64 node pools with FIPS enabled.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/FIPS nodes

symptom: SSH key generated on non-FIPS Ubuntu 18.04 (OpenSSL 1.0.x, before April 2021) fails to authenticate to FIPS-enabled AKS nodes.
rootCause: FIPS nodes use OpenSSL 1.1.1. SSH keys generated with OpenSSL 1.0.x are incompatible with the SSH daemon on FIPS nodes.
solution: Generate SSH keys on a system with OpenSSL 1.1.1+ (Ubuntu 18.04 after April 2021 or newer OS). Keys generated with 1.1.1 work across both FIPS and non-FIPS nodes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/FIPS nodes

symptom: AKS cluster with KMS etcd encryption and private Key Vault enters deadlock after stop/start or full node scale-down. API server and konnectivity pods cannot start.
rootCause: Deadlock: API server needs KMS keys from private Key Vault (accessible only via konnectivity), but konnectivity needs API server online for node registration. When both are down, neither can start.
solution: Upgrade to API Server VNet Integration (eliminates konnectivity dependency). Do not use cluster start/stop on KMS clusters with private keyvaults. Do not manually scale VMSS to 0.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/KMS konnectivity

symptom: Kubelogin/kubectl returns 401 Unauthorized with invalid bearer token error. kube-apiserver logs: Unable to authenticate the request: invalid bearer token. Guard pod shows AADSTS7000113.
rootCause: Service Principal or User is assigned to >200 Entra ID groups. Token contains groups:src1 link instead of inline group claims. AKS API server cannot resolve this indirect reference.
solution: 1) Decode token at https://jwt.ms and check for groups:src1 claim. 2) Reduce group memberships below 200 or configure group filtering in AAD app to include only relevant groups.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/Kube Token Analyze

symptom: Running kubectl commands against AAD-integrated AKS cluster on Kubernetes 1.24+ fails with: error: The azure auth plugin has been removed.
rootCause: Starting with K8s 1.24, AAD-integrated clusters use exec-based credential plugin (kubelogin) instead of legacy azure auth plugin. Recent kubectl versions removed the built-in plugin.
solution: 1) Install kubelogin: az aks install-cli or download from https://github.com/Azure/kubelogin/releases. 2) Convert kubeconfig: kubelogin convert-kubeconfig. On Windows use Install-AzAksCliTool.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/Kubelogin requirements for 1.24+

symptom: Microsoft Defender publisher pods crash (OOMKilled) with 403 error sending data to Log Analytics workspace.
rootCause: Invalid LA workspace credentials in kubernetes secret microsoft-defender-ws-secret (due to key rotation, workspace recreation).
solution: Compare secret credentials (kubectl get secrets -n kube-system microsoft-defender-ws-secret) with LA workspace portal keys. Update secret if mismatched or re-onboard Defender.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/Microsoft defender publisher pods crashing (OOMKilled) with 403 errors

symptom: Microsoft Defender publisher pods crash with 403. Log Analytics workspace has network isolation (private endpoint) blocking ingestion.
rootCause: LA workspace network isolation blocks data ingestion from public networks. Defender pods cannot reach the LA endpoint.
solution: Enable data ingestion through public networks in the Log Analytics workspace Networking settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/Microsoft defender publisher pods crashing (OOMKilled) with 403 errors

symptom: Microsoft Defender publisher pods crash with 403. Log Analytics workspace has local authentication disabled (disableLocalAuth=True).
rootCause: LA workspaces with local auth disabled cannot be used with Defender for containers. Defender agent authenticates using local workspace keys.
solution: Enable local auth: az resource update --ids /subscriptions/{sub}/resourcegroups/{rg}/providers/microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces/{ws} --api-version 2021-06-01 --set properties.features.disableLocalAuth=False
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/Microsoft defender publisher pods crashing (OOMKilled) with 403 errors

symptom: Microsoft Defender publisher pods crash with 403 on a single specific node only. Other nodes work fine.
rootCause: Corrupted or expired certificate/private keys on the specific node under /var/microsoft/microsoft-defender-for-cloud/.
solution: SSH to the node, delete all files under /var/microsoft/microsoft-defender-for-cloud/, restart the defender pod. Agent will re-onboard with new certificate.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/Microsoft defender publisher pods crashing (OOMKilled) with 403 errors

symptom: AKS virtual node fails: error creating provider: error setting up network: unable to delegate subnet to Azure Container Instance since it references the route table. aci-connector-linux pod logs show level=fatal.
rootCause: ACI does not support user-defined routing (UDR). Subnet for ACI virtual nodes has a route table attached, blocking subnet delegation.
solution: Remove the route table association from the subnet used for ACI virtual node. ACI virtual node subnet must not reference any route table.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/error setting up network - AKS virtual node

symptom: az aks command invoke fails with error: Unschedulable - 0/1 nodes are available: 1 Insufficient memory/cpu. No preemption victims found for incoming pod.
rootCause: az aks command invoke creates a run-command pod in the aks-command namespace with resource requests of 200m CPU and 500Mi memory. When no node has enough allocatable resources, the pod cannot be scheduled.
solution: Scale out the node pool or temporarily scale down less important workloads to free up at least 200m CPU and 500Mi memory. Use: az aks nodepool scale --cluster-name <name> --name <pool> --resource-group <rg> --node-count <count>
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/az aks command invoke fail with insufficient resource

symptom: AGIC pod logs: ErrorGetApplicationGatewayError / Failed fetching configuration for Application Gateway / dial timeout / Retrying in 10s.
rootCause: AGIC pod lacks TCP/443 connectivity to management.azure.com. Blocked by UDR to NVA/Firewall, NSG denying AzureResourceManager service tag, or K8s Network Policies.
solution: Check: 1) NVA/Firewall rules for management.azure.com:443. 2) Route table 0.0.0.0/0 entries. 3) NSG AzureResourceManager service tag. 4) K8s NetworkPolicies for AGIC pod egress.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: az aks get-credentials fails with error: The current subscription type is not permitted to perform operations on any provider namespace.
rootCause: The Azure CLI session is set to the wrong subscription context, so the command targets a subscription that does not contain the AKS cluster or lacks the required provider registration.
solution: Verify and set the correct subscription: az account set --subscription <subId>, then retry. If still failing, rerun with --verbose --debug for diagnostics.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/az aks get credential throws subscription type not permitted

symptom: AGIC pod logs show AuthorizationFailed error when configuring Application Gateway.
rootCause: AGIC identity missing role assignments. Required: Contributor on AppGW + Reader on AppGW RG. Addon identity: ingressapplicationgateway-<CLUSTER_NAME>.
solution: Assign Contributor role on AppGW resource and Reader role on AppGW resource group to AGIC identity.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: az aks command invoke fails on private cluster with error: admission webhook validation.gatekeeper.sh denied the request: Privilege escalation container is not allowed.
rootCause: Azure Policy with privilege escalation constraint is enabled but does NOT exclude the aks-command namespace used by command invoke, causing the run-command pod to be denied by gatekeeper.
solution: Update Azure Policy to exclude aks-command namespace from the privilege escalation constraint. Portal > Cluster > Policies > Edit definition > add aks-command to namespace exclusions. Or create a policy exemption. Changes take effect within 30 minutes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/run command fails on private clusters

symptom: AGIC pod logs show invalid_client error. Application Gateway config updates fail.
rootCause: AGIC uses Service Principal for auth and the SP secret/credential has expired.
solution: Renew SP secret and update AGIC config per MS docs ingress-controller-install-existing#using-a-service-principal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: Blob CSI driver provisioning fails with 403 AuthorizationFailure when using --enable-blob-driver on private AKS cluster with public access disabled on storage.
rootCause: The AKS control plane managed identity (same name as cluster) lacks Storage Account Contributor permissions on the target storage account.
solution: Grant Storage Account Contributor role to the AKS control plane managed identity on the storage account. Portal: Storage Account > IAM > Add role assignment > Storage Account Contributor > select cluster control plane identity.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/403 Authorization Error - CSI driver

symptom: AGIC pod logs: Ignore mutating App Gateway as it is not mutable / Application Gateway is in stopped state.
rootCause: Application Gateway is in Stopped state. AGIC requires Running state to apply config.
solution: Start AppGW: az network application-gateway start -g <RG> -n <NAME>. Verify: az network application-gateway show --query operationalState.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AKS pod cannot reach Azure Blob Storage with AuthorizationPermissionMismatch error when using workload identity or managed identity. Client ID may change after pod restart.
rootCause: The managed identity authenticates successfully but lacks Storage Blob Data Contributor RBAC role. Common misconfigs: wrong client ID, wrong OIDC issuer URL, missing workload identity label, missing service account annotation.
solution: 1) Verify workload identity enabled: az aks show --query securityProfile.workloadIdentity.enabled. 2) Get identity client ID. 3) Assign Storage Blob Data Contributor: az role assignment create --assignee <id> --role 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' --scope <storage-id>. 4) Verify from pod via metadata endpoint. 5) Redeploy pods after changes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/AKS AuthorizationPermissionMismatch BlobStroage

symptom: AKS apps experience intermittent connectivity timeouts, connection resets, or 503 errors accessing Azure Storage. Pod logs show connection timeout, connection reset by peer, or 503 Service Unavailable. No PVC errors.
rootCause: 1) SNAT port exhaustion - AKS nodes via Azure Load Balancer run out of SNAT ports under heavy traffic. 2) Public endpoint dependency - traffic over public internet susceptible to transient issues and DDoS protection throttling.
solution: 1) Enable Private Endpoints for Storage in same VNet. 2) Use Azure NAT Gateway on AKS subnet for more SNAT ports. 3) Implement app-level connection pooling. 4) Enable NSG Flow Logs and DDoS Diagnostic Logs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/AKS Storage Connectivity DDOS

symptom: AKS pod fails to upload files to Azure blob container intermittently with 403 error. Storage account configured with private endpoint and service endpoint.
rootCause: AKS pods intermittently resolve storage FQDN to public IP instead of private endpoint IP (client DNS issue). Traffic routes through service endpoint and is denied by storage firewall since AKS subnet is not allowed.
solution: Option 1: Allow AKS subnet in storage account firewall rules. Option 2: Fix client DNS resolution - ensure CoreDNS and private DNS zone config are correct so storage FQDN consistently resolves to private endpoint IP.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/AKS pod failed to upload files to Azure blob container Intermittently due to client DNS issues

symptom: After migrating data between storage types (e.g., Managed Disk to Azure File Share), origin and destination show different disk utilization despite matching file counts
rootCause: Different filesystems use different block sizes. ext4 defaults to 4096-byte blocks while CIFS uses 512-byte blocks. Small files waste different amounts of space due to internal fragmentation, causing apparent utilization differences.
solution: For Azure Managed Disks, configure the StorageClass with custom blocksize parameter (e.g., blocksize: '512') to match destination filesystem. Azure File Shares do not support block size customization. Efficient disk utilization requires understanding customer usage patterns.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/Analyzing Disk Utilization

symptom: PVC expansion past 5TiB for Azure Files Standard shares fails with VolumeResizeFailed error: StatusCode=400 Code='InvalidHeaderValue'. CSI keeps retrying but never succeeds, PVC stuck in limbo.
rootCause: Azure File Standard-tier shares have a maximum quota of 5TiB (5120 GiB) unless Large File Share feature is enabled. Attempting to resize beyond this limit causes persistent 400 errors. Resizing down is also unsupported.
solution: Enable Large File Shares on the storage account: Azure Portal > Storage Account > File shares > Enable 'Large file shares' > Save. Or CLI: az storage account update --name <name> -g <rg> --enable-large-file-share. Max bumps to 100 TiB.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/Azure File CSI PVC Expansion 5TiB fails

symptom: StatefulSet pods fail to mount Azure Disk after PVC resize (e.g., 300Gi to 13Ti) with fsck errors: filesystem size mismatch, invalid journal, block group descriptor checksums invalid.
rootCause: For Azure Disks >= 4TiB, host-level disk caching (cachingMode: ReadOnly) is not supported. Using caching on large disks causes stale metadata and filesystem corruption during resize/reuse.
solution: Set cachingMode: None in StorageClass parameters for large disks (>= 4TiB). Recovery: scale StatefulSet to 0, delete corrupted PVC/PV, scale back up for fresh disk. YAML: parameters: { cachingMode: None }
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/Azure disk corrupted data

symptom: Blobfuse process OOM killed by Linux kernel when mounting blob storage volumes. Pod events: 'System OOM encountered, victim process: blobfuse'. Blob CSI driver fails to mount.
rootCause: Blobfuse (< 1.3.1) uses up to 80% of available host memory for caching. As files increase in blob storage, cache grows until hitting the limit, triggering OOM killer.
solution: Upgrade blobfuse to >= 1.3.1 (Ubuntu 18.04/22.04 already updated). Set cache-size-mb mount option: mountOptions: ['cache-size-mb=3000']. Or deploy DaemonSet to update blobfuse on all nodes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/Blob CSI Driver OOM issues when mounting volumes

symptom: After AKS/node image upgrade, Azure Blob CSI mount failures: pods stuck in ContainerCreating, MountVolume.MountDevice failed, context deadline exceeded, 403 AuthorizationFailure. No customer config changes.
rootCause: AKS node image upgrades implicitly upgrade blobfuse2 (e.g., 2.4.1 to 2.5.0). Newer versions require explicit storage account type (block/adls), correct DFS endpoint for HNS, changed auth validation. Existing PVCs not auto-updated.
solution: Explicitly specify storage account type in blobfuse config: 'block' for standard Blob, 'adls' for ADLS Gen2/HNS. Ensure DFS private endpoint for ADLS Gen2. Update PVC/StorageClass parameters to match new blobfuse requirements.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/Blob CSI DriverMountFailure VolumeMountFailure

symptom: After AKS upgrade to 1.25+ (Ubuntu 22.04/blobfuse2), pods fail to mount blob containers backed by HNS storage accounts. Error: 'unable to start container process: error mounting ... no such file or directory'.
rootCause: Blobfuse2 on Ubuntu 22.04 requires --use-adls=true mount option for HNS (Hierarchical Namespace) enabled storage accounts. Without this flag, blobfuse cannot mount the container.
solution: Add --use-adls=true to StorageClass mountOptions. For existing PVs, patch each PV mountOptions individually. kubectl patch storageclass <name> -p with --use-adls=true in mountOptions array.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/Blob containers failing due to Storage Account HNS

symptom: Blob PV mount fails with: 'rpc error: code = Aborted desc = An operation with the given Volume ID xxx already exists'. Volume ID is unique in cluster but mount still fails with DeadlineExceeded.
rootCause: fsGroup ownership setting takes extremely long with large number of files in blob container. CSI driver retries mount while previous operation still runs (setting volume ownership), causing 'already exists' conflict and eventual timeout.
solution: Check file count in blob container. Use fsGroupChangePolicy: OnRootMismatch in pod securityContext to reduce ownership change time. Verify network connectivity. See MS Learn blob storage mount troubleshooting guide for other causes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/Blob mount error the given Volume ID already exists

symptom: Script to attach data disk to AKS VMSS via service principal fails: LinkedAuthorizationFailed 403 - tenant not authorized to access linked subscription. Two subscription IDs in error but customer has only one.
rootCause: VMSS StorageProfile.ImageReference references an image in a different subscription/tenant. Update-AzVmss validates access to both subscriptions, but service principal only has access to one.
solution: Set ImageReference to null before updating VMSS: $vmssCliObject.virtualMachineProfile.StorageProfile.ImageReference = $null. Then proceed with Add-AzVmssDataDisk and Update-AzVmss. Ref: https://github.com/Azure/AKS/issues/1819
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/Cannot attach data disk with service principal

symptom: Azure Disk PV fails to mount: 'MountVolume.MountDevice failed while expanding volume - NodeExpand found CSI plugin to not support node expansion'. Pods stuck in creating with FailedMount.
rootCause: Azure Disk CSI driver < 1.11 does not support online volume resizing. If PV/PVC capacity mismatch (e.g., disk resized in Portal without updating PV/PVC), mount fails.
solution: 1) Scale down to 0. 2) Update PV and PVC capacity to match actual disk size. 3) If resized via Portal, snapshot disk then update PV/PVC. 4) If still failing, recreate PV/PVC at desired size, restore from snapshot. Upgrade CSI driver >= 1.11 for online resize.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/Disk fails to mount due to node expansion errors

symptom: AKS cluster delete fails with ResourceGroupDeletionBlocked/NetworkProfileAlreadyInUseWithContainerNics - network profile vk-* still in use with container nics (Virtual Kubelet/ACI)
rootCause: ACI network profile container NICs not cleaned up properly after Virtual Nodes/ACI removal. Known bug (ADO work item 14104, fix PR 4202309) with possible regression.
solution: No customer-ready mitigation. Escalate via IcM to Support / EEE ACI team with troubleshooting details including AKS operation ID, ARM correlation ID, and NRP HTTP 400 errors against the vk-* network profile.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Cluster delete with NetworkProfileAlreadyInUseWithContainerNics

symptom: Unable to delete subnet or parent VNet after AKS cluster deletion - subnet still delegated to Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters with ServiceAssociationLink (SAL) remaining
rootCause: When AKS cluster using Azure CNI Overlay or Pod Subnet networking is deleted, the ServiceAssociationLink (SAL) created during cluster provisioning is not automatically removed
solution: 1) Remove delegation via Portal: Virtual Networks > Subnets > select subnet > Delegate subnet to service > None > Save. 2) Or via CLI: az network vnet subnet update --resource-group <RG> --name <subnet> --vnet-name <vnet> --remove delegations. 3) If above fails, use Jarvis actions for undelegation (note: restricted in China/21V, CSS cannot perform Jarvis undelegation in China).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Undelegate Subnet

symptom: AKS node pool rollback fails with OperationNotAllowed: cannot rollback with blue-green upgrade strategy when there is no in-progress blue-green upgrade
rootCause: UpgradeStrategy_BlueGreen requires an active blue/green cycle. If the upgrade already finished or was never started, rollbacks are blocked.
solution: Resume the original blue/green upgrade or switch the pool to rolling strategy, then issue the rollback.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentpool Rollback

symptom: NAP/Karpenter scaling fails with InsufficientCapacityError: TotalRegionalCores quota exceeded, new nodes cannot be provisioned
rootCause: The subscription's total regional vCPU cores quota is exhausted, preventing Karpenter from launching new VMs for pending pods.
solution: Customer needs to submit a quota increase request for their subscription's total regional cores limit via Azure portal or support request.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Node Autoprovision (Karpenter)
Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Tools/Jarvis/Synchronize ARM when VMSS not visible Jarvis action
symptom: AKS resources (VMSS, disks, load balancers) not visible in Azure Portal, Azure Resource Explorer, or Azure Support Center Resource Explorer, even though the resources exist and are functional in the underlying Resource Provider
rootCause: ARM cache hydration issue - Azure Resource Manager cache is not synchronized with underlying Resource Providers (CRP, DiskRP, AKS RP). The resource exists in the RP but is not reflected in the ARM cache.
solution: Use Jarvis action 'Azure Resource Manager > Resource Synchronization > Synchronize resource state' with the fully qualified resource ID to rehydrate ARM cache. If the resource ID is unknown, use CRP QoS Kusto query (cluster('azcrp').database('crp_allprod').ApiQosEvent_nonGet) filtering on SubscriptionID and MC_ resource group to identify it. Resource appears in ARM after a few minutes.

symptom: Blob CSI Driver installation with useDataPlaneAPI=true parameter fails with StatusCode=403, ErrorCode=AuthorizationFailure when the storage account has firewall or VNet restrictions on a private storage endpoint.
rootCause: The useDataPlaneAPI=true parameter in the storage class triggers data plane API calls for blob container create/delete operations. While data plane API has higher throttling limits than SRP API, it is blocked by storage account firewall or Virtual Network restrictions, resulting in AuthorizationFailure.
solution: Remove the useDataPlaneAPI=true parameter from the storage class configuration. After updating, create a new PVC to apply the change. Existing PVCs retain the old storage class settings until recreated.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/useDataPlaneAPI=true:StatusCode=403, ErrorCode=AuthorizationFailure

symptom: AKS node pool rollback fails with AgentPoolRollbackNoHistory: rollback to node image X with orchestrator Y is not permitted
rootCause: recentlyUsedVersions is empty, usually because this pool has never completed a full upgrade.
solution: Let at least one upgrade finish so history is populated, then retry the rollback.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentpool Rollback

symptom: NAP/Karpenter scaling fails with InsufficientCapacityError: all requested instance types were unavailable during launch
rootCause: Customer is out of quota for the specific SKU families selected in their NodePool requirements. Karpenter cannot find available capacity for any of the allowed instance types.
solution: 1) Expand nodepool to include additional SKU families (e.g. add F, E families alongside D) via karpenter.azure.com/sku-family values; 2) Or request per-VM quota for additional SKUs via Azure support.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Node Autoprovision (Karpenter)

symptom: GMSA enabled pods fail to start with error "The RPC server is unavailable" and customer pods fail with ImagePullBackOff when using a private ACR in a private AKS cluster with on-premises DNS.
rootCause: On-premises DNS server is unable to resolve the private endpoints for AKS (azmk8s.io) and ACR (azurecr.io), causing GMSA credential retrieval and container image pull failures.
solution: Configure on-premises DNS servers with conditional forwarders: create a forwarder of azmk8s.io to 168.63.129.16 for AKS clusters, and a forwarder of azurecr.io to 168.63.129.16 for ACR registries. This enables resolution of private endpoints.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/TSG GMSA Deployments Entered Failed State

symptom: Private AKS cluster nodes cannot resolve API server private FQDN when custom DNS server is configured in the VNet (on Azure or on-prem)
rootCause: Custom DNS server cannot resolve A records under Azure private DNS zone. The DNS queries need to be forwarded to Azure default DNS server (168.63.129.16). Additionally, the private DNS zone must have a virtual network link to the DNS server VNet.
solution: 1) For Azure-based DNS server: link the private DNS zone to both the custom DNS server VNet and AKS VNet (AKS VNet is auto-linked). Configure DNS forwarding to 168.63.129.16. 2) For on-prem DNS server: set up an Azure VM as DNS forwarder, link private DNS zone to that VM VNet. On-prem DNS forwards to Azure VM, which forwards to 168.63.129.16. Key: ensure virtual network link exists between private DNS zone and DNS server VNet.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/How Tos/Networking/Setup private AKS with custom DNS server

symptom: AKS node pool rollback fails with AgentPoolRollbackVersionNotAllowed: invalid version pair rollback
rootCause: The (nodeImageVersion, orchestratorVersion) pair requested does not match any entry in recentlyUsedVersions.
solution: Fetch the pool (expand properties.recentlyUsedVersions) and choose an exact pair from that list. Alternatively perform another upgrade to create the pair you need.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentpool Rollback

symptom: NAP/Karpenter stuck provisioning and repeatedly failing on the same instance type, unable to scale out
rootCause: Karpenter does not yet have proper implementation for certain instance types or their specific settings. When it encounters an unsupported SKU, it keeps retrying the same failing type.
solution: Exclude the problematic SKU from the NodePool using 'node.kubernetes.io/instance-type' with operator 'NotIn' in the requirements section. This unblocks the scaler to use other instance types.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Node Autoprovision (Karpenter)

symptom: AKS node pool rollback fails with AgentPoolRollbackVersionExpired - rollback target version pair is older than 7 days
rootCause: The rollback target version pair exists but its timestamp is older than 7 days. AKS refuses rollback for safety after this window.
solution: Roll back to a more recent pair within 7 days, or re-upgrade to refresh history then roll back promptly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentpool Rollback

symptom: NAP NodeClaim initialization failures across multiple regions with VMExtensionProvisioningError loops
rootCause: CRP (Compute Resource Provider) provisioning issues causing VMExtensionProvisioningError failures that affect NodeClaims across regions. May correlate with known Sev1 incidents.
solution: 1) Check async_context_activity for VMExtensionProvisioningError loops; 2) Verify CRP health status; 3) Cross-reference known Sev1 incidents if NodeClaims fail across regions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Node Autoprovision (Karpenter)

symptom: AKS node pool rollback fails with AgentPoolRollbackVersionNotAllowed: Agent pool rollback version is older than control plane by more than 3 minor versions
rootCause: The requested rollback version is more than 3 minors behind the control plane (futureMCVer - futAPVer > 3).
solution: Pick a rollback target within 3 minor versions of the control-plane version.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentpool Rollback

symptom: Self-hosted Karpenter using unsupported Community Gallery VHDs instead of AKS-supported images
rootCause: Customer-hosted Karpenter is pulling VHDs from the Community Image Gallery rather than official AKS-supported images from the AKS release page. This configuration is not supported.
solution: 1) Use 'kubectl describe node' to verify node image origin; 2) Flag as unsupported scenario early in triage; 3) Redirect customer to use only AKS VHDs from https://releases.aks.azure.com/
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Node Autoprovision (Karpenter)

symptom: AKS Artifact Streaming: Conversion operation failed due to an unknown error when converting image in ACR
rootCause: Artifact Streaming image conversion failed in ACR backend. Root cause needs investigation via ACR Kusto logs.
solution: Query ACR Kusto (cluster ACR, database acrprod, table KubernetesContainers) filtering by operationId shown in CLI output. Check if ACR Artifact Streaming is properly enabled on the repo.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Artifact Streaming

symptom: Karpenter consolidation messages appear in logs but customer interprets them as scaling errors or failures
rootCause: Karpenter consolidation is working as designed - it attempts to reschedule/consolidate workloads to reduce infrastructure costs. These messages are informational, not errors.
solution: 1) Explain consolidation logic to customer; 2) Verify behavior via KarpenterEvents Kusto table; 3) If needed, reproduce in isolated cluster using inflation deployment from wiki doc to demonstrate normal behavior.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Node Autoprovision (Karpenter)

symptom: AKS pod deployment fails with rpc error 503 Service Unavailable when pulling image with Artifact Streaming enabled
rootCause: ACR is not properly attached to AKS, so AKS lacks permission to access ACR for Artifact Streaming.
solution: Verify ACR is attached to AKS properly. Ensure the AKS managed identity has AcrPull role on the ACR. Use az aks check-acr to validate.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Artifact Streaming

symptom: AKS upgrade operation taking unusually long time, nodes are being drained very slowly
rootCause: The nodepool's node drain timeout is set to a high custom value. When specified, AKS respects waiting on pod disruption budgets for the configured duration (in minutes). Default is 30 minutes if not set.
solution: 1) Check ASC Agent Pools tab for custom drain timeout value; 2) Use Kusto query joining AsyncContextActivity with LatencyTraceEvent on spanID to check exact drain start/end times per node; 3) If timeout is too high, customer can lower it via CLI/API.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Node Drain Timeout

symptom: Cannot disable Artifact Streaming on an AKS node pool - az aks nodepool update does not expose a way to turn it off
rootCause: AKS does not expose a way to disable Artifact Streaming at the nodepool level via az aks nodepool update.
solution: Delete and recreate the node pool without --enable-artifact-streaming. This is disruptive: nodes will be replaced and workloads rescheduled.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Artifact Streaming

symptom: PUT Agent Pool request containing node initialization taints returns Bad Request (400) error
rootCause: Due to a limitation in cluster autoscaler, PUT AP (Agent Pool level) requests containing node initialization taints are blocked by AKS RP. Only PUT MC (Managed Cluster level) operations are allowed.
solution: Customer should perform managed cluster level operations (PUT MC) instead of agent pool level operations (PUT AP) when including node initialization taints. This limitation will be removed at a later date.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Node Initialization Taints

symptom: AKS Teleport (private preview) retirement: after April 15 2025 cannot create new node pool with Teleport; after July 15 2025 all Teleport code removed causing potential workload breakage
rootCause: Teleport (private preview) is retired. AKS released Artifact Streaming as replacement in Nov 2023 public preview.
solution: Disable Teleport on existing node pools: az aks nodepool update --aks-custom-headers EnableACRTeleport=false. Migrate to Artifact Streaming: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/aks/artifact-streaming
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Artifact Streaming

symptom: After updating node initialization taints and upgrading the cluster, old init taints still appear on some nodes
rootCause: AKS-RP does not guarantee removal of non-AKS-managed taints during upgrade. If old init taints are still on node replicas when upgrade runs, they get re-applied to re-created nodes.
solution: Before upgrading, ensure old init taints are removed from all nodes using: kubectl taint nodes <nodeName> <taintKey>=<taintValue>:<taintEffect>-
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Node Initialization Taints

symptom: AKS Auto Assign Host Ports: pod mutation skipped with Invalid host port annotation or Unknown protocol in host port annotation in ccp-webhook logs
rootCause: The host port annotation format is invalid. Valid format: <port>/<protocol>,<port>-<port>/<protocol> (e.g. 80/tcp,53/udp,40000-50000/tcp).
solution: Fix the annotation value to match the valid format. Check ccp-webhook mutator logs via Kusto ControlPlaneEventsAll filtering AutoAssignedHostPortPodMutator.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Auto Assign Host Ports

symptom: Request to add node initialization taints fails with duplicate permanent/initialization taints error
rootCause: AKS blocks requests that contain duplicate taints between the 'nodeTaints' (permanent, AKS-managed) and 'nodeInitializationTaints' fields.
solution: Remove the duplicate taint from one of the two fields (nodeTaints or nodeInitializationTaints) to resolve the conflict.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Node Initialization Taints

symptom: AKS Auto Assign Host Ports: Failed to allocate host port for container - no enough host ports available or fetch port status error
rootCause: ccp-webhook could not find available ports. Either it failed to fetch pod.HostPort/svc.NodePort status, or all ports in the defined range are occupied.
solution: Check error field in mutator logs. If no enough host ports available: reduce pods per node or expand port range. If fetch port status error: investigate Kubernetes API connectivity from ccp-webhook.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Auto Assign Host Ports

symptom: Node Public IPTags configuration fails with frontend validation error on AKS create or nodepool add
rootCause: Multiple validation requirements not met: feature flag Microsoft.ContainerService/NodePublicIPTagsPreview not registered, node-public-ip not enabled, VMSSNodePublicIPPrefixID set, invalid tag name/value, or RoutingPreference mixed with other IPTags.
solution: Verify: 1) NodePublicIPTagsPreview feature flag is registered; 2) --enable-node-public-ip is set; 3) VMSSNodePublicIPPrefixID is not set; 4) Only valid tags (FirstPartyUsage/NetworkDomain/RoutingPreference); 5) RoutingPreference value must be 'Internet' and not mixed with other IPTag types.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Node Public IPTags

symptom: VMSS scale down blocked by 'VM NIC not found' (NotFound) error when using Node Public IPTags
rootCause: Known NRP (Network Resource Provider) issue where VM NIC cannot be found during VMSS scale-down operations with public IPTags configured. Under investigation (WI: 26231812).
solution: Retry the operation. This is a known intermittent NRP issue being tracked via work item https://msazure.visualstudio.com/One/_workitems/edit/26231812.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Node Public IPTags

symptom: AKS upgrade operation taking unusually long, appears stuck between node drain and reimaging steps
rootCause: The node pool's node soak duration is set to a non-zero value (up to 30 minutes max). This is the wait time after draining a node and before reimaging it, added per-node during upgrade. Default is 0.
solution: 1) Check node pool configuration for soak duration setting; 2) Use Kusto query on AsyncContextActivity filtering for 'using customer node soak duration' message to see actual soak time used; 3) If too high, customer can lower it.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Node Upgrade Soak Duration

symptom: OSSKU Migration returns 'property change not allowed' error when attempting to change node OS type
rootCause: For preview features, the OSSKUMigrationPreview feature flag must be registered on the subscription. ARM/Bicep templates require API version 2023-07-01 or newer.
solution: 1) Register 'OSSKUMigrationPreview' feature flag on the subscription; 2) If using ARM/Bicep, ensure API version is 2023-07-01 or later.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/OSSKU Migration

symptom: AKS Workload Identity: pod cannot fetch token, logs show failed to create confidential client with Authority does not appear to use https
rootCause: Workload Identity webhook was not ready when the pod spawned, so AZURE_AUTHORITY_HOST and other required env vars were not injected into pod manifest.
solution: Restart the affected pod. The webhook should be ready on subsequent pod creation. Verify webhook running: kubectl logs -n kube-system -l azure-workload-identity.io/system
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure AD Workload Identity

symptom: OSSKU Migration cannot select AzureLinux as target OSSKU, returns InvalidOSSKU error
rootCause: The nodepool uses UseGPUDedicatedVHD or Confidential VM (CVM), which are Ubuntu-exclusive features and not compatible with AzureLinux.
solution: 1) Check operation context logs for 'Agentpool has UseGPUDedicatedVHD/CVM enabled, cannot change OSSKU'; 2) Check ASI for VM Image name starting with 'aks-ubuntu-gpu-' (GPU VHD) or node label 'kubernetes.azure.com/security-type=ConfidentialVM' (CVM); 3) These nodepools cannot migrate to AzureLinux.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/OSSKU Migration

symptom: AKS Workload Identity components running in legacy namespace azure-workload-identity-system instead of kube-system
rootCause: Previous versions of Workload Identity feature used the azure-workload-identity-system namespace. Cluster was not migrated after upgrade.
solution: An IcM to PG is required to perform the migration to kube-system namespace. Query AsyncContextActivity for 'cluster is using legacy webhook namespace' to identify affected clusters.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure AD Workload Identity

symptom: OSSKU Migration cannot select Ubuntu as target OSSKU for nodepool using Kata pod sandboxing
rootCause: The nodepool has Kata pod sandboxing enabled (workloadRuntime: KataMshvVmIsolation), which is AzureLinux-exclusive and not compatible with Ubuntu.
solution: Check if the nodepool uses Kata pod sandboxing. If so, the nodepool cannot migrate to Ubuntu OSSKU.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/OSSKU Migration

symptom: OSSKU Migration fails when attempting to change node count simultaneously with OSSKU change
rootCause: Simultaneous agentpool scale and OSSKU migration operations are not allowed. The agentpool count field must stay the same during the migration.
solution: Perform OSSKU migration and scale operations separately - first migrate OSSKU, then scale (or vice versa).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/OSSKU Migration

symptom: AKS Cilium CNI: cilium monitor shows CT: Map insertion failed - connection tracking table filling up causing packet drops
rootCause: Connection tracking (CT) table is filling up due to too many connections within a 5-minute period from pods on the node.
solution: Schedule fewer pods per node, or investigate if application workload is creating excessive connections. NOTE: Cilium docs suggest adjusting CT GC interval or map size, but this is NOT currently configurable in AKS.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure Cilium CNI

symptom: OSSKU Migration fails due to Pod Disruption Budget blocking node drain during upgrade
rootCause: Customer's PDB is too restrictive for the upgrade surge capacity, preventing AKS from draining nodes to update them during the OSSKU migration (which follows node image upgrade flow).
solution: Refine PDBs following Azure best practices to allow AKS upgrade operations. Ensure PDB minAvailable/maxUnavailable allows at least one node to be drained with the configured surge capacity.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/OSSKU Migration

symptom: AKS Cilium CNI: cluster has multiple nodes but only one cilium-operator pod running instead of expected two
rootCause: Customer likely created cluster with single node then scaled up. Cilium operator replica count was not reconciled.
solution: Reconcile the cluster (e.g. az aks update or trigger a no-op operation). After reconcile, should see two cilium-operator replicas.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure Cilium CNI

symptom: AKS operation abort returns 'Operation Not supported' error message
rootCause: A toggle has been put in place that disabled the abort operation, possibly due to a breaking change in the abort feature.
solution: Reach out to the owner SIG listed on the TSG for clarification on why abort has been disabled. Do not attempt to work around the toggle.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Operation Abort

symptom: AKS Cilium CNI: single node cluster has two cilium-operator pods with one stuck in Pending state
rootCause: Customer created cluster with multiple nodes then scaled down to single node. Cilium operator still expects 2 replicas.
solution: Reconcile the cluster to adjust cilium-operator replica count to 1 for single-node cluster.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure Cilium CNI

symptom: AKS operation abort was accepted but provisioning state does not change to Canceled within a minute
rootCause: The abort request passed sync-side validations but failed on the async side. Possible edge case where the operation terminated before being aborted, or an internal issue preventing the cancel.
solution: 1) Run GET on MC/AP to check provisioningState; 2) Check AsyncContextActivity for logs before context cancellation; 3) Check AsyncContextlessActivity for logs after context was canceled; 4) Look for 'Operation finished with status' log message to verify final state.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Operation Abort

symptom: AKS Cilium CNI: ASI shows Cluster Degraded with CustomerNodesNotReadyCNI even though nodes are actually Ready
rootCause: Known display issue in ASI. Cilium CNI is installed via daemonset at node startup and ASI detects the brief initial NotReady state, showing Degraded even after CNI installation completes.
solution: Verify actual node status in ASI Node Pools tab. If nodes show Ready, cluster is healthy - this is an ASI display issue. Fix expected in the new monitoring stack.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure Cilium CNI

symptom: Networking issues (traffic blocked, connectivity failures) after AKS outbound type migration
rootCause: Changing outbound type (loadBalancer/NATGateway/userDefinedRouting) changes the cluster's egress configuration, which can alter source IPs. Existing firewall rules, NSGs, or third-party services filtering by source IP will break.
solution: 1) Use Kusto query on AsyncQoSEvents filtering for 'enableOutboundMigration' to confirm migration occurred; 2) Update downstream firewall rules, NSGs, and allowlists with the new source IPs from the new outbound configuration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Outbound Type Migration

symptom: AKS Cilium CNI: Unattended Upgrades on Ubuntu 22.04 leaves nodes in NotReady state until manually restarted
rootCause: Known issue affecting both BYO CNI + Cilium and AKS-managed Cilium on Ubuntu 22.04. Unattended OS upgrades break Cilium networking.
solution: Manually mitigate by restarting affected nodes. Track repair: https://msazure.visualstudio.com/CloudNativeCompute/_workitems/edit/17508542
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure Cilium CNI

symptom: Kata pod fails to start with FailedCreatePodSandBox: failed to create shim task with EOF error on vm.boot
rootCause: This error indicates an issue in the Kata software stack (HVLite/containerd shim layer), not a customer configuration problem.
solution: Escalate to the Kata/Pod Sandboxing feature team via ICM. This is a Kata stack bug, not resolvable by customer-side actions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Pod Sandboxing and Kernel Isolation (kata)

symptom: AKS Cilium CNI: aad-pod-identity pods experience i/o timeout connecting to 169.254.169.254 (IMDS) on Cilium clusters
rootCause: Known bug with aad-pod-identity on Cilium clusters. aad-pod-identity is deprecated since Oct 2023.
solution: Migrate to Azure Workload Identity: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/workload-identity-overview. aad-pod-identity is no longer maintained.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure Cilium CNI

symptom: Kata pod stuck in Running phase and never reaches Succeeded phase after command completion
rootCause: Known intermittent issue in the Kata runtime where the pod does not transition to Succeeded phase even after the container command finishes. Rare and not consistently reproducible.
solution: Escalate to the Kata feature owner (Fanglu Guo). This is a known intermittent Kata runtime issue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Pod Sandboxing and Kernel Isolation (kata)

symptom: AKS Azure Linux: container fails with certificate errors because /etc/ssl/certs is a symbolic link to /etc/pki/tls/certs which does not resolve inside the container
rootCause: Azure Linux (RPM-based) stores certificates at /etc/pki/tls/certs with /etc/ssl/certs as a symlink. Containers mapping only /etc/ssl/certs get a dangling symlink since /etc/pki is not mapped.
solution: Map /etc/pki into the container using a hostPath volume with type DirectoryOrCreate. This works on both Ubuntu and Azure Linux hosts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure Linux (Mariner)

symptom: Pod creation fails with RuntimeClass kata-mshv-vm-isolation not found error when trying to use Kata pod sandboxing
rootCause: The runtimeClass kata-mshv-vm-isolation is automatically created by OverlayManager for clusters with k8s >= 1.24.0 and KataVMIsolationPreview feature registered. Missing class means feature flag not registered or k8s version too old.
solution: 1) Verify KataVMIsolationPreview feature flag is registered on the subscription; 2) Ensure cluster k8s version >= 1.24.0; 3) Check that at least one nodepool has matching labels (kubernetes.azure.com/kata-mshv-vm-isolation=true) and correct workloadRuntime.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Pod Sandboxing and Kernel Isolation (kata)

symptom: AKS Azure Linux: OSSKU is invalid or InvalidOSSKU error when creating or upgrading node pool with AzureLinux3 osSku on unsupported Kubernetes version
rootCause: The osSku AzureLinux3 is not supported for the Kubernetes version being used, or required feature flags (AFEC) are not registered. CVM on AzureLinux requires Azure Linux 3.0+.
solution: Check supported osSku by k8s version: AzureLinux3 GA on k8s 1.28-1.36. AzureLinux2 retirement Nov 30 2025. For CVM: requires Azure Linux 3.0+ (k8s 1.28+). Register AFEC flag if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure Linux (Mariner)

symptom: AKS cluster delete fails with ResourceGroupDeletionBlocked, inner error NetworkProfileAlreadyInUseWithContainerNics. Network profile starting with 'vk-' (Virtual Kubelet/ACI) cannot be deleted because container NICs are still bound.
rootCause: ACI container NICs remain bound to the Virtual Kubelet network profile after cluster/virtual node operations. Possible regression of a 2021 bug in ACI (fix PR 4202309).
solution: No customer-ready mitigation. Escalate via ICM to 'Support / EEE ACI'. Provide AKS RP operation logs and ARM correlation ID showing HTTP 400 against Microsoft.Network on the 'vk-' network profile.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Cluster delete with NetworkProfileAlreadyInUseWithContainerNics

symptom: Cannot delete subnet or parent VNet after removing AKS cluster. Error: InUseSubnetCannotBeDeleted, subnet still has ServiceAssociationLink 'AzureKubernetesService' from CNI Overlay or Pod Subnet delegation.
rootCause: When AKS cluster with Azure CNI Overlay or Pod Subnet is deleted, the ServiceAssociationLink (SAL) created by subnet delegation to Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters should be auto-removed but sometimes remains.
solution: 1) Remove delegation via Azure Portal: VNet > Subnets > select subnet > Delegate to 'None' > Save. 2) Via CLI: 'az network vnet subnet update --resource-group RG --name subnet --vnet-name vnet --remove delegations'. 3) If above fails: use Jarvis actions for undelegation (CSS except China has access with normal AKSJIT permissions). Ref: CloudNativeCompute wiki Subnet-Delegation-and-Undelegation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Undelegate Subnet

symptom: ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady error during CRUD/Start operations: kube-proxy pods stuck in Pending/PodInitializing with ImagePullBackOff, kubelet logs show 'x509: certificate signed by unknown authority' when pulling mcr.microsoft.com images
rootCause: Third-party firewall or network appliance performing SSL/TLS deep packet inspection (DPI) between AKS nodes and MCR, inserting its own certificate. Containerd/kubelet rejects the substituted certificate as untrusted.
solution: 1) SSH/node-shell into the affected node, check kubelet logs for x509 certificate errors; 2) Ask customer to temporarily disable SSL packet inspection on their firewall/proxy for MCR endpoints; 3) Retry the cluster operation after disabling DPI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady because kube proxy stuck pending

symptom: ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady error during CRUD/Start operations: non-AKS user pods (e.g. create-cmr-metrics-server) deployed in kube-system namespace are not in Running state, blocking cluster operations
rootCause: AKS RP validates all pods in kube-system namespace during operations. User workloads deployed in kube-system (especially those with names matching system components like metrics-server) that are in Completed/Failed/Pending state cause validation to fail.
solution: 1) Identify the failing pods in ContextActivity logs (look for non-AKS pod names); 2) Ask customer to move user workloads out of kube-system namespace; 3) Per AKS FAQ, do NOT deploy user workloads in kube-system namespace; 4) Temporarily delete/move problematic pods to unblock operations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady kube system namespace for user pods

symptom: During nodepool upgrade, autoscaler deletes the surge node causing GetSurgedVms_CountNotMatch / AgentCountNotMatch error; existing nodes upgraded in-place instead of creating new surge nodes; pod eviction takes 5-10 minutes per node
rootCause: Race condition between AKS RP and cluster autoscaler. AKS RP failed to annotate the surge node with ScaleDownDisabledReasonUpgrade tag, allowing autoscaler to issue forceDelete on the surge node while upgrade was in progress.
solution: 1) Recommend customer disable cluster autoscaler before performing nodepool upgrades; 2) Re-enable autoscaler after upgrade completes; 3) Bug tracked: ADO work items 30155571 / 31289428; 4) Check AsyncContextActivity for surge node creation and CRP logs for VMSS scale events.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/GetSurgedVms_CountNotMatch_for_upgrade

symptom: AKS nodepool operations (scale, update, add disk) fail with MMAExtension error: Enable failed with exit code 53 - Installation failed due to incorrect workspace key
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace key configured in AKS (stored in omsagent-secret in kube-system) does not match the actual workspace primary key in Azure portal, or the node lacks outbound connectivity to required Log Analytics endpoints.
solution: 1) Get LAW resource ID from az aks show addonProfiles.omsagent; 2) Compare workspace key from kubectl get secrets omsagent-secret -n kube-system -o yaml (base64 decode) with LAW portal Agents Management > Primary Key; 3) If keys mismatch, update the secret; 4) Verify outbound NSG rules allow access to LAW endpoints; 5) Engage Azure Monitor team if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Invalid workspace key fails cluster operations

symptom: Newly created nodes fail to register with AKS API server; autoscaler scales up VMSS VMs but they never join the cluster; ScaleUpTimedOut warning after 15 minutes; issue persists with manual scale-up
rootCause: VMSS Custom Script Extension (vmssCSE) was accidentally removed from the VMSS. Without CSE, nodes cannot bootstrap essential components and register with the Kubernetes API server.
solution: 1) Verify all 3 required extensions are present on AKS VMSS: vmssCSE, AKSLinuxBilling, AKSLinuxExtension; 2) If vmssCSE is missing, create a new agent pool and migrate workloads; 3) Do not manually remove VMSS extensions from AKS-managed scale sets.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/New nodes are not getting registered with AKS API server

symptom: NAP (Node Auto Provision / Karpenter) scaled nodes stuck in NotReady state; NodeNetworkConfig events show 'primaryIP is nil' and 'Subnet is full' errors; NodeClaim status Unknown; azure-cns cannot create network container
rootCause: Pod CIDR exhaustion in Azure CNI Overlay cluster. Each node pre-allocates a /24 block (256 IPs) from Pod CIDR. When Pod CIDR is too small (e.g. /21 supports only 8 nodes), additional nodes cannot obtain IP allocation.
solution: 1) Check NodeNetworkConfig events for 'Subnet is full' and 'primaryIP is nil' errors; 2) Expand Pod CIDR using az aks update per docs (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/azure-cni-overlay-pod-expand); 3) After expansion, NotReady nodes auto-recover; azure-cns pods redeploy but existing pods unaffected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node Auto Provision subnet is full and pod cidr exhausted

symptom: AKS cluster or nodepool creation fails with SkuNotAvailable / VM Size Not Available error; requested VM SKU not offered in the target region or zone
rootCause: Requested VM size is not available in the subscription due to regional restrictions, zonal limitations, or platform capacity constraints.
solution: 1) Check SKU availability: az vm list-skus --location <region> --size <size> --all; 2) Check ASC Resource Explorer > Subscription > RP Details > SKU Restrictions; 3) Advise alternative VM size or zone; 4) Transfer to SAP: Azure > Subscription management > Compute-VM (cores-vCPUs) subscription limit increases.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node scaling fails due to allocation or capacity issues

symptom: AKS node scaling, creation, or upgrade fails due to vCPU quota exceeded; operation triggers new node creation but exceeds Total Regional vCPUs or VM family quota
rootCause: vCPU quotas are arranged in two tiers per subscription per region: Total Regional vCPUs and VM size family cores. When node creation would exceed either quota, VMSS deployment is blocked.
solution: 1) Customer submits direct quota request (many standard VM family increases auto-approved); 2) Check Usage + quotas in Azure portal; 3) For large/high-demand VMs, manual review required; 4) Transfer to SAP: Azure > Subscription management > Compute-VM (cores-vCPUs); 5) Include: Subscription ID, Region, SKU Size, Restriction Type, cores requested. Do NOT transfer to VM/VMSS team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node scaling fails due to allocation or capacity issues

symptom: AKS operations (scale/update/upgrade) fail with 'Unable to get log analytics workspace info. ResourceNotFound' error pointing to monitoring addon; error is misleading as actual component may be Azure Defender or Container Insights
rootCause: Log Analytics Workspace (LAW) referenced by AKS addon (Container Insights, OMSAgent, or Azure Defender) has been deleted or is inaccessible. Error message misleadingly blames the monitoring addon even when Defender is the actual consumer.
solution: 1) In ASC main cluster view, search LAW resource ID to identify which component uses it; 2) Option A: Disable/re-enable Defender: az aks update --disable-defender then --enable-defender; 3) Option B: Update in-place with existing LAW via config.json; 4) If LAW is for OMSAgent, see separate TSG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Operation fails due to missing Log Analytics workspace

symptom: AKS cluster CRUD operations (upgrade/scale) fail with InternalServerError, GetSKUStoreError, or InvalidAuthenticationToken: The received access token is not valid - at least one of the claims puid or altsecid or oid should be present
rootCause: The Service Principal linked to the AKS PG well-known App ID (7319c514-...) was deleted or soft-deleted from the customer tenant, breaking AKS RP ability to call ARM/CRP
solution: Re-register the AKS resource provider to recreate the SP: az provider register --namespace Microsoft.ContainerService. If SP is still in soft-delete, recover it (may require collab with Azure Identity team)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Operations Fail with code GetSKUStoreError InvalidAuthenticationToken

symptom: AKS operations fail with InvalidLoadBalancerProfileAllocatedOutboundPorts: allocated ports X is not in an allowable range given the number of nodes and IPs provisioned
rootCause: Node count x allocated outbound ports per node exceeds total available SNAT ports from outbound public IPs (each IP provides 64000 ports). Default: 1024 ports/node for <=50 nodes
solution: Validate config with formula: 64000 x outbound_IPs / ports_per_node >= total_node_count (including maxSurge during upgrades). Either increase outbound IP count or reduce allocated ports per node
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Operations fail with code InvalidLoadBalancerProfileAllocatedOutboundPorts

symptom: AKS scale/upgrade/create fails with QuotaExceeded: Operation results in exceeding quota limits of Core. Maximum allowed: N, Current in use: M, Additional requested: X
rootCause: Subscription compute (CPU vCPU/core) quota exceeded for the target VM size or region
solution: Customer requests core quota increase via Azure Portal (per-vm-quota-requests). After quota increase, retry the operation. If upgrade was in progress, re-run upgrade via CLI
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Quota limits are exceeded

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with QuotaExceeded ManagedClusterCountExceedsQuotaLimit: exceeding quota limits for managed clusters per subscription per region
rootCause: AKS managed cluster count per subscription per region quota exceeded (enforced since 2025-09-02). Distinct from compute/core quota - limits number of AKS clusters
solution: File support ticket to Azure/Subscription limits and quota/Azure Kubernetes Service (NOT AKS support path). ICM auto-created to AzureKubernetesService/ClusterQuotaRequests. Quota increase via Geneva Action PutSubscriptionLimit. Decline for student/free-trial subs; internal subs route to Platform team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/QuotaExceeded QuotaExceededManagedCluster

symptom: AKS scaling or upgrade fails with image not found: InvalidParameter - images referenced from deployment template are not found (Publisher/Offer/Sku/Version no longer available)
rootCause: Kubernetes version is outdated/unsupported and base VM images (e.g. WindowsServer 2019-datacenter-core-smalldisk) are no longer available in marketplace
solution: 1) Create new nodepool with supported K8s version 2) Migrate pods (check custom labels with kubectl get nodes --show-labels) 3) kubectl drain old nodes 4) Delete old nodepool 5) Upgrade cluster to same or higher version 6) Verify provisioning state
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Scale or upgrade failed with image not found

symptom: AKS node pool operations (scale/upgrade/reconcile) fail with VMExtensionHandlerNonTransientError for unsupported VM extension (e.g. DependencyAgentLinux, AzureMonitorLinuxAgent installed outside AKS)
rootCause: Unsupported VM extensions manually installed on AKS VMSS outside AKS control. AKS cannot validate/maintain extension state, causing non-retriable reconcile failures
solution: 1) Identify unsupported extensions on VMSS (AppLens detector available) 2) Remove extensions from VMSS via Portal/CLI/ARM 3) Reconcile or retry failed operation 4) If removal impossible, recreate nodepool. Prevention: never manually install VM extensions on AKS VMSS
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/UnsupportedVMExtensions

symptom: AKS cluster stuck in Starting state or autoscaler nodes not visible despite VMSS instances being created. Kusto shows: vmss <name> instance N has no vmss cse extension
rootCause: The vmssCSE (CustomScript) extension was removed/deleted from the VMSS backing the AKS node pool. Without this extension, nodes cannot complete provisioning and join the cluster
solution: 1) Abort ongoing operation: az aks operation-abort -n <cluster> -g <rg> 2) Confirm abort successful (Status=Canceled) 3) Upgrade nodepool image: az aks nodepool upgrade --node-image-only --cluster-name <cluster> -g <rg> --name <pool> to reinstall vmssCSE
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/vmssCSE deleted

symptom: AKS upgrade or update fails with FailedToWaitForOverlayExtensionConfigCleanup. The agic-overlay-extension-config OEC resource is stuck in deletion in kube-system namespace with dnc-operations finalizer.
rootCause: On Azure CNI Overlay clusters with AGIC/AGC, DNC-RC cleanup failed (e.g. missing Cosmos DB SubnetTokenMap entry), leaving the finalizers.acn.azure.com/dnc-operations finalizer on the OEC resource and blocking control plane operations.
solution: 1) Verify cluster uses overlay networking and agic-overlay-extension-config is stuck in deletion with dnc-operations finalizer. 2) Confirm no pending DNC cleanup activity via Kusto. 3) kubectl edit oec agic-overlay-extension-config -n kube-system and remove the finalizers block. 4) OEC auto-deletes, then reconcile the cluster.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/AKS Upgrade FailedToWaitForOverlayExtensionConfigCleanup

symptom: AKS cluster enters Failed state with errors referencing Log Analytics workspace: ResourceGroupNotFound, AddContainerInsightsSolutionError, or MMAExtension failure with exit code 53.
rootCause: The Log Analytics workspace or its resource group referenced by the monitoring addon was deleted, moved, or misconfigured.
solution: Disable the monitoring addon via Azure CLI (az aks disable-addons -a monitoring), then re-enable it through Azure Portal pointing to a valid workspace.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster operations fail with SubnetIsFull error: subnet does not have enough capacity for required IP addresses.
rootCause: The subnet assigned to the AKS cluster has insufficient available IP addresses for the requested node scaling or upgrade operation.
solution: Either reduce node count to free address space, or create a temporary nodepool in a different subnet, migrate workloads, then recreate the original nodepool with proper subnet sizing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster operations fail with ScopeLocked error: cannot perform delete/write operations because of resource locks on the MC_ resource group.
rootCause: Resource locks (CanNotDelete or ReadOnly) applied to the AKS managed resource group (MC_*) or associated resources prevent AKS from modifying underlying IaaS resources.
solution: Remove the corresponding resource locks from the MC_ resource group and associated resources, then retry the AKS operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster operations fail with RequestDisallowedByPolicy: Azure Policy blocks resource creation or modification required by AKS managed resources.
rootCause: Azure Policy assignments enforce tag, naming, or configuration requirements that conflict with AKS-managed resources in the MC_ resource group.
solution: Locate the blocking policy assignments via error details, then create a policy exemption for the MC_ resource group or adjust policy rules to exclude AKS-managed resources.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster update/upgrade fails with CreateOrUpdateVirtualNetworkLinkFailed: 'A virtual network cannot be linked to multiple zones with overlapping namespaces' for privatelink.<region>.azmk8s.io zones.
rootCause: Customer created a private DNS zone with the same name as the AKS private DNS zone in a different subscription and linked it to the same VNet, causing ambiguous DNS resolution.
solution: Remove the VNet link between the AKS VNet and the duplicate private DNS zone in the wrong subscription, then reconcile the cluster.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Create or update virtual network link failure

symptom: AKS upgrade fails with CreateVMSSAgentPoolFailed: 'Cannot complete the operation since the provided disk size N is lesser than the existing disk size M' due to OS disk size discrepancy.
rootCause: Customer manually resized VMSS OS disks at the IaaS level outside the AKS API, causing mismatch between the AKS control plane expected disk size and the actual VMSS configuration.
solution: Identify affected node pools by running per-nodepool upgrades. Delete and recreate them (same name) with the correct OS disk size via az CLI, then re-run the AKS upgrade.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Disk size mismatch causes upgrade failures

symptom: Unable to update or delete AKS cluster due to EtagMismatch error: 'Operation is not allowed: Another operation is in progress' with subcode PutManagedClusterAndComponents_FailedPrecondition.
rootCause: A concurrent or previous AKS operation left the cluster in an inconsistent state, causing etag mismatch between the client request and the current resource version.
solution: Reconcile the cluster: 1) Customer runs 'az resource update --ids <AKS resource id>'. 2) CSS can reconcile from Geneva. 3) If persists, file IcM to AKS PG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/EtagMismatch error when updating or deleting AKS cluster

symptom: After AKS upgrade, pods schedule on unintended node pools. Manually applied node taints are lost, causing workloads to land on system or shared pools with no explicit errors.
rootCause: AKS upgrades recreate nodes. Taints applied manually at the node level (kubectl taint) are not persisted through node recreation; only node pool-level taints survive.
solution: Configure taints at the node pool level: 'az aks nodepool update --node-taints key=value:NoSchedule'. Combine with nodeSelector/nodeAffinity and tolerations for deterministic scheduling.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Lost Taint After Upgrade

symptom: After AKS upgrade, pods schedule onto system or untainted node pools unexpectedly despite having tolerations configured. No errors, but scheduling behavior changes.
rootCause: Pods configured with tolerations only without nodeSelector or nodeAffinity. Tolerations only ALLOW scheduling on tainted nodes but do NOT force or prefer it; the scheduler may place pods on any eligible node.
solution: Use both tolerations AND nodeSelector/nodeAffinity. Add nodeSelector: {agentpool: <target-pool>} to pod spec alongside toleration to ensure deterministic scheduling.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Misunderstanding tolerations behavior

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade or nodepool scaling fails with PodDrainFailure error: Drain did not complete pods within 10m0s on vm. Check Pod Disruption Budget. ScaleVMSSAgentPoolFailed error code.
rootCause: Pod Disruption Budget (PDB) with AllowedDisruptions=0 prevents pods from draining off nodes during upgrade. If deployment replica count <= minAvailable in PDB, draining a single node violates the PDB constraint.
solution: Option 1: Back up PDB (kubectl get pdb -n <ns> -o yaml > backup.yaml), delete PDB, complete upgrade, restore PDB. Option 2: Scale up deployment/statefulset replicas so PDB allows disruptions during drain. After upgrade, scale back. Restart upgrade via original workflow or Jarvis reconcile.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Pod Disruption Budgets cause nodepool operations to fail

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade/scale/nodepool operations fail with ScopeLocked error: The scope cannot perform delete operation because following scope(s) are locked. ResourceOperationFailure with UpgradeFailed.
rootCause: A resource lock (CanNotDelete or ReadOnly) exists on the MC_ node resource group or individual infrastructure resources, preventing AKS RP from modifying resources during operations.
solution: Remove the resource lock from the MC_ resource group or individual locked resources via Azure Portal, CLI (az lock delete), or PowerShell. Then retry the failed operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Resource locks block cluster operations
