symptom: AKS update/upgrade operations fail with Unable to get log analytics workspace info. ResourceGroupNotFound or 404 error referencing the monitoring Log Analytics workspace.
rootCause: Customer deleted the Log Analytics Workspace or its resource group without first disabling the AKS monitoring addon (omsagent). AKS control plane tries to access the workspace during operations and fails.
solution: Mitigation 1 (deleted <14 days): Recover workspace using New-AzOperationalInsightsWorkspace with same name/region/RG. Mitigation 2 (>14 days): Disable monitoring addon: az aks disable-addons -a monitoring. Mitigation 3: Recreate the missing LA workspace from Portal, then retry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Upgrade or Scale failed due to Log Analytics Workspace not found

symptom: Microsoft.ContainerService Resource Provider stuck in 'Registering' state for >30 minutes; 'az provider list' shows sustained Registering status preventing new cluster deployment
rootCause: RP failing to respond to registration request due to high load, internal errors, code changes, or ARM/RP regional issues; verifiable via ARMProd Kusto logs filtering by correlationID and register+containerservice keywords
solution: Unregister and restart the Resource Provider registration via Azure Portal. Use ARMProd Kusto queries (Requests/HttpOutgoingRequests, Traces/Errors tables) with correlationID and register+containerservice filters to diagnose root cause
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Resource Provider registration stuck

symptom: Cluster deletion fails with 'ResourceGroupDeletionBlocked' - MC_ resource group cannot be deleted because Microsoft.RecoveryServices vault keeps recreating itself during cluster deletion
rootCause: Azure Backup/ASR vault dependency on the MC_ resource group: vault auto-recreates during deletion, blocking the managed resource group from being removed
solution: Retry vault deletion manually; if vault continues recreating, open collab with ASR team (support area: Azure/Azure Backup/Vault delete and move) to remove vault dependency, then retry cluster deletion
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/ResourceGroupDeletionBlocked: Vault Recreating During Cluster Deletion

symptom: Node pool creation fails with: OSSKU='Ubuntu2404' is invalid, details: Selected kubernetes version is not supported for Ubuntu2404, use at least 1.32.0
rootCause: Ubuntu2404 osSku requires minimum Kubernetes 1.32.0; using it with older k8s versions is not supported
solution: Upgrade cluster Kubernetes version to at least 1.32.0 before using Ubuntu2404 osSku, or use Ubuntu/Ubuntu2204 osSku for Kubernetes versions below 1.32
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Ubuntu

symptom: Node pool create/update fails with InvalidOSSKU: FIPS mode is not supported with ossku=Ubuntu2404 or Selected VM size is not supported with ossku=Ubuntu2404 (CVM+Ubuntu2404)
rootCause: Ubuntu2404 has feature compatibility restrictions: FIPS not supported (requires Ubuntu 22.04 or below); CVM+Ubuntu2404 requires k8s 1.33+; Ubuntu2404 cannot be combined with FIPS or certain Arm64/Trusted Launch+CVM configurations
solution: For FIPS workloads: use Ubuntu/Ubuntu2204 osSku (FIPS for Ubuntu2404 not yet supported). For CVM+Ubuntu2404: ensure k8s >= 1.33. Check OS SKU compatibility matrix before combining osSku with special features
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Ubuntu

symptom: Pod fails to start on newly provisioned GPU node with: rpc error: code = Unavailable desc = connection error: transport: Error while dialing: dial unix /run/containerd/containerd.sock: connect: connection refused - but recovers after first restart
rootCause: New GPU node transitions to ready/schedulable state before full initialization; pod (e.g. GitLab CI runner) is scheduled and attempts to pull container image before containerd runtime is ready, causing CrashLoopBackOff on first attempt
solution: 1) Taint GPU node pool and add tolerations/nodeSelectors for GPU-requiring pods only. 2) Add retry logic to CI job definitions. 3) Add pre-check script waiting for containerd socket availability. 4) Use DaemonSet to pre-pull large images on GPU node startup
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Containerd socket errors on GPU nodes

symptom: Node Problem Detector (NPD) reports GPU conditions (GpuCount) on AKS nodes; pods fail scheduling due to missing/incorrect GPU resource counts; nvidia-smi reports Failed to initialize NVML: Unknown Error or shows fewer GPUs than expected
rootCause: GPU driver issues (incorrect, corrupted, or outdated drivers preventing proper GPU detection), GPU hardware malfunction, or NPD misconfiguration leading to incorrect GPU condition detection/reporting
solution: 1) Identify affected nodes via KubeNodeStatusCondition Kusto query filtering by GpuCount condition. 2) Run nvidia-smi (or rocm-smi for AMD) on the node to check driver status. 3) Review /var/log/azure/cluster-provision.log and dmesg | grep -i nvidia for driver errors. 4) Reboot for transient issues; reimage/delete node for persistent driver/hardware problems
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/GPU Node Problem Detector

symptom: Azure portal cannot list ACR container images when deploying AKS 'Starter Application' — CORS error 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin header not present' and HTTP 400 from registry endpoint. Only affects users with large Azure AD group memberships.
rootCause: OAuth2 bearer tokens for users with many Azure AD group memberships exceed 8192 bytes (max HTTP header size accepted by ACR endpoints). The oversized token causes the registry to reject the request without returning CORS headers, so the browser blocks the response. Users below the 'overage' threshold but with many groups are specifically affected.
solution: 1) Permanent fix deployed by PG: Azure portal updated to remove Authorization header from browser HTTP request, relying solely on auth token in request body for CORS authorization. 2) Temporary workaround: affected users can use Azure CLI or ARM templates for deployments (no CORS involved). 3) PG can provide temporary portal 'flight' link for affected users as interim workaround. 4) Verify by capturing HAR logs and browser console output to confirm CORS errors and HTTP 400.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/ACR CORS Policy Blocked Portal Issue

symptom: Retina Capture pod fails to start with ImagePullBackOff. kubectl retina plugin creates Capture pods from MCR image matching the plugin version, but the image cannot be found.
rootCause: kubectl retina plugin was built from a local development environment. The MCR image matching that local build version does not exist in the container registry.
solution: Use an officially released kubectl retina plugin version that has a corresponding MCR image. Or manually specify a valid image in the capture spec.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Monitoring/Network Observability (Kappie)/Capture TSG

symptom: Creating a Retina network capture on a Windows node fails with error when another capture is already pending or running on that node.
rootCause: Retina Capture uses netsh trace on Windows. Only one netsh trace session is allowed per Windows node. A pending or running capture pod already occupies the session.
solution: 1) List capture pods on the Windows node: kubectl get pod -A -o wide | grep <WindowsNodeName>. 2) Get capture name: kubectl describe pod <capturePod> | grep capture-name | awk -F '=' '{print $2}'. 3) Delete existing capture: kubectl retina capture delete --namespace <ns> --name <captureName>. Then retry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Monitoring/Network Observability (Kappie)/Capture TSG

symptom: kubectl get containernetworkmetric fails with 'resource not found'. CRD creation fails with schema validation errors. Dynamic metrics feature not working on AKS Cilium cluster.
rootCause: ContainerNetworkMetric CRD not installed. Possible causes: AdvancedNetworkingDynamicMetricsPreview feature flag not enabled, cilium-operator not running, or incorrect CRD schema (must use apiVersion acn.azure.com/v1alpha1).
solution: 1) Verify CRD: kubectl get crd | grep containernetworkmetric. 2) Check cilium-operator: kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l name=cilium-operator. 3) Verify feature flag enabled for subscription. 4) Use correct CRD schema with acn.azure.com/v1alpha1. Prerequisites: K8s >= 1.32, Cilium dataplane, retina-operator >= 0.1.12.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Monitoring/Network Observability (Kappie)/Container Network Metrics Filtering-Dynamic Metrics

symptom: ContainerNetworkMetric CR created successfully but cilium-dynamic-metrics-config ConfigMap is not updated. CR shows CONFIGURED state but no metrics generated.
rootCause: Reconciliation timing delay (normal up to 2 minutes), retina-crd-operator processing errors, or ConfigMap RBAC permission issues preventing cilium-operator from updating the ConfigMap.
solution: 1) Check CR status: kubectl describe containernetworkmetric <name>. 2) Check ConfigMap: kubectl get cm cilium-dynamic-metrics-config -n kube-system -o yaml. 3) Check operator logs: kubectl logs deployment/cilium-operator -n kube-system | grep containernetworkmetric. 4) Wait 2 min for reconciliation. 5) If stuck: kubectl rollout restart deployment/cilium-operator -n kube-system.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Monitoring/Network Observability (Kappie)/Container Network Metrics Filtering-Dynamic Metrics

symptom: Dynamic metrics ConfigMap (cilium-dynamic-metrics-config) updated correctly and ContainerNetworkMetric CR shows CONFIGURED, but no Hubble metrics appear at expected endpoints.
rootCause: Cilium pods have not reloaded the new dynamic metrics configuration. Possible causes: incorrect filter configuration syntax, Cilium agent not healthy, or no network traffic matching the configured filters.
solution: 1) Restart Cilium: kubectl rollout restart daemonset/cilium -n kube-system. 2) Verify filter syntax matches schema. 3) Generate traffic matching filters. 4) Check cilium-config for hubble-dynamic-metrics-config-path. 5) Verify metrics endpoints accessible.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Monitoring/Network Observability (Kappie)/Container Network Metrics Filtering-Dynamic Metrics

symptom: Retina/Network Observability pod logs show 'unsupported value type' error, e.g. eth0LinkError='unsupported value type'. Customer reports error logs from network observability components.
rootCause: Known non-issue. The 'unsupported value type' log message is caused by a Go code library limitation in the logger. It does not affect functionality.
solution: Inform customer this is a known cosmetic log issue and NOT an actual error. No action required. The Retina team is working on fixing the log message.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Monitoring/Network Observability (Kappie)/Errors

symptom: Private AKS cluster: Container Insights shows no logs/metrics. AMA pods (ama-logs, ama-metrics) show connection timeout, 403 Forbidden (public network access disabled), or DNS resolution failures for *.ods.opinsights.azure.com / *.monitor.azure.com. nslookup returns public IP or NXDOMAIN.
rootCause: Azure Monitor Private Link Scope (AMPLS) is missing or misconfigured. Private AKS clusters cannot reach Azure Monitor public endpoints. Without AMPLS: no private endpoints created, DNS cannot resolve privatelink endpoints, public network access rejected, and data plane Private Link ID validation fails.
solution: 1) Validate DNS inside AKS node: nslookup <workspaceId>.ods.opinsights.azure.com (should return private IP). 2) Check AMPLS resource mapping: add Log Analytics Workspace, Application Insights, DCE, Managed Prometheus. 3) Validate Private Endpoint status=Approved. 4) Ensure Private DNS zones (privatelink.monitor.azure.com, privatelink.ods.opinsights.azure.com) linked to AKS VNet. 5) For custom DNS add forwarders to 168.63.129.16. 6) Set workspace to Private Only. 7) Restart: kubectl rollout restart ds/ama-logs ds/ama-metrics -n kube-system.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Monitoring/Private AKS Azure Monitor Logging Not Working Without AMPLS

symptom: PromQL query using apiserver metrics suddenly becomes consistently high and noisy (spiky output). The distortion persists for days even after the triggering event (e.g., konnectivity failure or apiserver latency spike) resolves within hours.
rootCause: Customer configured Prometheus to scrape apiserver directly (not using AKS Control Plane Metrics feature). Due to load balancing, consecutive scrape requests may hit different apiserver pods. Since each pod maintains independent counters, rate() calculations between samples from different pods produce wildly incorrect values.
solution: Recommend customer switch to AKS Control Plane Metrics feature (https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/aks/control-plane-metrics-monitor) instead of scraping apiserver directly. If customer must scrape directly, use 'by (pod)' in PromQL to separate per-pod rates.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Monitoring/prometheus distorted output when scraping apiserver

symptom: Pod stuck in CrashLoopBackOff with 'exec format error' in logs. Binary file on disk is incomplete or corrupted.
rootCause: Containerd bug (github.com/containerd/containerd/issues/5854) causes incomplete image layer writes during pull. Affects containerd <1.7.14 or >=1.7.14 without image_pull_with_sync_fs enabled.
solution: 1) Verify with 'crictl info | grep imagepullwithsyncfs'. 2) Enable image_pull_with_sync_fs by deploying a DaemonSet that modifies /etc/containerd/config.toml and restarts containerd. 3) Quick temp fix: delete corrupted image and redeploy pods to re-pull.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Others/Pod General Investigation/Pod failed to start with error exec format error

symptom: Pods stuck in Pending state. kubectl describe shows 'Insufficient memory' or 'Insufficient cpu' even though node appears to have resources available.
rootCause: AKS reserves a flat amount of CPU and regressive percentage of memory for kubelet/OS processes. This non-configurable reservation reduces allocatable resources below what 'kubectl top nodes' might suggest.
solution: 1) Run 'kubectl describe node' to check Allocatable vs Allocated resources. 2) Calculate usable = Allocatable - Allocated. 3) If insufficient, reduce pod resource requests or add nodes to the pool. Use kube-scheduler Kusto query (ControlPlaneEvents, category=='kube-scheduler') to confirm scheduling failure reasons.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Others/Pod General Investigation/Pods Stuck in Pending State due to resource limits

symptom: Pods stuck in Pending state. Scheduler error shows 'Too many pods' for nodes. Especially common with Azure CNI clusters (default maxPods=30).
rootCause: MaxPodsPerNode limit reached. Azure CNI default is 30 pods/node (vs kubenet default 110). All IP addresses on the node are exhausted, preventing new pod scheduling.
solution: 1) Check maxPods value via ASC Agent Pool Profile or 'az aks nodepool show'. 2) Count current pods per node with 'kubectl get po -A -o wide'. 3) If limit hit: scale out by adding nodes, enable cluster autoscaler, or create new node pool with higher maxPods value at creation time.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Others/Pod General Investigation/Pods stuck in Pending due to max pod limit

symptom: Windows container pods restarting with exit code -1073741510 (0xC000013A / STATUS_CONTROL_C_EXIT). Logs show 'There is not enough space on the disk' errors from kubeproxy and svchost.
rootCause: Disk space exhaustion on Windows node. Exit code 0xC000013A indicates forced program termination. The node OS disk runs out of space causing container and system service failures.
solution: 1) Check disk usage via Azure Portal Metrics (OS Disk Used Percentage). 2) Use 'kubectl describe pod' to identify affected node. 3) Get CSI driver logs. 4) Customer should check/update Windows microservice code, Helm chart configs. 5) If deeper investigation needed, escalate to AKS Windows Containers team via ICM and collect logs with collect-windows-logs.ps1.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Others/Pod General Investigation/Windows Containers Pod Restarts Referencing exit code 073741510

symptom: AKS extension creation (e.g., backup) fails with RequestDisallowedByPolicy targeting extension-pls. ARM activity log shows Forbidden for Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration/privateLinkScopes/write.
rootCause: Customer Azure Policy denies creation of privateLinkScopes resource in MC_ resource group. When enabling AKS extensions, an extension-pls privateLinkScopes object is automatically created and gets blocked by deny policy.
solution: Two options: 1) Add policy exception/exclusion for AKS MC_ resource group to allow Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration/privateLinkScopes. 2) Disable/modify the deny policy. Verify with ARM Kusto query filtering EventServiceEntries by subscriptionId and RequestDisallowedByPolicy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Others/RequestDisallowedByPolicy_extension pls

symptom: Azure Pipelines deployment to AKS fails with: services is forbidden: User system:serviceaccount:default:*** cannot get resource services in API group in the namespace.
rootCause: Kubernetes ServiceAccount used by Azure DevOps service connection lacks RBAC permissions (Role/ClusterRole + RoleBinding/ClusterRoleBinding) in the target namespace.
solution: 1) Check current permissions: kubectl auth can-i --list --as system:serviceaccount:<ns>:<sa>. 2) Create ClusterRole+ClusterRoleBinding (cross-namespace) or Role+RoleBinding (single namespace). 3) Apply with kubectl apply -f. 4) Recreate Azure DevOps service connection.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Others/Role and RoleBindings for Azure DevOps

symptom: AKS Service Connections (Storage Account, Key Vault) stuck in Failed/Accepted state. az aks connection show returns InvalidResourceOperation with state Accepted. sc-job pod shows ErrImagePull.
rootCause: Service Connection extension (sc-extension) version 0.1.0 references old container images (scaksextension.azurecr.io/prod/image/sc-operator:20250417.1) that no longer exist in registry. The sc-job responsible for provisioning cannot pull the image.
solution: 1) Confirm version: az k8s-extension show --name sc-extension --query currentVersion. 2) Enable auto-upgrade: az k8s-extension update --name sc-extension --auto-upgrade true. 3) Delete stuck service connections. 4) Verify sc-job deleted: kubectl get job -n sc-system. 5) Recreate and verify provisioningState becomes Succeeded.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Others/Service Connectors Troubleshooting

symptom: AKS node becomes unresponsive minutes after startup. CPU reaches 100% for 3-4 hours then self-resolves. Node reports Ready but pods are stuck. Syslog shows tasks blocked >120s waiting for rtnl lock.
rootCause: Netlink (rtnl) lock contention caused by custom node-exporter DaemonSet. node-exporter acquires the global kernel rtnl lock then gets CPU throttled, holding the lock and blocking all other processes needing it.
solution: 1) Disable unnecessary collectors: --no-collector.wifi, --no-collector.hwmon, --no-collector.arp.netlink. 2) Exclude virtual interfaces: --collector.netclass.ignored-devices and --collector.netdev.device-exclude. 3) Increase node-exporter CPU limit to at least 1000m to prevent throttling.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Others/The node becomes unresponsive due to sudden CPU spikes without an apparent cause

symptom: AKS virtual node 创建失败: error creating provider: error setting up network: unable to delegate subnet to Azure Container Instance since it references the route table
rootCause: ACI (virtual node) 子网不支持 User-Defined Routing (UDR)。aci-connector 会校验并阻止使用关联了路由表的子网进行委派
solution: 从 ACI virtual node 使用的子网中移除 User-Defined Routing (路由表)。ACI 服务不支持子网上的 UDR
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/error setting up network - AKS virtual node

symptom: Image pulling from ACR fails or takes over an hour for large container images (>20GB). containerd logs show 'cancel pulling image because of no progress in 1m0s'. kubectl describe shows Failed pull with ErrImagePull/ImagePullBackOff. A misleading 401 Unauthorized error may also appear (red herring from empty credential retry).
rootCause: The VM SKU used for AKS nodes has insufficient IOPS for pulling large images. For example, Standard_NC4as_T4_v3 has only 4000 IOPS while actual IO demand exceeds this limit, causing IO throttling. containerd imagePullProgressTimeout (default 1m) cancels pulls with no progress. After first pull fails, AKS retries with empty credentials resulting in misleading 401 error.
solution: Replace the AKS node VM SKU with one that has higher IOPS (e.g., upgrade from Standard_NC4as_T4_v3 to Standard_NC8as_T4_v3). Check IO throttling in ASI: Host Analyser > VM Counter > Throttling. For VM SKUs without documented IOPS, query Kusto azurevmcentral cluster to check NetworkDiskIOPS values.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Others/Troubleshooting image pull with huge image

symptom: VPA addon enabled but recommendations not applied to pods. VPA admission controller logs show TLS handshake errors: 'http: TLS handshake error from <IP>: EOF'. Recommender generates correct recommendations, audit logs confirm pod creation requests sent to webhook, but pod specs remain unchanged.
rootCause: VPA objects configured with updatePolicy.updateMode set to 'Off'. In this mode VPA only produces recommendations but does not apply them. TLS handshake errors are benign - from konnectivity-agent pod IPs, normal connection resets, not blocking webhook functionality.
solution: Check VPA updateMode: 'kubectl get vpa -A -o yaml | grep -i updateMode'. If 'Off', change to 'Auto' (mutate running pods) or 'Initial' (apply at pod creation) via 'kubectl edit vpa <name>'. To reset: 'az aks disable-addons --addons vertical-pod-autoscaler' then re-enable.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Others/VPA Certificate issues AdmissionPod

symptom: Pod stuck in ContainerCreating with error: 'MountVolume.SetUp failed for volume kube-api-access: write /var/lib/kubelet/pods/.../ca.crt: no space left on device'. Node disk usage (df -h), inode usage (df -i), and kubelet directory sizes all appear normal.
rootCause: Memory resource limits in pod spec specified without unit suffix (e.g., memory: '3' instead of '3Gi'). Without units, Kubernetes interprets the value as bytes rather than gigabytes, leading to extremely low memory allocation that causes disk-related mount failures.
solution: Add proper unit suffix to memory resource limits/requests in pod YAML. Change 'memory: "3"' to 'memory: "3Gi"' and 'memory: "2"' to 'memory: "2Gi"'. Redeploy pod to verify fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Others/no space on device

symptom: Users unable to authenticate to AAD-integrated AKS cluster. Error: 'Failed to acquire a token: waiting for device code authentication to complete: autorest/adal/devicetoken: Error while retrieving OAuth token: Unknown Error' when using kubectl.
rootCause: Conditional access policy requires MFA for all devices. This blocks applications and build environments (Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions) where MFA interactive prompt is not possible.
solution: Update the conditional access policy to exclude impacted users, applications, or platforms from MFA requirements. Alternatively, adjust conditions to exclude specific devices/platforms used to access the AKS cluster.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/AAD integrated with AKS -   MFA enabled

symptom: AGIC 场景下通过 Application Gateway 访问后端应用返回 HTTP 404 Not Found，请求路径与后端应用期望路径不匹配
rootCause: Application Gateway 将请求转发到后端时使用了客户端原始路径，但后端应用期望在不同的路径上接收请求
solution: 在 ingress resource 上添加 appgw.ingress.kubernetes.io/backend-path-prefix 注解，将外部请求路径重写为后端应用实际期望的路径。也可修改应用配置使其在请求的路径上监听，或修改客户端请求 URL
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting 404 Not Found

symptom: Enabling AAD on AKS cluster fails: 'az aks update --enable-aad --enable-azure-rbac' returns '(RBACNotEnabledForAAD) RBAC must be enabled for AAD to be enabled.'
rootCause: AKS cluster was created with RBAC disabled. RBAC cannot be enabled on an existing non-RBAC cluster. AAD integration requires RBAC as prerequisite - this is a fundamental platform limitation.
solution: Create a new AKS cluster with RBAC enabled and migrate workloads. Verify RBAC status: 'az aks show -g <rg> -n <cluster> -o tsv --query enableRbac'. No way to enable RBAC on existing non-RBAC cluster.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/AAD/AKS RBAC Enablement Limitation

symptom: AGIC 场景下通过 Application Gateway 访问后端应用返回 HTTP 502 Bad Gateway，特别是在 K8s 工作负载滚动更新期间频繁出现
rootCause: Application Gateway 后端池中没有健康的后端成员。在 K8s rolling update 期间旧 Pod 已终止但新 Pod 尚未就绪时，会短暂出现无可用后端的情况
solution: 1. 检查 AppGW Backend Health 确认后端健康状态。2. 如果 502 在 rolling update 期间频发，按照 AGIC 'Minimizing Downtime During Deployments' 文档配置 preStop hook、readinessProbe 和 minReadySeconds 来减少部署期间的停机时间
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting 502 Bad Gateway

symptom: Backup jobs fail with identity assignment errors after AAD Pod Identity addon removed/disabled. nmi pods still running in kube-system. Authentication failures and timeouts when pods access Azure resources.
rootCause: Residual nmi DaemonSet from deprecated AAD Pod Identity addon (deprecated Oct 2023, removed after March 2024) remains in cluster. Leftover nmi intercepts traffic to Azure IMDS, causing identity assignment failures for other pods.
solution: 1) Check addon: 'az aks show --query addonProfiles.azurePodIdentity'. 2) Check residual nmi: 'kubectl get daemonset -n kube-system | grep nmi'. 3) Install aks-preview extension. 4) Disable: 'az aks update -g <rg> -n <cluster> --disable-pod-identity'. Migrate to Azure Workload Identity.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/AAD/Residual nmi daemonset after aad removal

symptom: AGIC 场景下通过 Application Gateway 访问后端应用返回 HTTP 504 Gateway Timeout，响应时间与配置的 request-timeout 一致
rootCause: Application Gateway 的 request timeout（默认 30s）过短，后端应用处理请求耗时超过该超时值
solution: 在 ingress resource 上设置 appgw.ingress.kubernetes.io/request-timeout 注解增大超时值（单位秒）。通过 kubectl describe ingress 确认当前值，在 Azure Portal AppGW Backend Settings 中验证生效。如果 504 时间与 timeout 不一致，需排查后端应用本身问题
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting 504 Gateway Timeout

symptom: Running 'az aks get-credentials' fails with: 'The client does not have authorization to perform action Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters/listClusterUserCredential/action'. Cluster is AAD-integrated and RBAC-enabled.
rootCause: User lacks necessary Azure IAM role assignment to retrieve AKS cluster credentials. The listClusterUserCredential/action permission is required but not assigned.
solution: Assign user 'Azure Kubernetes Service Cluster User Role' or 'Cluster Admin Role'. Alternative: Owner, Contributor, or custom role with 'Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters/listClusterUserCredential/action' or 'listClusterAdminCredentials/action'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/AAD/Unable to retrieve cluster credentials

symptom: AGIC pod 日志显示 ErrorGetApplicationGatewayError: Failed fetching configuration for Application Gateway，伴随 dial timeout 错误和 Retrying in 10s
rootCause: AGIC pod 无法通过 TCP 443 连接到 Azure Resource Manager (management.azure.com)。通常由 AKS 集群出站流量限制导致：UDR 指向 NVA/Azure Firewall 但未放行 ARM 端点、NSG 阻止了 AzureResourceManager service tag、或 Network Policy 阻止了 AGIC pod 的出站流量
solution: 1. 如 outbound type 为 UDR，检查 NVA/Firewall 规则是否允许到 management.azure.com:443 的流量。2. 如 outbound type 为 LoadBalancer，检查子网路由表是否有 0.0.0.0/0 → NVA 的路由。3. 检查 NSG 出站规则是否阻止了 AzureResourceManager service tag。4. 如启用了 Network Policy，检查是否有策略阻止 AGIC pod 出站到 ARM IP 范围
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AGIC pod 日志显示 AuthorizationFailed 错误，AGIC 无法获取或更新 Application Gateway 配置
rootCause: AGIC 使用的 Managed Identity 缺少必要的 RBAC 角色分配。需要 Contributor 角色在 Application Gateway 资源上，Reader 角色在 Application Gateway 所在资源组上
solution: 为 AGIC identity 添加角色分配：1. 在 Application Gateway 上分配 Contributor 角色。2. 在 Application Gateway 所在 Resource Group 上分配 Reader 角色。对于 AGIC addon 启用的集群，managed identity 名称格式为 ingressapplicationgateway-<CLUSTER_NAME>
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues
Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/Multiple user assigned identities error with AKS extensions
symptom: AKS Backup extension pods (dataprotection-microsoft-geneva-service) constantly restart with error 'Multiple user assigned identities exist, please specify the clientId/resourceId of the identity in the token request' from IMDS. Addon-token-adapter logs show 'cannot initialize iptables table nat: Permission denied'.
rootCause: Azure Policy or Kyverno drops NET_RAW capability from dataprotection-microsoft-geneva-service pods. Without NET_RAW the addon-token-adapter sidecar cannot modify iptables to intercept IMDS traffic. Requests hit regular IMDS which fails because VMSS has multiple user-assigned identities and no specific clientId is specified.
solution: Exclude dataprotection-microsoft namespace from the Azure Policy/Kyverno policy that drops NET_RAW. Kyverno: add 'dataprotection-microsoft' to spec.rules[].exclude.resources.namespaces. Azure Policy: configure exclusion per Azure Policy for Kubernetes docs.

symptom: AGIC/AppGW returns HTTP 404 Not Found when the request path does not match the backend application expected path
rootCause: The URL path in the client request does not match the path on which the backend application is listening. In a reverse proxy setup (AKS/AGIC/AppGW), the request reaches the correct backend but the app cannot find the resource at that path.
solution: Add 'appgw.ingress.kubernetes.io/backend-path-prefix' annotation to the ingress resource to rewrite the request path. Alternatively, modify the request URL or reconfigure the app to listen on the expected path.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting 404 Not Found
Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/Remediating Microsoft Defender alerts in AKS
symptom: Microsoft Defender for Cloud triggers Medium severity alerts on AKS: 'Usage of host networking and ports should be restricted' and 'Usage of pod HostPath volume mounts should be restricted'. Customer wants to remediate these security recommendations.
rootCause: Azure Policy add-on monitors AKS security posture. Non-compliant pods trigger Defender alerts because their namespaces are not excluded from security policy constraints. Constraint templates audit hostNetwork, hostPort, hostPath usage against allowed lists.
solution: Check constraints: kubectl get constraint, kubectl describe constrainttemplate. Find ASC Default policy assignment in Azure Portal (Policy > Assignments). Add customer namespace to excluded namespaces for host networking/HostPath parameters. Wait 15min-24h for policy sync.

symptom: AGIC/AppGW returns HTTP 502 Bad Gateway during Kubernetes rolling deployments
rootCause: During rolling updates, old pods are terminated before AppGW health probes detect the change, leaving no healthy backend pool members temporarily.
solution: Implement 'Minimizing Downtime During Deployments' recommendations from Azure AGIC docs (https://azure.github.io/application-gateway-kubernetes-ingress/how-tos/minimize-downtime-during-deployments/). Check backend health in Azure Portal (Monitoring > Backend health) or ASC.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting 502 Bad Gateway
Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/Repeated 401 Unauthorized Errors in Azure Key Vault (AKV)
symptom: Azure Key Vault experiences high number of 401 Unauthorized failed requests from AKS cluster, potentially disrupting services relying on AKV.
rootCause: AKV provider relies on Azure Key Vault SDKs which use a token acquisition pattern that inherently generates 401 responses before obtaining valid tokens. This is expected by-design behavior of the SDK authentication flow.
solution: No mitigation needed from AKS side - by-design behavior of Azure Key Vault SDKs. Refer customer to GitHub issue azure-sdk-for-python#31228 for detailed explanation.

symptom: AGIC/AppGW returns HTTP 504 Gateway Timeout when backend response exceeds configured timeout
rootCause: AppGW enforces a request timeout (default 30s via AGIC annotation). If the backend takes longer to respond, AppGW returns HTTP 504.
solution: Check 'appgw.ingress.kubernetes.io/request-timeout' annotation on the ingress (kubectl describe ingress). Increase timeout if backend legitimately needs more time. Verify in Azure Portal under AppGW > Backend Settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting 504 Gateway Timeout
Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/Role assignments fail with AuthorizationFailed
symptom: AKS operations fail with 403 AuthorizationFailed: client does not have authorization to perform Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments/write. Object ID belongs to AzureContainerService service principal.
rootCause: AzureContainerService first-party service principal removed from tenant or Azure tenant migration occurred. Resource provider needs re-registration in each subscription to restore the service principal.
solution: Re-register Microsoft.ContainerService: az provider unregister/register --namespace Microsoft.ContainerService. If unresolved, check AKS PG tenant migration docs. Use Kusto FrontEndContextActivity to investigate.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/Security Center alerts on invalid AppArmor profiles
symptom: Azure Security Center alerts for overridden/disabled AppArmor profiles in AKS clusters. Profiles used by nodes missing from approved list in Azure Policy.
rootCause: Azure Policy auditing AppArmor profiles is missing one or more profiles leveraged by AKS nodes. Allowed profiles list in ASC Default Security Policy incomplete.
solution: SSH to node, run 'sudo aa-status' to list profiles in use. Navigate to Security Center > Security Policy > ASC Default > Parameters. Add missing profiles to allowed list. Save to apply.

symptom: AGIC pod logs show 'AuthorizationFailed' error when trying to configure Application Gateway
rootCause: AGIC identity missing required role assignments: Contributor on AppGW resource and Reader on AppGW resource group.
solution: Create role assignments: (1) Contributor to AGIC identity on AppGW. (2) Reader to AGIC identity on AppGW RG. For addon clusters, identity name is 'ingressapplicationgateway-<CLUSTER_NAME>'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues
Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/TLS Connectivity Errors From AKS to Private Container Registries
symptom: Image pulls from private container registries with self-signed certs fail: 'x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'. ErrImagePull/ImagePullBackOff events. Curl from nodes shows 'unable to get local issuer certificate'.
rootCause: AKS nodes lack the self-signed CA certificate in trust store (/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt). Private registry self-signed cert not in default trusted CA bundle, causing TLS handshake failure.
solution: Option 1 (Preferred): Use AKS Custom Certificate Authority feature to add custom CA to all nodes' trust store. Option 2: Deploy DaemonSet to copy CA cert to nodes, run update-ca-certificates, restart containerd. Also configure ImagePullSecret for registry auth.

symptom: AGIC pod logs show 'invalid_client' error when authenticating to configure Application Gateway
rootCause: AGIC uses a Service Principal whose secret has expired.
solution: Add a new secret to the Service Principal and update AGIC config. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/application-gateway/ingress-controller-install-existing#using-a-service-principal
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues
Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Security and Identity/Troubleshooting the CustomPrivateDNSZoneMissingPermissionError error
symptom: AKS cluster create/update fails with CustomPrivateDNSZoneMissingPermissionError: identity must be given permission to read and write to custom private dns zone.
rootCause: Cluster control plane managed identity lacks 'Private DNS Zone Contributor' role on the custom Private DNS Zone resource. AKS pre-checks permissions and blocks if insufficient.
solution: Get identity principal ID from ASC Resource Explorer. Get Private DNS Zone resource ID. Create role assignment: az role assignment create --assignee <principal-id> --scope <dns-zone-id> --role 'Private DNS Zone Contributor'. Retry cluster operation.

symptom: Attaching Azure Disk >= 4TiB to AKS pod fails with FailedAttachVolume: Only Disk CachingType None is supported for disk with size greater than 4095 GB
rootCause: Default StorageClass managed-premium sets cachingmode=ReadOnly, but Azure Managed Disks >= 4TiB only support CachingType=None. The disk provisioning succeeds but the attach operation is rejected by CRP.
solution: Create a custom StorageClass with cachingmode: None (e.g. managed-premium-caching-none) and reference it in the PVC. The CSI driver in newer versions silently forces caching to None for >4095GB disks, but the PV describe may still show the original setting.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/DiskCachingInvalidForDiskSize - Only Disk CachingType 'None' is supported for disk with size greater than 4095 GB

symptom: Azure Files NFS PV mount fails with access denied by server when using a pre-created (statically provisioned) storage account
rootCause: Pre-created storage account network configuration is not set to Enabled from selected virtual networks and IP addresses with the AKS subnet authorized. The CSI driver auto-configures this for dynamically created accounts, but static provisioning requires manual network setup.
solution: 1) Set storage account Public network access to Enabled from selected virtual networks and IP addresses. 2) Add the AKS subnet to the authorized virtual networks list. 3) Wait several minutes for propagation, then redeploy pods.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/Incorrect network configuration causes NFS mount failures

symptom: PVC mount fails on Windows AKS node with New-SmbGlobalMapping: Multiple connections to a server or shared resource by the same user, using more than one user name, are not allowed (error 1219)
rootCause: SMB limitation on Windows: when two PVs referencing the same Azure Files storage account but different folder names are mounted on the same node, the second mount fails due to SMB single-session-per-server constraint.
solution: Instead of creating separate PVs for different folders in the same share, use a single PV and use subPath in the pod volumeMounts to mount different subdirectories.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/Mount failures on windows node

symptom: Azure Files PV mount fails with MountVolume.MountDevice failed / mount failed: exit status 32 in an environment with NVA or firewall in the network path
rootCause: The NVA or firewall present in the network path does not have rules allowing SMB traffic to the Azure File Share. Connection attempts to the storage account URL time out.
solution: Update firewall rules to allow SMB traffic and access to storage wildcard domains (*.file.core.windows.net). Alternatively, use Private Endpoints (requires GPv2 storage) or Service Endpoints on the AKS subnet to bypass the firewall for storage traffic.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/MountVolume.MountDevice failed for volume

symptom: Mounting Azure Files fails on FIPS-enabled AKS nodes with default Azure Files provider or default CSI driver
rootCause: Default Azure Files authentication methods (SMB with NTLM/Kerberos) conflict with FIPS encryption requirements on FIPS-enabled nodes, causing mount operations to fail.
solution: Install Azure File CSI driver (for AKS < 1.21; built-in for >= 1.21) and create a custom StorageClass with protocol: nfs. Ensure AKS node pool identity has Storage Contributor role on the target resource group. Remove manual driver installation before upgrading to 1.21+.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/Mounting Azure Files on FIPS nodes

symptom: PVC attach fails with AttachDiskWhileBeingDetached: Cannot attach data disk to VM because the disk is currently being detached or the last detach operation failed
rootCause: A previous disk detach operation on the VM/VMSS instance either has not completed or failed, leaving the disk in a limbo state that prevents new attach operations.
solution: 1) Confirm in VMSS instances that the disk is not actually attached. 2) Run az vm update (for VMs) or az vmss update-instances (for VMSS) to reconcile the VM state and clear the stale detach.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/PVC failure with error AttachDiskWhileBeingDetached

symptom: PVCs stuck in Pending after Azure Backup restore; pod error: PVC was not created for pod (pod is not owner); PVC shows waiting for pod to be scheduled
rootCause: Inline ephemeral volumes in StatefulSets create PVCs with ownerReferences pointing to the pod. Azure Backup restore recreates PVCs without the ownerReferences metadata, so pods do not recognize the restored PVCs as owned by them, causing a deadlock.
solution: Configure the Azure Backup plan to exclude ephemeral volumes: 1) Add labels (e.g. notbackup=1) to the ephemeral volumeClaimTemplate metadata in StatefulSet spec. 2) Set backup label exclusion rule. Ephemeral volumes should not be backed up since their lifecycle is tied to the pod.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/PVC in pending after restoration

symptom: Pods fail to schedule with volume node affinity conflict and pod has unbound immediate PersistentVolumeClaims; cluster autoscaler cannot resolve by scaling
rootCause: Changes to the StorageClass used for dynamic PVC provisioning (swapping to a new class or updating an existing one) altered the node affinity/selector constraints, creating a mismatch between volume topology and available node capacity.
solution: Revert the StorageClass to the original configuration, or set VolumeBindingMode to WaitForFirstConsumer so that PV provisioning is delayed until a pod is scheduled, allowing the volume to be created in the same zone as the scheduled node.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/PVC node affinity conflict resulting pods to unscheduled

symptom: Pod fails to start with volume attachment is being deleted after scaling deployment from 0 to 1 replica when using PremiumV2_LRS Azure Disk on non-zonal nodes
rootCause: PremiumV2_LRS disks only support VMs in Availability Zones. Initial attach to non-zonal VM succeeds (bug) but detach always fails with error about PremiumV2_LRS requiring AZ VMs, leaving a stale VolumeAttachment with detach error that blocks new attachments.
solution: Immediate: Delete the VolumeAttachment finalizers (kubectl edit volumeattachment and remove the finalizers section) to allow the stale attachment to be cleaned up. Long-term: Ensure PremiumV2_LRS PVs are only used with node pools in Availability Zones.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Storage/Pod fails to start with volume attachment is being deleted error

symptom: AKS cluster creation, node pool addition, scaling, start, or upgrade fails with quota exceeded error: 'Operation results in exceeding quota limits of Core' or 'would exceed the quota of N resources of type...per resource group'
rootCause: Subscription vCPU quota (Total Regional vCPUs or specific VM size family vCPUs) is insufficient for the requested AKS operation
solution: Submit quota increase via Azure portal (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/quotas/quickstart-increase-quota-portal). For large/high-demand SKUs requiring manual review, or if customer declines new request, transfer case to SAP path: Azure\Service and subscription limits (quotas)\ComputeVM (coresvCPUs) with: Subscription ID, Region, SKU Size, Restriction Type (Regional/Zonal), cores requested. Do NOT transfer to VM/VMSS team for quota issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Quota errors
Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Private Link Service for AKS Load Balancers
symptom: Private Link Service (PLS) provision fails with PrivateIPAddressInUse error when updating service with static IP. Error: IP configuration is using the same private IP address as IP configuration aks-subnet-pls-name-dynamic-0.
rootCause: Customer is trying to set a PLS static IP that is the same as an existing dynamically allocated IP on the PLS NIC.
solution: Select a different static IP address, or first release the conflicting dynamic IP (if there are multiple) and then re-specify the desired static IP.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Linux/Containerd File Descriptor Limits on Ubuntu 24 04 Nodes
symptom: Pods on AKS Ubuntu 24.04 nodes crash with Too many open files errors (java.net.SocketException or bash redirection error). Pods enter CrashLoopBackOff. ulimit -n shows 1024 on Ubuntu 24.04 vs 1048576 on Ubuntu 22.04.
rootCause: Ubuntu 24.04 updated systemd defaults set LimitNOFILESoft=1024 for containerd. Pods inherit this low limit and workloads needing many open sockets/handles exceed it.
solution: Apply a DaemonSet to create systemd override increasing LimitNOFILE and LimitNOFILESoft for containerd, reload and restart containerd. Ref: https://github.com/VijayRod/demo/blob/master/ai/kubernetes/ubuntu-nofile-soft-limits/daemonset.yaml

symptom: AKS cluster or node pool creation fails with 'SKU Not Found' or 'VM Size Not Available' error for the requested VM size
rootCause: Requested VM size is restricted at the subscription level (ASC > Resource Explorer > Subscription > RP Details > SKU Restrictions), or the VM size is not offered in the specific region/zone due to platform limitations
solution: Check SKU availability: `az vm list-skus --location <region> --size <vmsize> --all --output table`. If subscription-level restriction, create collaboration request to Azure\Service and subscription limits (quotas)\ComputeVM with: Subscription ID, Region, SKU Size, Restriction Type, cores requested. If platform limitation, advise customer to select alternative VM size or zone using Compute Capacity Advisor.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/SKU Not Found OR VM Size Not Available
Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Private Link Service for AKS Load Balancers
symptom: Private Link Service provision fails with error: reconcilePrivateLinkService for service failed: LB frontend already has unmanaged private link service.
rootCause: The LB frontend is already associated with a Private Link Service not managed by AKS, or customer accidentally modified/deleted existing PLS tags.
solution: Check the PLS tags. It should have k8s-azure-owner-service (value = owner service name) and k8s-azure-cluster-name (value = cluster name) tags. Restore them if missing.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Linux/Linux PID Exhaustion AKA pthread_create failed: Resource temporarily unavailable
symptom: AKS node becomes NotReady. /var/log/syslog shows repeated pthread_create failed: Resource temporarily unavailable from containerd and kubelet. Node rebooted by AKS remediator.
rootCause: A container exhausted all available PIDs (default ~32K). Linux cannot allocate new threads via clone syscall. Kubelet/containerd thread creation fails causing node NotReady.
solution: 1) Identify offending container via ps -e -w -o thcount,cgname or Prometheus container_threads metric. 2) Configure --pod-max-pids via AKS custom node configuration. 3) Reboot node or wait for remediator. Do NOT blindly increase pid_max.

symptom: AKS node provisioning fails; ASC Extensions tab shows vmssCSE (custom script extension) failed with error code 50 or 'ERR_OUTBOUND_CONN_FAIL'
rootCause: Outbound connectivity from the new node is blocked by NSG rules or UDR (User Defined Routes) configuration, preventing the node from reaching required endpoints during provisioning
solution: Ask customer to review NSG and UDR rules to ensure required outbound connectivity is not blocked. Reference CSE error codes in AgentBaker cse_helpers.sh: https://github.com/Azure/AgentBaker/blob/master/parts/linux/cloud-init/artifacts/cse_helpers.sh
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/VMSS Overview
Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Service Allowed IP Ranges Annotation
symptom: Service event warning: Found invalid LoadBalancerSourceRanges, ignoring and adding a default DenyAll rule in security group. Occurs when using azure-allowed-ip-ranges annotation.
rootCause: Customer provided IP ranges in invalid CIDR format (e.g., 10.0.0.0 instead of 10.0.0.0/32) in the azure-allowed-ip-ranges annotation.
solution: Correct the CIDR format in the annotation value to valid CIDR notation (e.g., 10.0.0.0/32). Do not use this annotation together with spec.loadBalancerSourceRanges as they conflict.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Monitoring/AMA Pod Crashing and Restarting
symptom: AMA pod continuously restarts with exit code 143. Pod describe shows dcr_env_var has been updated - dcr config changed.
rootCause: ama-logs container cannot connect to global.handler.control.monitor.azure.com. mdsd.err shows Could not obtain configuration. Caused by firewall blocking or DNS resolution failure.
solution: Verify nodes can reach global.handler.control.monitor.azure.com. Follow egress rules: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/outbound-rules-control-egress#azure-monitor-for-containers. Check firewall and DNS. Retrieve AMA logs via kubectl cp for confirmation.

symptom: AKS GPU node provisioning fails; ASC Extensions tab shows vmssCSE failed with error code 85 or 'ERR_GPU_DRIVERS_INSTALL_TIMEOUT'
rootCause: GPU driver installation timed out during node provisioning; the installation may still be in progress and can eventually complete successfully
solution: Wait for GPU driver installation to complete - error code 85 indicates timeout but installation may succeed given enough time. Monitor node status; if it does not recover, reimage the node and allow sufficient time for GPU driver install.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/VMSS Overview
Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Trusted Access
symptom: Trusted Access partner service cannot connect to AKS cluster. ResponseFlags show dash but request is rejected with 403 by API server.
rootCause: Target AKS cluster certificate has expired, causing the API server to reject Trusted Access requests with 403 Forbidden.
solution: Follow the certificate rotation documentation (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/certificate-rotation) to rotate the cluster certificates.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Monitoring/AMA Pod Restarts with exit code 137
symptom: ama-logs-rs and ama-logs pods restart with exit code 137, NOT due to OOM. CrowdStrike Falcon pods present in cluster.
rootCause: CrowdStrike Container Drift Prevention runtime protection blocks new processes in containers, killing ama-logs processes.
solution: Advise customer to review CrowdStrike Runtime Protection - Container Drift Prevention policy. Add exception for AMA pods or adjust policy. First confirm restarts are not from node resource issues.

symptom: kubectl exec / docker exec fails with 'OCI runtime exec failed: exec failed: unable to start container process: open /dev/ptmx: no space left on device: unknown'; accessing pods or nodes with debug commands fails
rootCause: PTY (pseudo-terminal) pool exhausted on the node: /proc/sys/kernel/pty/nr has reached /proc/sys/kernel/pty/max (default 4096). Caused by processes or containers creating PTY sessions without closing them (PTY leak), often from third-party containers (e.g., aqua-agent)
solution: (1) Temporarily increase PTY limit: `az vmss run-command invoke --resource-group <rg> --name <vmss> --command-id RunShellScript --instance-id <id> --scripts 'sysctl -w kernel.pty.max=8192'`. (2) Use node-shell to access the node. (3) Run `lsof > lsof.txt && ps axjf > ps.txt`. (4) Identify leaking processes: `grep -E '/ptmx|/pts/' lsof.txt | awk '{print $1, $2}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr`. (5) Map container IDs to pods with `crictl pods`. (6) If third-party pods are responsible, contact the vendor for a fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Virtual Machine TSGs/Node PTY
Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Trusted Access
symptom: Trusted Access partner service receives 400 error when connecting to AKS cluster. Dataplane returns HTTP 400 with reason authz_stopped.
rootCause: Target AKS cluster is in stopped state. Trusted Access dataplane rejects connections to stopped clusters with 400.
solution: Start the AKS cluster before attempting Trusted Access connection. Use az aks start to start the cluster.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Monitoring/Data missing from insights tab
symptom: AKS Insights page shows No data for selected filters. Container Insights dashboards empty.
rootCause: ama-logs pods cannot send data to Log Analytics workspace. Causes: firewall blocking outbound, incorrect RBAC on AKS identity, or ama-logs DaemonSet not running.
solution: 1) Verify ama-logs running: kubectl get ds ama-logs -n kube-system. 2) Check AKS identity Contributor role on LA workspace RG. 3) Test connectivity: nslookup/curl <workspace_id>.oms.opinsights.azure.com:443. 4) Verify egress rules for Azure Monitor.

symptom: AKS nodes report NodeNotReady status despite no memory/disk/network/kubelet resource pressure. Node kubelet logs show: 'Failed to ensure lease exists, will retry' with error 'unable to create new content in namespace kube-node-lease because it is being terminated'
rootCause: The kube-node-lease namespace is stuck in Terminating state due to a finalizer (e.g., Rancher controller.cattle.io/namespace-auth) blocking namespace deletion. This prevents kubelet from updating its heartbeat lease, causing the control plane to mark nodes as NotReady.
solution: (1) Inspect namespace: `kubectl describe namespace kube-node-lease`. (2) Remove blocking finalizers: `kubectl patch namespace kube-node-lease -p '{"metadata":{"finalizers":[]}}' --type=merge`. (3) Verify namespace returns to Active status and nodes return to Ready. Note: kube-node-lease namespace is expected to remain Active in AKS; if accidentally deleted it is auto-recreated.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Virtual Machine TSGs/NodeNotReady Kube node lease namespace
Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Trusted Launch
symptom: AKS node creation times out. Logs show: The kernel module failed to load. Secure boot is enabled on this system, so this is likely because it was not signed by a key that is trusted by the kernel.
rootCause: Unsigned kernel module (e.g., GPU driver) cannot be loaded when Secure Boot is enabled on Trusted Launch nodes. CSE attempts to install unsigned driver during node provisioning, causing timeout.
solution: Disable secure boot on the nodepool (az aks nodepool update --disable-secure-boot). For GPU nodepools specifically, install NVIDIA GPU Operator: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/gpu-cluster?tabs=add-ubuntu-gpu-node-pool#use-nvidia-gpu-operator-with-aks

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Monitoring/Metrics-Server VPA
symptom: High CPU throttling on metrics-server pods in AKS 1.24+. kubectl top returns errors or delayed results.
rootCause: Metrics-server VPA addon-resizer defaults (CPU=44+0.5n, Mem=51+4n MiB) use current node count, not max. During autoscaler scaling, metrics-server becomes under-provisioned.
solution: Apply ConfigMap metrics-server-config in kube-system with higher baseCPU/baseMemory (e.g. 100m/100Mi). Then kubectl rollout restart -n kube-system deploy metrics-server.

symptom: After scale-up with deallocate mode, AKS node is NotReady even though VMSS instance is running; pods show as running on the node; /var/lib/kubelet/kubeconfig not found on the node; node-problem-detector shows 'invalid kubernetes master url specified'; kubelet is not running
rootCause: Combination of kubelet_disk_type=Temporary + scale_down_mode=Deallocate + VM SKU supporting both OS cache disk and temporary storage (Dsv3, Esv3, M, FX-series). VM stop/start during scale-down/up causes temporary storage loss, deleting kubelet data including kubeconfig and containerd data.
solution: Short-term: Reimage the affected node. Long-term: Avoid combining kubelet_disk_type=Temporary and scale_down_mode=Deallocate on node pools using VM SKUs that support both OS cache disk and temporary storage (Dsv3/Esv3/M/FX-series). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/aks/managed-clusters/create-or-update?view=rest-aks-2024-10-01&tabs=HTTP#kubeletdisktype
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Virtual Machine TSGs/VM Node NotReady kubelet missing
Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/VNet Scaling (StaticBlock)
symptom: Pods stuck in ContainerCreating on Azure CNI VNet Scale (StaticBlock) cluster. Error: Failed to get IP address from CNS: Post http://localhost:10090/network/requestipconfig: connection refused.
rootCause: CNS (azure-cns daemonset) is not running properly, or the NodeNetworkConfiguration (NNC) CRD is not installed, or DNC-RC has not created NNC objects for the nodes.
solution: 1) Check if nodenetworkconfigs.acn.azure.com CRD is installed. 2) Verify NNCs exist (1 per node) via kubectl get nnc -A. 3) If no NNCs, check DNC-RC logs in ControlPlaneEventsAll. 4) If NNCs exist, collect CNS logs from each node and send to container networking team.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Scheduler Customization - kube-scheduler
symptom: kube-scheduler CrashLoopBackOff with error: unexpected field, remove it or turn on the feature gate. Scheduler configuration contains features not enabled for the k8s version.
rootCause: If Scheduler Profile Config Mode is ManagedByCRD: customer entered configuration with features unsupported in their k8s version. If Default mode: base kube-scheduler configuration was changed incompatibly with older clusters.
solution: For ManagedByCRD mode: SchedulerCtrl will automatically rollback to last known good config. For Default mode: check recent changes in aks-rp kube-scheduler charts/helmvalues and immediately escalate via ICM if multiple clusters are impacted.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Scheduler Customization - kube-scheduler
symptom: SchedulerConfiguration custom resource stuck in Terminating state and cannot be deleted.
rootCause: SchedulerConfigurations have a finalizer (schedulerconfigurations.aks.azure.com/finalizer) that only gets removed when the scheduler is returned to its default state and is healthy.
solution: Ensure the kube-scheduler is healthy first. Then manually edit the SchedulerConfiguration to remove the finalizer. Contact Serverless SIG for RCA. Do NOT disable User-Defined Scheduler Configuration feature before resolving this, as it may cause the cluster to get stuck in Updating state.

symptom: AGC (Application Gateway for Containers) does not route HTTP requests as configured. Requests return 404 Not Found with server: Microsoft-Azure-Application-LB/AGC. Gateway resource shows no PROGRAMMED status. ALB controller pod logs show 'Unable to create stream for endpoints update' with 'connection timed out' to AGC configuration endpoint IP on port 443.
rootCause: The ALB controller pod in the AKS cluster cannot reach the AGC control plane configuration endpoint (public FQDN/IP on port 443). This is typically caused by NSG rules, firewall, or UDR blocking outbound traffic from the node to the AGC configurationEndpoint address. The endpoint FQDN can be found in the AGC resource JSON view under configurationEndpoint. Without connectivity, AGC cannot receive user intent (Gateway/HTTPRoute configs) and falls back to default 404.
solution: 1. Find the AGC configurationEndpoint FQDN from the AGC resource JSON view in Azure Portal. 2. Verify DNS resolution of the endpoint from the node (nslookup). 3. Test connectivity from node to the endpoint on port 443 (curl -v <fqdn>:443). Expected: Connection reset by peer (working). Unexpected: Connection timed out (blocked). 4. Check and fix NSG, UDR, or firewall rules blocking traffic from the node subnet to the endpoint IP. 5. After fixing connectivity, delete ALB controller pods to trigger restart and config resync.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/AGC/AGC_routing_not_functioning

symptom: AGC gateway resource shows blank ADDRESS field (kubectl get gateway). Gateway status condition message shows ApplicationLoadBalancer resource referenced by Gateway was not found. ApplicationLoadBalancer status shows DefaultAzureCredential authentication failed with AADSTS700213 No matching federated identity record found for presented assertion subject.
rootCause: Two issues: (1) The Gateway annotations (alb-namespace/alb-name) reference a non-existent ApplicationLoadBalancer resource name. (2) The federated identity credential for the ALB controller service account is misconfigured - the subject claim does not match the actual service account namespace/name used by the ALB controller.
solution: 1. Fix federated identity: Create/update the managed identity with correct federated credential matching the ALB controller service account (system:serviceaccount:azure-alb-system:alb-controller-sa). 2. Assign required RBAC roles: Reader on MC resource group, AppGw for Containers Configuration Manager on MC RG, Network Contributor on ALB subnet. 3. Upgrade ALB controller helm chart with updated identity clientID. 4. Verify ApplicationLoadBalancer status shows Accepted=True and Deployment=True. 5. Update Gateway annotations to reference the correct ApplicationLoadBalancer name/namespace.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/AGC/Address In Gateway Resource wasn't getting Updated

symptom: AGIC backend pools not updated with new pod IPs after pod restart. AGIC pod in CrashLoopBackOff. Logs show DefaultAzureCredential failed to acquire a token with ManagedIdentityCredential IMDS token request timed out and ARMAuthFailure with 401 AuthenticationFailedMissingToken. Issue appears after migrating AKS cluster from Service Principal to Managed Identity.
rootCause: When managed identity is enabled on a cluster with Microsoft Entra pod-managed identity (aad-pod-identity) installed manually (not via addon), the NMI pods modify node iptables to intercept all calls to the IMDS endpoint (169.254.169.254). This blocks AGIC pod authentication requests to IMDS, causing credential acquisition failure. This does not occur with Service Principal auth (no IMDS needed) or when using the aad-pod-identity addon (which auto-creates AzurePodIdentityException).
solution: Option 1: Install the Microsoft Entra pod-managed identities addon (az aks update), which automatically creates AzurePodIdentityException for system pods. Option 2: If using manual aad-pod-identity installation, create AzurePodIdentityException resources for kube-system namespace with podLabels matching kubernetes.azure.com/managedby: aks to exempt AGIC and other AKS addon pods from NMI interception.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/AGIC/AGIC Backend Pools Not Getting Updated After Migrating From service Principal to ManageIdentity

symptom: Ingress resource created immediately after enabling AGIC addon does not get an IP address. AGIC pod logs show Application Gateway is in stopped state and Skipping event. Reason: pod is not used by any Ingress. The AGIC addon enable operation reports completed within ~1 minute, but Application Gateway resource is still provisioning for 10+ minutes. Even after AppGW is fully created, the pre-existing Ingress is never reconciled.
rootCause: The AGIC addon enable operation reports completion before the Application Gateway resource is fully provisioned (takes 10+ minutes). AGIC detects the AppGW in stopped/updating state and skips Ingress reconciliation. Critically, Ingress resources created during this window are never picked up even after AppGW becomes ready - AGIC does not retroactively reconcile them.
solution: Delete and recreate the Ingress resource after verifying the Application Gateway resource is fully provisioned and no longer in updating state. The recreated Ingress will immediately be applied and obtain an IP. To avoid this issue: wait for Application Gateway to show Succeeded provisioning state before creating Ingress resources.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/AGIC/AGIC does not apply ingress right after addon enabled

symptom: AGIC returns 400 error when updating Application Gateway: InvalidResourceReference - urlPathMaps/url-xxxx referenced by requestRoutingRules was not found. Occurs when multiple AKS clusters share a single Application Gateway, especially after upgrading AGIC from v1.7.4 to v1.7.5+.
rootCause: AGIC v1.7.5+ introduced a fix to clean up stale URL path maps not defined in the local cluster Ingress resources. When two AGIC instances (from different AKS clusters) share the same Application Gateway, each AGIC reconciliation removes paths defined by the other cluster, causing invalid references. Sharing Application Gateway between clusters via AGIC addon is not supported.
solution: Option 1: Use a unique Application Gateway per AKS cluster when using the AGIC addon. Option 2: For shared Application Gateway, deploy AGIC via Helm (not addon) with subResourceNamePrefix to isolate path/rule names per cluster: helm upgrade ingress-azure ... --set appgw.subResourceNamePrefix=abc. This ensures each AGIC only manages its own prefixed resources. Start with v1.7.4 + prefix, then upgrade to v1.7.6+.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/AGIC/AGIC fails to update shared application gateway

symptom: TLS certificate issuance fails with cert-manager and Let's Encrypt using HTTP-01 challenge. kubectl describe challenge shows: Waiting for HTTP-01 challenge propagation: failed to perform self check GET request to .well-known/acme-challenge/... context deadline exceeded. Certificate remains in pending state.
rootCause: The Application Gateway used by AGIC does not expose the HTTP-01 challenge path (.well-known/acme-challenge) by default. The cert-manager solver pod needs to serve the challenge token via the ingress controller, but without specifying the ingress class in the Issuer/ClusterIssuer configuration, cert-manager cannot create the temporary Ingress resource needed to route challenge traffic to the solver pods.
solution: Update the ClusterIssuer/Issuer http01 solver section to include the ingress class: solvers: - http01: ingress: class: azure/application-gateway. This tells cert-manager to create a temporary Ingress with the correct class for AGIC to route challenge traffic. As of cert-manager 1.12, this is the recommended configuration. Alternatively, switch from HTTP-01 to DNS-01 challenges by configuring a DNS provider in the Issuer.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/AGIC/HTTP01 challenges fail with Lets Encrypt and cert manager

symptom: DNS resolution fails for pods on a specific AKS node with Temporary failure in name resolution (errno -3). Node-level DNS (nslookup from host) works normally. CoreDNS pods are healthy. Packet capture shows less than 1% DNS response success rate. Issue appears after cluster upgrade. iptables-save shows orphaned TWISTLOCK-NET-INPUT-DNS chain with NFQUEUE rules on UDP port 53. Twistlock Defender pod logs show OOM events.
rootCause: Twistlock (Prisma Cloud) Defender uses iptables NFQUEUE rules to intercept DNS traffic (UDP port 53) for inspection. When Defender is OOM-killed during a cluster upgrade, its NFQUEUE consumer process terminates without cleaning up the iptables rules. The orphaned NFQUEUE rules continue redirecting DNS response packets to a queue with no consumer, causing packets to be dropped. Node-level DNS is unaffected because host traffic does not traverse these iptables chains.
solution: 1. Identify affected node via pod logs and kubectl get pods -o wide. 2. Cordon the node: kubectl cordon <node>. 3. Drain workloads: kubectl drain <node> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data. 4. Delete/reimage the VMSS instance (az vmss delete-instances) to get a clean node. 5. Preventive: Increase Twistlock Defender memory limits (1Gi+), monitor for OOM events, consider excluding critical namespaces from Twistlock network interception.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/DNS/AKS DNS failures caused by Twistlock Defender NFQUEUE after OOM

symptom: Intermittent DNS timeouts and failures as request volume increases. CoreDNS logs show dns: overflowing header size errors mixed with read udp i/o timeout errors for AAAA queries to upstream 168.63.129.16.
rootCause: The DNS response from the upstream DNS server (168.63.129.16) exceeds the default CoreDNS buffer size of 512 bytes. Large DNS responses (from records with many entries or DNSSEC-signed responses) are truncated, causing the overflowing header size error and subsequent resolution failures.
solution: Increase CoreDNS buffer size by editing the coredns-custom configmap: kubectl edit cm coredns-custom -n kube-system. Add data section: buf.override: | bufsize 1232. Then restart CoreDNS: kubectl rollout restart deployment coredns -n kube-system. The 1232-byte buffer size avoids IP fragmentation while accommodating larger DNS responses.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/DNS/DNS Timeouts - Overflowing header size

symptom: AKS Extensions creation stuck in creating state with 'Errorcode: 403, Message: This traffic is not authorized'. Extension-agent pod logs show 403 errors when calling regional DP endpoint (e.g. eastus2euap.dp.kubernetesconfiguration.azure.com).
rootCause: A pre-existing PrivateLinkScope (PLS) for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes extensions data plane causes AKS extension DP traffic to route through a private IP instead of the public endpoint. This happens when the VNET or private DNS is shared between Arc-enabled K8s and the AKS managed cluster, causing DNS resolution to return the Arc private endpoint IP.
solution: Option 1: Create separate VNETs for AKS cluster and Arc-enabled Kubernetes. Option 2: Create a CoreDNS override (coredns-custom ConfigMap with hosts plugin) to map the regional DP endpoint (e.g. eastus2euap.dp.kubernetesconfiguration.azure.com) to its public IP. After applying the ConfigMap, restart CoreDNS with 'kubectl -n kube-system rollout restart deployment coredns'. Verify with nslookup from the extension-agent pod.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/Ungrouped/AKSExtension AzureArc

symptom: Node event logs (kubectl describe node) show 'Warning FailedToCreateRoute' with error 'PreconditionFailed' and detail 'PreconditionFailedEtagMismatch'. The route_controller cannot create routes for node pod CIDRs. Cluster uses Kubenet CNI.
rootCause: The route table used by the AKS cluster is shared between multiple AKS clusters using Kubenet CNI, and the pod CIDRs of these clusters overlap. This causes route table entries to be modified by different clusters simultaneously, leading to etag mismatches and precondition failures in the route controller.
solution: Each AKS Kubenet cluster must use a unique route table. Update the subnet to use a dedicated route table, or recreate the cluster with a non-overlapping pod CIDR. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/configure-kubenet#bring-your-own-subnet-and-route-table-with-kubenet. Use AppLens 'Shared Route Tables' detector or Kusto query on ManagedClusterSnapshot to identify shared route tables.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/Ungrouped/FailedToCreateRoute PreconditionFailed logged on all nodes Describe

symptom: Some calico-node pods are in 0/1 Running state (not ready) on nodes that do not host calico-typha pods. Calico-node pods on nodes with calico-typha are running fine. Cluster uses Calico network policy.
rootCause: Customer NSG has a Deny rule where the Source Address Prefix range (e.g. 10.224.0.0/15) includes the Destination Address Prefix range (e.g. 10.224.0.0/24). This overlapping deny rule blocks intra-subnet communication required for calico-node to reach calico-typha pods on other nodes.
solution: Review and modify the NSG rules to ensure Deny rules do not have overlapping source and destination address prefixes that would block intra-VNET traffic. Specifically, remove or narrow the deny rule so that node-to-node communication within the AKS subnet is not blocked. After fixing NSG rules, calico-node pods should become ready.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Networking/Ungrouped/NSG prevents Calico pods from starting

symptom: AKS pods experience higher-than-expected latency for initial outbound requests (including DNS resolution) immediately after starting. Connection latencies persist even when using IP addresses directly. Cluster has Calico network policy enabled.
rootCause: After pod start, Calico has a delay in updating iptables rules on the node. During this window, inbound and outbound traffic for the pod may experience connectivity issues or high latency. This is a known Calico behavior (see calico/calico#3530, calico/calico#4680).
solution: Use an init container or application startup delay to provide enough time for Calico to complete iptables updates before the main container begins making network requests.
